acceptance_criteria: renderToStaticMarkup emits data-cost-estimate, data-cost-ceiling and data-cost-verdict; an over-ceiling fixture renders data-cost-verdict="refused" plus the estimate value; board2d fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; conformance.test.tsx it() asserts each axis by value.

title: [09x][L] Column-lineage/drift view (Arc K)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: a column-lineage/drift view in Explorer. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-edge-status in {verified, drift-missing, drift-type-mismatch, partial}; it() asserts an injected drift renders the correct typed marker.

title: [09x][L] Policy-narrowing badge (Arc N)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: policy narrowings surfaced on the profile cards. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-policy-effect in {Deny, Narrow} and data-narrowed-from; it() asserts a narrowed verdict renders the pre-narrow level.

title: [09x][L] SCN time-scrubber (Arc A)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: an SCN time-scrubber dragging the console through history. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-scn-current, data-scn-min, data-scn-max; it() asserts current is clamped within [min,max].

title: [09x][L] Live CQN change feed (Arc C1)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: CQN change events streaming into the live classifier feed. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-change-event-id, data-change-scope, data-coalesced; it() asserts scope matches the proven query (never object-level) and a coalesced batch renders data-coalesced="true".

title: [09x][L] Edition linear timeline (Arc D)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: edition stages rendered as a LINEAR timeline on the Reviews board (not a git graph). Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-edition-stage, data-edition-parent, data-linear-order; it() asserts a single-child LINEAR chain renders (never a branch/graph node).

title: [09x][L] Reversible undo-tree (Arc I)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: the reversible undo-tree as a walkable branch off each snapshot. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-checkpoint-name, data-undoable, data-cannot-undo-reason; a sequence-touching node renders data-undoable="false" with a reason; it() asserts the label.

title: [09x][L] Egress mask badge (Arc M)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: a lock badge on masked columns from Arc M egress. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-masked, data-mask-policy-id; a masked column renders data-masked="true" plus policy id; it() asserts an unmasked column carries data-masked="false".

title: [09x][L] Verdict-proof inspector (Arc B1)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: verdict certificates as inspectable proofs on each GO/NO-GO action. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-rule-id, data-verdict, data-cert-hash; it() asserts the rendered rule-id set equals the certificate derivation and cert-hash is present.

title: [09x][L] Vector cluster panel (Arc F)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: vector-neighborhoods as a cluster panel in Explorer. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-metric, data-k, data-neighbor-distance; it() asserts k neighbors render and distances are monotonic non-decreasing.

title: [09x][L] Fleet map with per-DB status (Arc H)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: a fleet map with per-DB reachability/drift status. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-db-id, data-db-status per DB; it() asserts an UNREACHABLE lane still renders a node with data-db-status="unreachable" (never dropped).

title: [09x][GATE-SEAL] GUARD-REGRESSION-SEAL — re-run mutation gate + refresh marker after 0.9.x guard/audit changes
description: Closing counterpart to the GUARD-SOUND entry gate (.1). After every 0.9.x bead that mutates a mutation-gated crate lands (F1 VECTOR_DISTANCE + DBMS_VECTOR marker, D1 EDITION allowlist, N2/N3/N4 policy composition, B1.1 derivation emit + cert-into-audit, A3 observed_scn, M2 mask-cert-into-audit), re-run scripts/mutation_safety_gate.sh over oraclemcp-guard and oraclemcp-audit and refresh the committed marker in docs/quality/mutation-safety.md. No 0.9.x train ships until this is green.
acceptance_criteria: a fresh scripts/mutation_safety_gate.sh run records oraclemcp-guard >= 90 and oraclemcp-audit >= 90 with status=enforcing; the docs/quality/mutation-safety.md marker and results table are refreshed and committed; scripts/release_preflight.sh check-report passes.

title: [09x][G] Query economics — cost as a governed dimension
description:
Extend the fail-closed guard to a second dimension: COST. Agents write runaway full-scans; the
server already extracts optimizer plan cost (K3: plan_cost_estimate / PlanCostSummary in
oraclemcp-db/src/intelligence.rs:1254). Close the loop so a query whose estimated cost exceeds a
per-profile ceiling is REFUSED before it runs, with the plan and a predicate hint instead of a
40-minute scan. This is a second fail-closed gate composed with the classifier: it only ever ADDS
a refusal, never admits what the classifier refused. Lead arc (zero new deps, version-agnostic).
Guard-safety: cost probe uses EXPLAIN PLAN FOR (optimizes target WITHOUT executing it; PLAN_TABLE
write is a session GTT, tiered ReadWrite, behind the existing allow_plan_table_write gate) — NOT
DBMS_XPLAN.DISPLAY_CURSOR, which needs the statement already executed. A base object whose VPD/
policy purity is unproven is refused before the EXPLAIN (inherits P0 .82). Children: G1-G4.

title: [09x][G1] Profile + per-call max_query_cost knob
description:
Arc G (cost). SCOPE: add a per-profile cost ceiling and an optional per-call override that may only
LOWER it (never raise). FILES: crates/oraclemcp-config/src/profile.rs — add
`max_query_cost: Option<u64>` to ConnectionProfile (struct is #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] at
:486, so the field is mandatory to accept the TOML key); add it to the manual Debug impl (:593) and
ProfileMetadata (:801); crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/args.rs — add optional `max_query_cost` to
QueryArgs. ACCEPTANCE: a profile TOML with max_query_cost parses; a per-call value above the
profile ceiling is clamped down (never up); unit test asserts clamp-down; ProfileMetadata surfaces
it to list_profiles. DEPENDS: gate only (entry bead).

title: [09x][G2] Pre-execution cost gate (EXPLAIN-first, null-cost fails closed)
description:
Arc G (cost). SCOPE: in the oracle_query handler (crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/mod.rs, the
oracle_query prepared block ~:6984), after ensure_read_only passes and before running, if the
active profile has max_query_cost and the estimated total_cost exceeds it, REFUSE. Reuse
plan_cost_estimate/PlanCostSummary (oraclemcp-db/src/intelligence.rs:1254) via EXPLAIN PLAN FOR
behind the existing allow_plan_table_write gate — target is optimized, NOT executed. CRITICAL:
PlanCostSummary.total_cost is Option<i64>; on a NULL cost (dynamic sampling) FAIL CLOSED (refuse
with cost_unavailable), do not admit. Refuse a base object whose VPD/policy purity is unproven
before the EXPLAIN. ACCEPTANCE: unit tests — total_cost Some(190000) over a ceiling of 50000 ->
refusal; Some(2) -> pass; None -> refusal (cost_unavailable). Live test (gated): an intentionally
costly synthetic query over the ceiling refuses pre-execution and never scans. DEPENDS: G1.

title: [09x][G3] Structured cost-refusal payload (plan + predicate hint)
description:
Arc G (cost). SCOPE: shape the refusal so the agent can self-correct: return the estimated cost,
the plan, and a suggested-predicate/index hint drawn from the plan's own access_predicates /
filter_predicates columns (first cut — not a new optimizer/advisor). Build via ErrorEnvelope with a
structured reason. ACCEPTANCE: a refused over-ceiling query returns cost, plan rows, and at least
one predicate hint; a snapshot/unit test pins the payload shape; the message says "estimated cost"
(never implies a guarantee). DEPENDS: G2.

title: [09x][G4] Cumulative per-principal cost budget (follow-up)
description:
Arc G (cost). SCOPE: beyond the per-query gate, a windowed cumulative budget — an agent that has
burned N cost-units in a window is throttled. Store the windowed counter in state files
(files-not-DB ethos); window size + reset are per-profile config. ACCEPTANCE: a principal exceeding
its window budget is throttled with a typed refusal; the counter resets on window roll; unit test
covers accrual + reset. DEPENDS: G2 (reuses the estimate). Independent follow-up — gated only.

title: [09x][G-E2E] e2e script: cost-gate live matrix
description: New scripts/e2e/cost_gate.sh sourcing scripts/e2e/lib.sh; drives oracle_query over the gvenzl matrix (18/21/23) via MCP stdio. An intentionally costly synthetic full-scan over a per-profile max_query_cost ceiling is REFUSED pre-execution and never scans; a cheap query passes; a dynamic-sampling NULL-cost query is refused with cost_unavailable (fail-closed). Emits e2e_log_event JSON per case (phase, outcome, duration_ms); connect_or_skip + e2e_finish_skip when creds absent; register in scripts/e2e/run_all.sh.
acceptance_criteria: cost_gate.sh runs green on all three lanes (or SKIPs cleanly when creds absent); asserts refuse-over-ceiling never scanned, cheap passes, NULL-cost refused; each assertion logged as a scenario_complete JSON event; listed in run_all.sh.

title: [09x][A] Time as a first-class dimension
description:
Make TIME a governed dimension using the server's shipped as_of capability (K9: AsOf enum at
oraclemcp-db/src/query.rs:134; AS OF SCN/TIMESTAMP via a DBMS_FLASHBACK session bracket, teardown
at connection.rs:779) + K3 plan cost + the append-only audit chain. Lead arc (no new deps).
DECISION (fork resolved): keep the shipped K9 DBMS_FLASHBACK session-bracket — do NOT rewrite to
inline AS OF SCN (sqlparser 0.62 will not reliably parse it and would fail-closed to Guarded). The
bracket is server-issued AROUND a classifier-proven read and is an internal privileged mechanism
the caller only parameterizes by SCN; the wrapped SELECT is still fully classified.
HONEST SCOPE: flashback reconstructs committed DATA from undo, bounded by UNDO_RETENTION + undo
sizing (NOT DB_FLASHBACK_RETENTION_TARGET, which governs Flashback Database) and the SMON SCN-time
map (~5 days) for timestamp flashback; it BREAKS after structural DDL (ORA-01466); it is NOT
byte-identical (needs total ORDER BY + pinned NLS); non-flashbackable objects are out of scope.
Children: A1-A4.

title: [09x][A1] oracle_diff — semantic row diff of a proven read at two SCNs
description:
Arc A (time). SCOPE: new tool oracle_diff — input { sql (proven read), scn_a int, scn_b int, key?
string[] }; output { added[], removed[], changed[{key,before,after}], keyed bool }. Runs the same
read_query_as_of twice (at scn_a and scn_b) and diffs in Rust. ROW ALIGNMENT (decided): by primary
key when the object has one (from all_constraints); else full-row hash set-diff reporting
add/remove only (no "changed" without a key). Guard sees two independent classifier-proven reads —
zero new admission surface. FILES: registry.rs (+oracle_diff, bump TOOL_NAMES count),
dispatch/args.rs (DiffArgs), dispatch/mod.rs (arm + generated_read_tool), intelligence.rs (diff
helper). ACCEPTANCE: unit — two canned row-sets diff to the correct add/remove/change set; keyless
-> add/remove only. Live (gated): a synthetic table changed between two SCNs diffs correctly.
DEPENDS: gate (entry).

title: [09x][A2] Typed refusals: beyond-retention + DDL-since-SCN
description:
Arc A (time). SCOPE: map Oracle flashback limits to typed, fail-closed refusals rather than silent
empty results. ORA-01555 / ORA-08180 -> a "beyond-retention" typed refusal; ORA-01466 (structural
DDL since the SCN) -> a first-class "definition-changed" typed refusal; non-flashbackable object ->
typed "not-flashbackable". FILES: the as_of read path + oracle_diff error mapping. ACCEPTANCE:
live tests (gated) — a post-DDL SCN returns the DDL typed refusal; a beyond-retention SCN returns
the retention typed refusal; each carries a next-step hint. DEPENDS: A1.

title: [09x][A3] SCN-stamped audit + read replay
description:
Arc A (time). SCOPE: stamp every audited read with the SCN it observed (add observed_scn to the
audit record) so any audited read becomes re-runnable at the SCN the agent saw — reconstructing the
committed DATA the agent decided on (not byte-identical; see epic scope). SEC-3: audit-write
failure fails closed. FILES: oraclemcp-audit record schema; the read/audit path. ACCEPTANCE: a
stored read replays to the same committed rows at its observed_scn; audit-write failure refuses the
read; unit test on the schema + a live replay test (gated). DEPENDS: gate (entry).
acceptance_criteria: a stored read replays to the same committed rows at observed_scn; audit-write failure refuses the read; schema unit test + gated live replay; mutation gate stays >= 90 for oraclemcp-audit after the observed_scn field is added.

title: [09x][A4] Plan time-machine (license-gated stretch)
description:
Arc A (time). SCOPE: "this query's plan flipped ~SCN X, cost 2 then 19" across history. NOTE:
costing a plan at a HISTORICAL SCN needs AWR / DBA_HIST_SQL_PLAN, which requires the Diagnostics
Pack (LICENSED) — probe for it and degrade to a typed "requires diagnostics pack" refusal when
absent. Ship data-diff (A1) + replay (A3) first; this is a stretch. ACCEPTANCE: on a licensed DB,
returns a plan-cost timeline; on an unlicensed DB, a typed capability refusal (no silent failure).
DEPENDS: A3.

title: [09x][A-E2E] e2e script: time-diff live matrix
description: New scripts/e2e/time_diff.sh. Over the matrix: mutate a synthetic table between two SCNs and assert oracle_diff returns the correct add/remove/change set (keyed) and add/remove-only (keyless); a post-DDL SCN yields the ORA-01466 definition-changed typed refusal; a beyond-retention SCN yields the retention typed refusal; an audited read replays to identical committed rows at observed_scn. e2e_log_event per case; connect_or_skip.
acceptance_criteria: time_diff.sh green on all lanes; the three typed-refusal paths and the replay path each asserted and logged; registered in run_all.sh.

title: [09x][M] Governed egress — the guard on the way out
description:
The guard governs what an agent may DO; nothing today governs what it may SEE. An agent blocked
from DELETE can still read raw PII into a context window it does not control. Arc M makes governed
egress a first-class, proof-carrying capability on the RESULT path. IMPORTANT (verified): the
result-path masker is NET-NEW — today's redaction (oraclemcp-auth/src/secrets.rs) covers only
secrets/credentials/profile output + the principal key, NOT query result data. Arc M builds the
server-side result masker (the only layer that can do mask-unknown-by-default + join-consistent
salted tokenization); optional Oracle-native DBMS_REDACT/VPD/RLS is a separate LICENSED tier
(Advanced Security). Egress is a TIGHTENING — it can only remove data from a result, never add
admission. Shared upstream of B1 (mask cert), F, H, J, E. Children: M0-M4.

title: [09x][M0] Design spike — masking-policy schema + token function
description:
Arc M (egress). SELF-CONTAINED FIRST BEAD (design spike). OUTPUT CONTRACT: a written masking-policy
schema + token function. RESOLVED DIRECTION: masker is a server-side result transformer; policy =
{ column_match:{schema?,table?,column|tag}, action: mask|tokenize|null } per profile; tokenize =
HMAC(profile_salt, plaintext) truncated (format-preserving optional); profile_salt stored
per-profile in state files (files-not-DB); mask-unknown-default = any column tagged
sensitive-or-unlisted is masked. ACCEPTANCE: a committed design note (in docs) defining the policy
record, the token function, salt storage/rotation, and the mask-unknown default; a test vector for
the token function. DEPENDS: gate (entry). Gates M1-M4.

title: [09x][M1] Result-path masking transformer
description:
Arc M (egress). SCOPE: apply the M0 policy to query results before serialization (in the query
result path, before rows_to_json / Arrow encoding). Mask/tokenize/null per column policy;
mask-unknown-by-default. FILES: the result serialization path in dispatch/oraclemcp-db.
ACCEPTANCE: a query over a masked column returns tokens; an unconfigured sensitive column is masked
by default; a test asserts NO plaintext leaks through a self-join (consistency vs inference). Unit
+ integration tests. DEPENDS: M0.

title: [09x][M2] Mask-decision certificate (feeds B1)
description:
Arc M (egress). SCOPE: emit a per-result mask-decision certificate (which columns masked, policy id,
profile) hashed into the audit chain — proof-carrying egress, consumed by Arc B1. SEC-3: audit-write
failure fails closed. ACCEPTANCE: a masked result carries a verifiable mask certificate bound to its
audit record; a test re-derives the mask decision from the certificate. DEPENDS: M1.
acceptance_criteria: a masked result carries a verifiable mask certificate bound to its audit record; a test re-derives the mask decision; audit-write failure fails closed (SEC-3); mutation gate stays >= 90 for oraclemcp-audit after the cert is hashed into the chain.

title: [09x][M3] Policy loader + mask-unknown-default enforcement
description:
Arc M (egress). SCOPE: load per-profile masking policies (files, deny_unknown_fields) and enforce
mask-unknown-default: any column not proven non-sensitive is masked. ACCEPTANCE: a malformed policy
is rejected at load; an unlisted column is masked; a test proves default-masking is fail-closed.
DEPENDS: M0.

title: [09x][M4] Optional Oracle-native DBMS_REDACT/VPD tier (licensed)
description:
Arc M (egress). SCOPE: optional in-database enforcement via DBMS_REDACT / VPD / RLS as a second
tier. NOTE: DBMS_REDACT (Data Redaction) needs the Advanced Security option (LICENSED) and per-object
policy objects (privileged setup) — probe + degrade honestly; not interchangeable with the server
seam. ACCEPTANCE: on a licensed DB, a native redaction policy is applied and audited; on an
unlicensed DB, a typed "requires advanced security" note and fallback to the server masker. DEPENDS:
M1.

title: [09x][M-E2E] e2e script: governed-egress live matrix
description: New scripts/e2e/egress.sh. Over the matrix: a query over a masked column returns tokens; an unconfigured sensitive column is masked by default (mask-unknown fail-closed); a self-join over a tokenized column leaks NO plaintext and is join-consistent (inference test); the per-result mask certificate re-derives the mask decision. e2e_log_event per case; connect_or_skip. SECURITY-CRITICAL: a missing test here is a data-exfiltration hole, not just a regression.
acceptance_criteria: egress.sh green on all lanes; tokenization, mask-unknown-default, no-plaintext-through-self-join, and cert re-derivation each asserted and logged; registered in run_all.sh.

title: [09x][N] Policy-as-code — the programmable guard
description:
The guard is one-size-fits-all; there is no operator-authored declarative policy ("principal X may
never touch schema HR"; "table Y is append-only"). Arc N is OPA/Rego-for-SQL. LARGELY NET-NEW: the
existing OperatorAuthorityPolicy (D17, oraclemcp-core/src/admin_auth.rs) governs WHO is the operator
(identity), not per-principal SQL rules — a thin seed only. The guard already has ONE deliberate
loosening lever (the audited operator allow_list in classifier.rs); N sits ABOVE the classifier and
must NEVER become a second one. STRICTLY MONOTONE: a policy returns Deny or Narrow over the base
verdict, never Allow-beyond-base; final = base AND policy. A Narrow that only lowers the verdict/level
is monotone; a Narrow that rewrites SQL produces a different statement that MUST re-enter the
classifier (SEC-1). Children: N0-N4.

title: [09x][N0] Design spike — policy grammar + monotone return type
description:
Arc N (policy). SELF-CONTAINED FIRST BEAD (design spike). OUTPUT CONTRACT: the policy grammar.
RESOLVED DIRECTION: a small declarative TOML rule list (not a DSL) reusing the config machinery —
rule = { match:{schema?,object?,verb?,principal?}, effect: Deny | RequireLevel(L) |
RequirePredicate(sql_fragment) }; evaluated AFTER base classification as final = base AND policy; a
RequirePredicate rewrite re-enters the classifier (SEC-1); return type is Deny or Narrow only (no
Allow) so it is structurally monotone. Confirm the D17 config is not already partly this. ACCEPTANCE:
a committed design note defining the rule grammar, the predicate vocabulary, the Deny|Narrow return
type, and whether Narrow may rewrite SQL. DEPENDS: gate (entry). Gates N1-N4.

title: [09x][N1] Policy loader + load-time loosening rejection
description:
Arc N (policy). SCOPE: load per-profile policy rules (TOML, deny_unknown_fields). Reject at load ANY
rule whose effect could loosen the base verdict (only Deny/RequireLevel/RequirePredicate allowed).
ACCEPTANCE: a valid policy loads; a malformed or loosening policy is rejected at load with a clear
error; unit tests. DEPENDS: N0.

title: [09x][N2] Monotone evaluator: final = base AND policy
description:
Arc N (policy). SCOPE: evaluate loaded policy after base classification, composing final = base AND
policy; the engine returns Deny or Narrow, never Allow-beyond-base. Every decision is proof-carrying
(feeds B1) and self-teaching (feeds J). ACCEPTANCE: an operator policy narrows a principal's admitted
set; a policy can never admit what the base classifier refused; unit tests over the composition.
DEPENDS: N1.
acceptance_criteria: final = base AND policy; a policy never admits what the base refused; add a never-loosen composition case to the adversarial CORPUS; if the evaluator lands in oraclemcp-guard, mutation gate stays >= 90.

title: [09x][N3] RequirePredicate rewrite re-enters the classifier (SEC-1)
description:
Arc N (policy). SCOPE: when a Narrow effect rewrites the SQL (adds a predicate / RLS clause), the
rewritten statement is a DIFFERENT statement and MUST be re-classified before execution (SEC-1);
never trust that the rewrite is still read-only. ACCEPTANCE: a RequirePredicate narrow is
re-classified; a rewrite that would become non-read-only is refused; unit test. DEPENDS: N2.
acceptance_criteria: a RequirePredicate narrow re-enters classify (SEC-1); a rewrite that becomes non-read-only is refused; add that rewrite to the CORPUS asserting min danger; mutation gate stays >= 90.

title: [09x][N4] Monotonicity property test
description:
Arc N (policy). SCOPE: a property test proving policy composition is monotone — for any base verdict
and any loadable policy, final <= base in permissiveness (never looser). ACCEPTANCE: a property/fuzz
test over generated (statement, policy) pairs asserts no policy ever loosens the base verdict; add a
representative case to the adversarial corpus. DEPENDS: N2.
acceptance_criteria: property/fuzz over generated (statement, policy) pairs asserts final <= base (never looser); a representative case added to the adversarial CORPUS; mutation gate stays >= 90 for the composition crate.

title: [09x][B] Proof-carrying access
description:
Make the guard's decisions externally verifiable. Three shippable trains: B1 verdict certificates
(the classifier emits a serialized derivation of WHY a statement is ProvenReadOnly or refused,
hashed into each audit record, re-checkable by an external verifier that never trusts the server);
B2 certificate transparency (anchor the audit-chain HEAD into Sigstore/Rekor); B3 a Lean proof of
the purity core. Strengthens the guard by construction — adds no execution path. B1's certificate
MUST be emitted inside the same classify call that gates execution (SEC-1); audit-write failure
fails closed (SEC-3). Depends on parallel-track 0.8.3 audit hardening (rotation/WORM) for a sound
chain to anchor — covered by the guard-sound gate. Children: B1.0/B1.1/B1.2, B2.1, B3.1.

title: [09x][B1.0] Design spike — verdict-certificate schema + rule-id registry
description:
Arc B (proof). SELF-CONTAINED FIRST BEAD (spike). OUTPUT CONTRACT: the certificate + derivation
grammar. RESOLVED DIRECTION: extend GuardDecision (crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/classifier.rs) to
emit derivation: Vec<DerivationStep{ rule_id, construct }> using the classifier's existing
R-numbered rules (R15 etc.). Certificate = { stmt_digest, level, verdict, derivation,
classifier_version, observed_scn, bound_audit_hash }. ACCEPTANCE: a committed schema note defining
DerivationStep, the rule-id registry (enumerate the classifier's rule ids), and the cert fields +
how bound_audit_hash ties the cert to the exact audit record. DEPENDS: gate (entry). Gates B1.1/1.2.

title: [09x][B1.1] Emit certificate inside the gating classify call + hash into audit
description:
Arc B (proof). SCOPE: emit the B1.0 certificate INSIDE the same classify() call that gates
execution (SEC-1) so it can never describe a different decision than the one enforced; hash it into
the audit record. SEC-3: audit/cert write failure fails closed (refuse the read). The derivation
must pass the Arc-M/redaction seam — no raw SQL, binds, or identifiers leave the host. FILES:
classifier.rs (derivation emit), oraclemcp-audit (bind cert to record). ACCEPTANCE: every gated
statement produces a redacted certificate bound to its audit record; a write failure refuses; unit
tests. DEPENDS: B1.0.
acceptance_criteria: every gated statement emits a redacted certificate bound to its audit record; write failure refuses (SEC-3); unit tests; mutation gate stays >= 90 for BOTH oraclemcp-guard (derivation emit in classifier.rs) and oraclemcp-audit (cert hashed into record).

title: [09x][B1.2] Standalone external verifier
description:
Arc B (proof). SCOPE: a standalone Rust lib/bin that re-runs the same ruleset to re-derive a verdict
from its certificate with ZERO server trust. ACCEPTANCE: the verifier re-derives a sample verdict
from its certificate and confirms bound_audit_hash; a test feeds a tampered certificate and the
verifier rejects it. DEPENDS: B1.1.

title: [09x][B2.1] Certificate transparency — async Rekor anchor
description:
Arc B (proof). SCOPE: periodically anchor the audit-chain HEAD into Sigstore/Rekor. HONEST SCOPE:
this gives RETROACTIVE tamper-evidence given an independently retained checkpoint; it does not
prevent an operator anchoring a doctored chain or withholding the proof. Anchoring is ASYNC and
NON-BLOCKING to admission — a Rekor outage must never gate a query. ACCEPTANCE: the chain head
appears in Rekor with an offline-checkable inclusion proof; a simulated Rekor outage does not block
any query (test). DEPENDS: B1.1 (a sound chain to anchor).

title: [09x][B3.1] Lean proof of the purity core (research)
description:
Arc B (proof). SCOPE (research, long-horizon): a Lean proof of the PURITY CORE ONLY. HONEST SCOPE:
proves the abstract purity model sound; the deployed Rust classifier is tied to it by CONFORMANCE
TESTS, not verified extraction — "verified spec + tested implementation", not "verified binary". As
far as we know this would be the first formally-specified SQL-safety purity core; state it that way.
ACCEPTANCE: the purity core's key lemma is Lean-proved and a conformance test pins the Rust
implementation to the model. DEPENDS: gate (entry) — independent, non-blocking research.

title: [09x][C] The living database — subscriptions, orient, Arrow
description:
Make new kinds of read safe. GROUND TRUTH: the driver ALREADY ships CQN (subscribe_register /
register_query at rust-oracledb crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs:3397,4630; EMON NOTIFY parser in
thin/subscr.rs; pyshim callbacks) and Arrow decode into RecordBatch — the v1 premise "wire CQN in
the driver" was WRONG. The remaining work is server-side. Three sub-arcs: C1 CQN to MCP
subscriptions (push change EVENTS, not rows); C2 oracle_orient (a shared catalog snapshot that H
and K depend on); C3 Arrow-IPC results (NEW driver work: no arrow-ipc dep exists). C1 needs the
Wave-2 http/mod.rs de-monolith for reviewable routes. Children: C1.1-1.4, C2.1-2.4, C3.1-3.3.

title: [09x][C1.1] Gate CQN registration as a first-class privileged op
description:
Arc C1 (living-DB). SCOPE: CQN registration (the OCI/DBMS_CHANGE_NOTIFICATION call, CHANGE
NOTIFICATION privilege) is NOT classified SQL text — it is invisible to the text classifier, so it
must be gated as a first-class privileged action with its own capability + step-up. FILES:
http/mod.rs (post-de-monolith) + a subscription registry in state files. ACCEPTANCE: registering a
subscription requires step-up; an un-stepped registration is refused; unit/integration tests.
DEPENDS: gate + the http de-monolith (reviewable routes).

title: [09x][C3.2] Server: bump oracledb pin to the arrow-ipc release
description:
Arc C3 (living-DB). SCOPE: bump the server's oracledb pin (Cargo.toml) to the release that carries
C3.1, and verify the full workspace builds/tests green. ACCEPTANCE: server compiles against the new
pin; existing suites stay green. DEPENDS: C3.1.

title: [09x][C3.3] oracle_query format=arrow (base64 Arrow IPC, egress applied)
description:
Arc C3 (living-DB). SCOPE: opt-in format:arrow on oracle_query returns arrow_ipc_b64 (base64 Arrow
IPC stream) instead of JSON rows; default stays JSON. Arc-M egress masking is applied to the rows
BEFORE Arrow encoding. FILES: dispatch/args.rs QueryArgs.format; the query arm. ACCEPTANCE: a real
query in arrow mode decodes into the same rows as JSON mode; masked columns are masked in the Arrow
output; unit + live. DEPENDS: C3.2 + Arc-M M1 (egress).

title: [09x][C-E2E] e2e script: living-DB live matrix
description: New scripts/e2e/living_db.sh. Over the matrix: oracle_orient returns a stable snapshot (schema, fks, hot_objects, freshness, recent_ddl) and the cache is keyed by catalog revision; a QUERY-level CQN subscription delivers a coalesced change event within the per-DB ceiling while an OBJECT-level request is refused; oracle_query format=arrow decodes to the SAME rows as JSON mode with masked columns still masked. e2e_log_event per case; connect_or_skip.
acceptance_criteria: living_db.sh green on all lanes; orient snapshot, CQN coalesced-event + object-level refusal, and arrow==json equivalence each asserted and logged; registered in run_all.sh.

title: [09x][C1.2] QUERY-level registration only; refuse OBJECT-level
description:
Arc C1 (living-DB). SCOPE: register at QUERY level bound to the classifier-proven predicate
(register_query); REFUSE OBJECT-level registration — object-level fires on any base-table DML, so
the feed would reveal mutations to rows the proven read's WHERE excludes (a scope leak). The feed
inherits the query's verdict; it never widens it. ACCEPTANCE: a QUERY-level subscription is
accepted; an OBJECT-level request is refused with a typed reason; a test proves out-of-predicate
rows never surface. DEPENDS: C1.1.

title: [09x][C1.3] EMON connection accounting + max_subscriptions
description:
Arc C1 (living-DB). SCOPE: each active subscription carries a second (EMON NOTIFY) connection —
account it as one connection against the per-DB ceiling; add a per-profile max_subscriptions
(Option<u32>, default 4) cap. FILES: profile.rs (max_subscriptions), the subscription registry.
ACCEPTANCE: subscriptions beyond the cap are refused; the EMON connection is counted in the ceiling;
unit test. DEPENDS: C1.1.

title: [09x][C1.4] resources/subscribe fan-out (best-effort, coalesced)
description:
Arc C1 (living-DB). SCOPE: fan the driver's change-notification callbacks out to MCP
resources/subscribe. CONTRACT (honest CQN semantics): delivers change EVENTS (object-level, or
ROWIDs under query-result-change) NOT row data — the agent re-reads the proven scope; delivery is
BEST-EFFORT and COALESCED by default (RELIABLE QoS optional for durable at-least-once). ACCEPTANCE:
a standing QUERY-level subscription over a synthetic table delivers a coalesced change event within
the ceiling; live/integration test with clear logging. DEPENDS: C1.2, C1.3.

title: [09x][C2.1] oracle_orient — schema map + FK topology
description:
Arc C2 (living-DB, shared snapshot — H and K depend on this). SCOPE: the schema/type map (from
ALL_OBJECTS) and FK topology (ALL_CONSTRAINTS R-to-P join + ALL_CONS_COLUMNS), each an
individually-classified dictionary read via guarded_metadata_conn, positional binds, upper-cased
idents, ROWNUM cap. FILES: intelligence.rs orient_schema/orient_fks. ACCEPTANCE: returns owner/
object/type list + child/parent/columns FK edges over a synthetic schema (unit with mock rows +
live). DEPENDS: gate (entry).

title: [09x][C2.2] oracle_orient — hot objects + freshness
description:
Arc C2 (living-DB). SCOPE: hot objects + data freshness from ALL_TAB_MODIFICATIONS (inserts/
updates/deletes since last stats). Classified dict read. ACCEPTANCE: returns per-object DML activity
+ freshness over a synthetic schema; unit + live. DEPENDS: C2.1.

title: [09x][C2.3] oracle_orient — recent DDL
description:
Arc C2 (living-DB). SCOPE: recent DDL from ALL_OBJECTS.LAST_DDL_TIME. Classified dict read.
ACCEPTANCE: returns owner/object/last_ddl_time ordered by recency over a synthetic schema; unit +
live. DEPENDS: C2.1.

title: [09x][C2.4] Assemble oracle_orient tool + snapshot cache
description:
Arc C2 (living-DB). SCOPE: register the oracle_orient tool (registry.rs +TOOL_NAMES, dispatch arm +
generated_read_tool, OrientArgs with include[] selector) assembling C2.1-2.3 into one snapshot,
cacheable per (profile, catalog-revision). This is the shared node H (--fleet) and K (lineage

recent_ddl } snapshot; snapshot cache keyed by catalog revision; unit + live. DEPENDS: C2.1/2.2/2.3.

title: [09x][C3.1] Driver: arrow-ipc serialization (rust-oracledb)
description:
Arc C3 (living-DB, DRIVER work). SCOPE: add the arrow-ipc dependency to rust-oracledb (verified
absent today) and serialize the existing RecordBatch (crates/oracledb/src/arrow/mod.rs) with an
Arrow StreamWriter. ACCEPTANCE: driver round-trips a RecordBatch to Arrow IPC bytes and back via an
arrow-ipc reader; driver unit test. DEPENDS: gate (entry). Cross-repo: this is a rust-oracledb bead.

title: [09x][F] Governed RAG — vector-semantic search
description:
Vector search is table-stakes (pgvector etc.) — the NOVELTY is the guard, not the vectors: the only
fail-closed, audited semantic-search surface over Oracle 23ai in-DB vectors we are aware of. Leans
on 23ai VECTOR + AI Vector Search + the driver's shipped VECTOR wire decode (vector.rs). The
columnar Arrow fast path (bead 0mk) is an optional enhancer, not a blocker. 23ai-GATED, so this is
a second-wave arc, not the lead (leading with it would blank for the 19c/21c installed base).
Children: F1-F5.

title: [09x][F1] Admit VECTOR_DISTANCE (pure); keep DBMS_VECTOR fail-closed
description:
Arc F (RAG). GUARD-SAFETY CRITICAL (was the #1 danger in review). SCOPE: admit ONLY VECTOR_DISTANCE
(documented pure deterministic math) to the proven-pure builtin set — add "vector_distance"
(lowercase) to BUILTINS in crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/classifier.rs:748 (is_builtin_function). This
is a reviewed, mutation-gated admission of a PROVEN-PURE construct (Doctrine 2), not an admission of
anything unproven; verified it is currently Guarded, so this is deliberate. KEEP ALL DBMS_VECTOR.*
fail-closed — DBMS_VECTOR.UTL_TO_EMBEDDING can name a REST provider and issue UTL_HTTP egress,
exfiltrating governed data under a green read-only verdict; additionally add "DBMS_VECTOR" to
PLSQL_SIDE_EFFECT_MARKERS (classifier.rs:150) so a PL/SQL-block form is Forbidden. ACCEPTANCE:
classifier tests — SELECT VECTOR_DISTANCE(a,b) FROM t is Safe; a DBMS_VECTOR.UTL_TO_EMBEDDING block
is Forbidden; both added to the adversarial corpus (asserts min danger) plus exact assert_eq tests;
mutation gate stays >= 90. DEPENDS: gate (entry).

title: [09x][F4] 23ai CI lane prerequisite
description:
Arc F (RAG). SCOPE: stand up a 23ai CI lane (gvenzl/oracle-free:23-slim, port 1522 per
scripts/e2e/oracle_version_matrix.sh) so F's positive-path live tests can run. ACCEPTANCE: the
matrix runner has a green 23ai lane; a smoke test creates a VECTOR column and runs VECTOR_DISTANCE.
DEPENDS: gate (entry).

title: [09x][F2] oracle_semantic_search tool + SQL template
description:
Arc F (RAG). SCOPE: tool oracle_semantic_search — input { over:{owner?,table,column}, query_text? |
query_vector? (exactly one), k:1..1000, metric?:COSINE|EUCLIDEAN|DOT, filter?:proven predicate };
output { rows[], metric, k, used_index }. SQL: SELECT <cols> FROM <owner.table> [WHERE <proven
filter>] ORDER BY VECTOR_DISTANCE(<column>, :qv, <metric>) FETCH FIRST :k ROWS ONLY; :qv is the
caller vector, or VECTOR_EMBEDDING(<model> USING :qt) when query_text + a VERIFIED in-db ONNX model
(the SQL operator runs the model in-database, no network). FILES: registry (+tool), args, dispatch
arm, intelligence.rs semantic_search_query. ACCEPTANCE: top-k over a synthetic VECTOR fixture on the
23ai lane; classifier admits the generated SELECT. DEPENDS: F1, F4.

title: [09x][F3] 23ai capability probe + typed degrade
description:
Arc F (RAG). SCOPE: probe COMPATIBLE >= 23.4 AND a pre-loaded ONNX embedding model (DBMS_VECTOR.
LOAD_ONNX_MODEL / USER_MINING_MODELS) — not merely "server is 23ai"; degrade to a typed
requires_23ai / no-in-db-model refusal on 18c/21c, never a silent full scan. ACCEPTANCE: the same
call on 18c/21c returns a typed capability refusal (live matrix). DEPENDS: F2.

title: [09x][F5] Hybrid retrieval (proven filter + distance)
description:
Arc F (RAG). SCOPE: combine vector distance with a classifier-proven relational filter in one
guarded query. ACCEPTANCE: a hybrid query returns top-k within the proven filter; the filter is
classified read-only; test on the 23ai lane. DEPENDS: F2.

title: [09x][F-E2E] e2e script: governed-RAG 23ai lane
description: New scripts/e2e/governed_rag.sh, free23 lane only (gvenzl oracle-free:23-slim, port 1522). oracle_semantic_search returns top-k over a synthetic VECTOR fixture; a DBMS_VECTOR.UTL_TO_EMBEDDING block is Forbidden end-to-end (never issues UTL_HTTP egress); a hybrid proven-filter-plus-distance query stays read-only; the same call on the 18/21 lanes returns the requires_23ai typed degrade (never a silent full scan). e2e_log_event per case.
acceptance_criteria: governed_rag.sh green on free23 and returns requires_23ai on 18/21; the DBMS_VECTOR-forbidden path asserted with no egress; registered in run_all.sh.

acceptance_criteria: live_lineage.sh green under the feature lane; verified/drift/partial markers each asserted and logged; registered in run_all.sh.

title: Epic: Truthful MCP tool annotations + outputSchema
description:
Idea #4. structuredContent is ALREADY emitted (server.rs:516); the gaps are (a) MCP tool annotations and (b) declared outputSchema. ToolSpec/ToolDescriptor carry only input_schema + an internal `destructive` bool (tools.rs); the wire emits only name/description/inputSchema.
CRITICAL: MCP annotation spec DEFAULTS ARE UNSAFE -- omitted destructiveHint=true, openWorldHint=true, readOnlyHint=false, idempotentHint=false. A fail-closed read-only server MUST set all four explicitly on read tools, or an omitted annotation reads as "destructive, open-world." genai-toolbox actually ships Oracle tools advertised destructive while running read-only; we can be the server that gets this right.
Annotations stay advisory (clients MUST treat them as untrusted) -- the server-side classifier remains the real gate.
Source: res-protocol #2/#3, res-pgpro #10, res-oracle (a)#7.
close_reason: All annotation/outputSchema children are closed: tools/list emits explicit titles and all four MCP annotations, oracle_query/query/oracle_explain_plan declare outputSchema with NUMBER-as-string preserved, and conformance/e2e/golden/docs coverage is green.

title: Add MCP tool annotations to ToolSpec and emit them on the wire
description:
WHY/STATE: tools.rs ToolSpec has only input_schema + internal `destructive` bool; tools/list emits no annotations.
DO: add readOnlyHint/destructiveHint/idempotentHint/openWorldHint + title to ToolSpec and emit in tools/list. Map existing `destructive` bool. Set EXPLICIT values on every tool: read tools (oracle_query/explain_plan/describe*/get_*/search_*/list_*/sample_rows/capabilities) -> readOnlyHint=true,destructiveHint=false,idempotentHint=true,openWorldHint=false; the inert write/DDL tools -> readOnlyHint=false,destructiveHint=true.
ACCEPTANCE: every advertised tool carries all four hints explicitly (no omissions); regression test asserts NO read tool relies on the unsafe spec defaults.
SAFETY: annotations are advisory; the classifier stays the real gate.
SOURCE: res-protocol #2 (unsafe-defaults gotcha), res-oracle (a)#7.
close_reason: Implemented explicit MCP tool titles and advisory annotations on every tools/list descriptor; mapped registry destructive classification; updated conformance/docs/goldens; verified fmt, clippy, workspace tests, deny, boundary lint, sensitive-data lint, release preflight, and diff check.

title: Declare outputSchema for oracle_query + oracle_explain_plan
description:
WHY/STATE: structuredContent is ALREADY emitted (server.rs:516); only the declared outputSchema is missing.
DO: declare outputSchema (columns metadata, rows, rowcount, truncation flag, plan tree) on oracle_query + oracle_explain_plan so clients can validate typed results. CRITICAL: type NUMBER columns as string (preserve the NUMBER->string lossless invariant) -- do not let a schema reintroduce float coercion.
ACCEPTANCE: outputSchema present; structuredContent validates against it; NUMBER stays string in the schema; test.
SOURCE: res-protocol #3.
close_reason: Declared MCP outputSchema for oracle_query, query, and oracle_explain_plan; preserved NUMBER-as-string in the query cell schema; added registry, conformance, e2e, docs, and golden coverage; verified fmt, clippy, workspace tests, deny, boundary lint, sensitive-data lint, release preflight, and diff check.

title: Tests: annotations correct on wire, unsafe-default regression, outputSchema validates
description:
DO: golden/conformance assertions that each tool's annotations are present+correct, that read tools never omit a hint, and that structuredContent conforms to the declared outputSchema (incl. NUMBER-as-string). Structured logs.
ACCEPTANCE: green in CI.
close_reason: Satisfied by annotation and output-schema commits: conformance and e2e tests assert explicit annotations, unsafe-default regression, outputSchema preservation, and actual oracle_query structuredContent required fields with NUMBER-as-string. Full workspace gates passed in the output-schema bead.

title: Epic: Read-only DBA diagnostic tool suite
description:
Idea #2/#5/#14 -- the Postgres-MCP-Pro-style differentiator, a perfect fit for a read-only server (pure V$/DBA_* reads + Oracle's own advisor engines).
VERIFIED: oraclemcp-db/src/awr.rs already has top_sql_query + DiagnosticsSource{AwrAsh,Statspack} + detect_statspack + select_diagnostics_source (license-gated, AWR DBA_HIST_* needs Diagnostics Pack, falls back to Statspack STATS$*). NO MCP tool surfaces it yet -- so oracle_top_queries SURFACES existing code, it does not rebuild it.
Health subchecks map to Oracle: invalid objects (DBA_OBJECTS), unusable/unused indexes (DBA_INDEXES,V$OBJECT_USAGE), tablespace+UNDO headroom (DBA_TABLESPACE_USAGE_METRICS), sequence ceiling (DBA_SEQUENCES), disabled/NOVALIDATE constraints (DBA_CONSTRAINTS), buffer cache hit (V$BUFFER_POOL_STATISTICS/V$SYSSTAT). Advisor = DBMS_ADVISOR/DBMS_SQLTUNE (recommendations only, read-compatible; Tuning Pack gated).
Source: res-pgpro #1/#2/#3/#4/#7. All packs must be license-gated like the existing AWR path and preflighted in doctor.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer the read-only DBA diagnostic suite to a post-RC feature wave. It needs careful privilege degradation, Diagnostics/Tuning Pack gating, doctor preflight UX, SQL-shape tests, and live Oracle coverage; shipping it late would expand the surface area beyond the stable thin-native release candidate.
close_reason: superseded by the 0.4.0 plan bead conversion (oraclemcp-040-* epics)

ACCEPTANCE: tool registered with readOnlyHint=true; 'all' runs available subchecks; privilege-degraded views handled (DBA_*->ALL_*); unit + live tests.

title: oracle_top_queries: surface the existing awr.rs top-SQL engine
description:
WHY/STATE: awr.rs already has top_sql_query + DiagnosticsSource{AwrAsh,Statspack} + detect_statspack + select_diagnostics_source (license-gated). No MCP tool surfaces it.

Reuse awr.rs.

title: Epic: Agent ergonomics and scale
description:
Ideas #7-#13 + #15. Quality-of-life + scale features that mostly build on epic-proto-surface (capability negotiation + resources).
completion for owner->type->object autocomplete; cursor pagination; export-to-resource for large results; resource_link from oracle_query; unified search_objects with detail_level; schema-context/RAG retrieval; hypothetical-index what-if (INVISIBLE indexes); connection-scope isolation; polish (progress notifications, list_changed on level change, apply statement_cache_size).

title: Export-to-resource for large result sets (CSV/JSON, resource_link + file path)

DO: stream a read query to a temp CSV/JSON file, register an oracle-export://{id} resource, return a resource_link PLUS a local file path (for clients that cannot resolve resource URIs); expire on a timer. Proper CSV escaping (quote any field with comma/quote/newline).
ACCEPTANCE: export resolvable via resource + path; expiry works; CSV escaping correct; access-controlled identically to the originating query.
SOURCE: res-universal #6, res-protocol #5.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer. export-to-resource needs a secure temp-resource lifecycle, expiry, path/resource access control, CSV escaping, and follow-on resource_link behavior. Do not rush into the release candidate.

Return {count, results, truncated}. Handle quoted/case-sensitive identifiers + owner distinction. Keep existing describe tools (compat).

description:

ACCEPTANCE: shows before/after cost without affecting other sessions.

title: Epic: Wire OAuth/TLS/host-origin into the binary HTTP transport (S1)
description:
VERIFIED gap (S1): the shipped binary builds HttpTransportConfig::default() at crates/oraclemcp/src/main.rs:707 -> OAuth None, no TLS/mTLS, JSON-only (no SSE), empty host/origin allowlists, loopback gated behind mandatory --allow-no-auth. Code comment main.rs:753-755 verbatim: "the HTTP transport has no OAuth wired from config yet." The W10 ScopeGrant scope-enforcement is real but reachable only at the dispatch layer / by an embedding caller, NOT through the served HTTP transport -- yet README:121-128 and docs/behavior-inventory.md describe OAuth scope enforcement + TLS as live. This is a truth-in-advertising + security gap.
Operator decision (2026-06-18): WIRE it into the binary (not doc-patch). The auth machinery already exists in oraclemcp-auth (oauth_rs.rs OAuth2.1 RS, scope.rs RBAC, tls.rs rustls); this is config-plumbing, not new crypto.
Children: config plumbing, OAuth validation, TLS/mTLS, session-id validation, protected-resource metadata, e2e tests, doc reconcile.
close_reason: All HTTP auth/TLS child beads are implemented, tested, documented, and closed: config plumbing, OAuth scope enforcement, protected-resource metadata, TLS/mTLS listener, stateful session validation, transport golden/conformance tests, and docs reconciliation.

title: Plumb HttpTransportConfig from CLI/config instead of ::default()
description:
WHY/STATE: main.rs:707 hardcodes HttpTransportConfig::default() (auth off, JSON-only, empty allowlists).
DO: derive HttpTransportConfig from CLI flags + profile/config: OAuth issuer allowlist + audience (RFC8707) + required scopes, host/origin allowlists, JSON-vs-Streamable-SSE mode, stateful session toggle, TLS material paths. Add flags (e.g. --oauth-issuer/--oauth-audience/--require-scope/--allowed-origin/--tls-cert/--tls-key/--http-stateful) with safe fail-closed defaults.
ACCEPTANCE: serve --listen consumes a real config; unit tests cover flag->config mapping; default with no auth flags still refuses routable bind without explicit opt-in.
SOURCE: self-map S1.
close_reason: Implemented binary HTTP transport config from profiles.toml and CLI: host/origin allowlists, JSON/stateful toggles, OAuth resource/issuer/authorization-server/scope/HS256 secret references, fail-closed startup, protected-profile secret policy, and TLS/mTLS config rejection until the native listener is wired. Added unit coverage for flag-to-config mapping and updated README/behavior inventory/robot docs.

title: Wire OAuth 2.1 resource-server validation + scope->level into served HTTP
description:
WHY: W10 ScopeGrant ceilings are tested at the dispatch layer but never supplied by the served HTTP transport.
DO: in serve_http, validate bearer via oraclemcp-auth (oauth_rs.rs: alg=none rejected, issuer allowlist, audience binding, exp/nbf, HS256+injectable RS256/ES256), build ScopeGrant from scopes, carry it into ToolDispatch so apply_oauth_scopes lowers the effective ceiling (min(profile max_level, scope ceiling); protected profiles stay READ_ONLY).
ACCEPTANCE: e2e tests prove narrow token < broad token < profile ceiling, protected profile pinned READ_ONLY, missing/invalid token -> 401 + WWW-Authenticate, insufficient scope -> 403. All through the binary HTTP server, not just DispatchContext.
SOURCE: self-map S1, res-protocol #10.
close_reason: Implemented OAuth 2.1 bearer enforcement through served HTTP: valid tokens carry scopes into ToolDispatch, scope ceilings are applied end-to-end, missing/invalid tokens return 401 WWW-Authenticate, insufficient scope returns 403, metadata remains public, and narrow/broad/profile ceilings are covered by binary HTTP e2e tests. Verified: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh; scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh.

title: Wire rustls TLS/mTLS into the binary HTTP listener
description:
WHY: oraclemcp-auth/src/tls.rs build_server_config (rustls, optional client-cert verify) is never invoked by the binary.
DO: when TLS material is configured, wrap the TcpListener with rustls; support optional mTLS client-cert verification; keep plaintext path behind --allow-no-auth only.
ACCEPTANCE: TLS handshake test; mTLS required-client-cert test; plaintext still requires explicit opt-in.
SOURCE: self-map S1.
close_reason: Wired native rustls TLS/mTLS into the binary HTTP listener. Added serve_https/serve_https_until using the existing HTTP parser after TLS termination, proper close_notify teardown, CLI/config PEM loading, mTLS as an auth mode, accurate HTTP/HTTPS startup warnings, docs updates, server-only TLS handshake test, mTLS rejects-no-client-cert/accepts-client-cert test, and binary PEM-path config test. Verified: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo test -p oraclemcp-core http::tests::serve_https -- --nocapture; cargo test -p oraclemcp http_tls_material_builds_native_tls_config -- --nocapture; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh; scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh.

title: Validate mcp-session-id on subsequent stateful HTTP requests
description:
WHY/STATE: self-map: mcp-session-id is emitted on stateful init but NEVER validated on later requests.
DO: persist issued session ids; reject/relabel requests bearing unknown/expired session ids per Streamable HTTP semantics; keep stateless mode working.
ACCEPTANCE: test that a forged/stale session id is rejected; valid session id round-trips.
SOURCE: self-map protocol gaps.
close_reason: Implemented stateful Streamable HTTP mcp-session-id validation. Initialize registers issued ids, subsequent stateful POST/DELETE requests require a known id, forged/missing ids are rejected before dispatch, DELETE invalidates ids so stale ids fail, and listener wrappers install a session store automatically. Updated golden harness and behavior inventory. Verified: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo test -p oraclemcp-core http::tests::stateful -- --nocapture; cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --test golden_behavior golden_http_stateful_streamable_session -- --nocapture; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh; scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh.

title: Serve protected-resource metadata + WWW-Authenticate from the binary
description:
WHY: RFC 9728 protected-resource metadata route and WWW-Authenticate are part of the OAuth RS contract but only reachable via non-default config.
DO: when OAuth is enabled, serve /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource (public) and emit WWW-Authenticate pointing at it on 401.
ACCEPTANCE: metadata document validates against RFC 9728; 401 carries correct WWW-Authenticate.
SOURCE: res-protocol #10, self-map.
close_reason: Verified and tightened binary HTTP OAuth metadata/challenge contract: /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource is public and returns resource, authorization_servers, bearer_methods_supported, and scopes_supported; 401 challenges point at the metadata URL exactly; invalid tokens include invalid_token; insufficient scope returns 403 with insufficient_scope. Verified through crates/oraclemcp/tests/e2e_http_oauth.rs plus cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace, cargo deny check, scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh, scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh.

title: End-to-end HTTP auth/scope/TLS golden + conformance tests through the binary
description:
WHY: the security claims must be proven at the transport boundary, with logging, before docs assert them.
DO: extend golden_behavior.rs + conformance to drive the actual served HTTP transport with: anonymous->refused, valid token+scope->allowed, narrow/broad/protected scope matrix, bad Origin->403, forged session-id->refused, TLS/mTLS handshake. Structured test logs.
ACCEPTANCE: all pass in CI (non-live); coverage matrix updated in tests/conformance/COVERAGE.md.
SOURCE: self-map quality posture.
close_reason: Completed served HTTP OAuth/Origin/session golden proof, binary HTTP OAuth scope/session e2e coverage, TLS/mTLS proof mapping, and conformance coverage matrix; gates passed.

title: Reconcile README + behavior-inventory with the now-wired HTTP security
description:
WHY: README:121-128 / behavior-inventory currently describe HTTP OAuth+TLS as live while the binary ships them off. After wiring, make docs exactly match reality (flags, defaults, fail-closed behavior).
DO: update README + docs/behavior-inventory to document the real flags/defaults; remove/replace the "no OAuth wired from config yet" note (main.rs:753-755) once true.
ACCEPTANCE: docs match the shipped binary; release_preflight doc checks pass.
SOURCE: self-map S1.
close_reason: Reconciled README, CHANGELOG, robot docs, CLI help, and behavior inventory with wired HTTP OAuth/TLS/mTLS behavior; removed stale TLS-rejected/proxy-only wording; release_preflight and full gates passed.

title: Epic: Positioning and documented guarantees
description:
Convert the research's converged positioning into documented, test-backed guarantees: "the safe-by-construction, precision-correct, single-binary read-only Oracle MCP." Neither SQLcl (privilege-delegated, write-capable, JVM) nor genai-toolbox (Query-vs-Exec, no parser, flag-flippable, NUMBER->lossy float) statically guarantees no mutation, and genai-toolbox loses NUMBER precision (int64/float64).
Source: res-oracle (b) differentiators.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer external comparison copy. The RC now has test-backed NUMBER string fidelity and direct safety claims; SQLcl/genai-toolbox comparison text needs current source verification and citations to avoid stale or overbroad release claims.
close_reason: superseded by the 0.4.0 plan bead conversion (oraclemcp-040-* epics)

title: Document NUMBER->string as a guarantee with a differential test vs lossy float
description:
WHY: genai-toolbox maps NUMBER to int64/float64 (lossy); oraclemcp's NUMBER->string is a concrete correctness win.
DO: add a differential test proving high-scale/large NUMBERs survive losslessly as strings (and would lose precision as float); document NUMBER->string as an explicit guarantee in README.
ACCEPTANCE: test passes; README states the guarantee.
SOURCE: res-oracle (b)#2.
close_reason: README documents Oracle NUMBER cells as strings by default, and crates/oraclemcp-db/tests/type_fidelity.rs now proves a 38-digit NUMBER round-trips exactly by default while numbers_as_float=true emits a lossy JSON number. Verified: cargo test -p oraclemcp-db --test type_fidelity.

title: README positioning + comparison vs SQLcl / genai-toolbox
description:
DO: add a positioning section: "safe-by-construction, precision-correct, single-binary read-only Oracle MCP." Contrast with SQLcl MCP (privilege-delegated, write-capable, JVM, stdio-only) and genai-toolbox (Query-vs-Exec, no classifier, flag-flippable, NUMBER-lossy). Lead with the fail-closed classifier; note zero-runtime (no JVM/Python/Instant-Client) deploy.
ACCEPTANCE: README section accurate + cited; no overclaiming.
SOURCE: res-oracle (b).
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer external comparison copy. The RC now has test-backed NUMBER string fidelity and direct safety claims; SQLcl/genai-toolbox comparison text needs current source verification and citations to avoid stale or overbroad release claims.

title: Epic: Serve and advertise the built-but-unserved MCP protocol surface
description:
VERIFIED gap (S2): crates/oraclemcp-core/src/server.rs:193-195 advertises only {"capabilities":{"tools":{}}}; the dispatcher (server.rs:369-373) handles only initialize / notifications/initialized / tools/list / tools/call. But resources.rs (resource templates like oracle://object/{owner}/{type}/{name} + a 5-playbook prompt catalog) and subscriptions.rs (resources/subscribe + resources/updated) are fully built, exported, and tested -- just never served. Spec-compliant clients gate resources/list, prompts/list, resources/subscribe, completion/complete on capability negotiation, so this shipped code is DEAD to strict clients.
Idea #1 (highest ROI, additive, no new safety surface): advertise + wire the dispatcher arms.
Source: res-protocol #1, self-map S2. Spec revision in tree: protocolVersion 2025-11-25 (server.rs:193).
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer the remaining unserved subscription/update surface. The stable RC serves and advertises resources/list, resources/read, resources/templates/list, prompts/list, and prompts/get with conformance coverage; resources/subscribe remains intentionally unadvertised until durable DBMS_CHANGE_NOTIFICATION or polling semantics are designed and tested.
close_reason: superseded by the 0.4.0 plan bead conversion (oraclemcp-040-* epics)

title: Advertise resources/prompts/completions/listChanged in initialize
description:
WHY/STATE: server.rs:194 emits only {"tools":{}} so built resources/prompts/subscriptions are invisible to compliant clients.
DO: emit capabilities reflecting what is actually served: tools{listChanged}, resources{subscribe,listChanged}, prompts{listChanged}, completions{} -- only flip each on as its dispatcher arm lands (do not advertise unserved features).
ACCEPTANCE: initialize result advertises served capabilities; conformance asserts negotiation; nothing advertised that 404s.
SOURCE: res-protocol #1, self-map S2. Additive, no new safety surface.
close_reason: Advertised only served MCP capabilities: tools/listChanged=false; added conformance coverage proving resources, prompts, and completion remain unadvertised until dispatcher arms exist; refreshed golden transcripts.

title: Wire resources/list + resources/read + resources/templates/list arms
description:
WHY: resources.rs (16KB, templates incl. oracle://object/{owner}/{type}/{name}) is built but not dispatched.
DO: add server.rs dispatcher arms for resources/list, resources/read, resources/templates/list backed by resources.rs; enforce that resource reads honor the active session lease + operating level + per-DB ceiling (only expose what the current grant can see).
ACCEPTANCE: resources resolve; authz parity with reads; golden+conformance tests.
SAFETY: resource reads route through the same fail-closed/authz path as oracle_query.
SOURCE: res-protocol #1, res-universal (schema-as-resources).
close_reason: Wired served MCP resource methods: initialize advertises resources with subscribe/listChanged false; resources/list and resources/templates/list respond; resources/read serves capabilities/tools directly and schema/object resources through the existing guarded tool dispatch path; prompts/completion/session resources remain unadvertised. Covered by conformance, golden fixtures, full workspace tests, and release gates.

title: Wire prompts/list + prompts/get dispatcher arms
description:

ACCEPTANCE: prompts resolve; conformance tests; advertised only after this lands.

close_reason: Served the built-in prompt catalog over prompts/list and prompts/get, advertised prompts only after handlers existed, validated prompt arguments with structured JSON-RPC error data, and kept completion unadvertised. Covered by conformance, golden fixtures, full workspace tests, and release gates.

title: Wire resources/subscribe + resources/updated notifications
description:

Shipping this late would expand the protocol surface and risk unstable promises; current initialize truthfully advertises resources.subscribe=false.

Assert unserved arms such as completion and subscriptions remain unadvertised until implemented; subscription behavior is tracked separately in oraclemcp-epic-proto-surface-57t.4.

title: Decision: keep oracle_session (guarded-write router) UNWIRED in the read-only binary
description:
WHY/STATE: session_tool.rs:194-336 implements a full lease/escalation/transaction router with step-up, but it is deliberately NOT in TOOL_NAMES (verified absent from registry.rs). AGENTS.md:46-48: the guarded-write machinery is built but deliberately not surfaced by THIS binary (writes are the sibling plsql-mcp's job).
DO: record this as an explicit, durable decision (code comment + behavior-inventory line) so a future agent does not "fix" the unserved tool by surfacing writes. If writes are ever desired they belong behind a separate, opt-in build, not the read-only release.
ACCEPTANCE: decision documented next to session_tool.rs and in behavior-inventory.
close_reason: Documented the durable decision next to session_tool.rs and in behavior-inventory: oracle_session remains unadvertised in the read-only oraclemcp binary unless a future explicit opt-in surface adds separate gates. Verified fmt check, registry surface test, release preflight, and diff check.

title: Epic: Ship v0.3.0 thin-native release honestly
description:
The v0.3.0 thin-native release candidate is implemented locally on oracledb 0.2.2. The branch contains committed work for thin-native config/profile handling, live-XE evidence, served HTTP OAuth/TLS/mTLS, MCP resources/prompts, tool annotations, output schemas, NUMBER string-fidelity proof, and explicit post-RC deferrals for larger feature waves.

Remaining release gates are outward-facing and operator-gated: push the local commits to origin, then cut/tag/publish v0.3.0 across crates.io / GitHub / GHCR / MCP registry only after explicit authorization. AGENTS.md requires pushed work to be up to date with origin, but also forbids push/publish without operator go-ahead.

Children: release verification, live-xe evidence, push committed RC body, cut/tag/publish.

title: Epic: Read-only defense-in-depth + auditability
description:
Ideas #3 + #6. Double down on the core identity (safe-by-construction read-only).
(1) Session-level read-only backstop so the guarantee is two-layer, not classifier-only -- even a hypothetical classifier bug cannot mutate. (2) SQLcl-style auditability: per-statement marker comment + dynamic V$SESSION tagging so a DBA can see/trust MCP traffic WITHOUT trusting the client. (3) prompt-injection output fencing for returned rows.
VERIFIED: connection.rs:504-537 already applies DBMS_APPLICATION_INFO.SET_MODULE/SET_ACTION/SET_CLIENT_INFO + client_identifier from profile session_identity (static) -- so dynamic LLM/agent tagging EXTENDS existing plumbing, not net-new.
Source: res-universal #2/#8, res-oracle (a)#1/#2 (DBTOOLS$MCP_LOG, /* LLM in use */ marker, MODULE/ACTION).
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer as a post-RC defense-in-depth wave. The RC already preserves the fail-closed SQL guard and profile/session gates; adding transaction backstops, audit-marker comments, dynamic V tags, and output fencing changes DB session/response semantics and needs a dedicated live regression cycle instead of late-release churn.
close_reason: superseded by the 0.4.0 plan bead conversion (oraclemcp-040-* epics)

title: Session-level read-only backstop: SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY per request
description:
WHY: today read-only is classifier-ONLY. Peers gain defense-in-depth from a DB-enforced read-only session (dbhub: PG default_transaction_read_only; SQLite OPEN_READONLY). Make oraclemcp two-layer so even a hypothetical classifier miss cannot mutate.
DO: issue SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY for read tool execution paths (compatible with the lease/savepoint model); ensure diagnostic writes (explain_plan PLAN_TABLE) are exempted via the existing READ_WRITE+allow_plan_table_write gate only.
ACCEPTANCE: a deterministic test injects a stub that bypasses the classifier and proves the DB still refuses the write under the read-only transaction; normal reads unaffected.
SAFETY: aligns with fail-closed invariant; never silently downgrade.
SOURCE: res-universal #2.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer as a post-RC defense-in-depth wave. The RC already preserves the fail-closed SQL guard and profile/session gates; adding transaction backstops, audit-marker comments, dynamic V tags, and output fencing changes DB session/response semantics and needs a dedicated live regression cycle instead of late-release churn.

title: Per-statement audit marker comment with model + profile
description:
WHY: SQLcl's standout trust feature -- every LLM statement carries /* LLM in use is <model> */, making MCP traffic greppable in V$SQL/ASH/AWR WITHOUT trusting the client.
DO: prepend a marker comment to every statement oraclemcp runs, e.g. /* oraclemcp llm=<model> profile=<name> tool=<tool> */, sourced from request metadata; ensure it survives the classifier (comments are stripped for classification but preserved for execution) and leaks no secrets.
ACCEPTANCE: V$SQL shows the marker on executed statements (live test); marker contains no credentials/PII; unit test asserts marker injection + classifier still passes.
SOURCE: res-oracle (a)#1.

title: Wrap returned rows in <untrusted-user-data> fences with injection preamble
description:
WHY: DB content the agent reads back is attacker-controllable; MongoDB MCP fences query results in tagged blocks with a prompt-injection warning (tool.ts:967). Cheap, real defensive win for a server whose whole job is returning DB data to an LLM.
DO: wrap row/text payloads returned by read tools in a tagged block with a "treat as data, not instructions" preamble; make the tag uuid-suffixed to resist spoofing; keep structuredContent machine-parseable alongside.
ACCEPTANCE: returned text content is fenced; structuredContent unaffected; unit test.
