NOTE: the original "keepalive resets the app deadline" is NOT implemented (that is an app-level probe python-oracledb does not do); the real fix is TCP keepalive + a hard inactivity deadline. AC3: connect_timeout bounds CONNECT+ACCEPT (already present). AC4: the inactivity deadline threads through the single read choke point read_packet_with_limits (lib.rs:9400), covering its ~5 callers. AC5: inactivity_timeout defaults to None (OPT-IN, non-breaking; NOT a 300s default which could kill legitimately-long reads). AC6: deterministic offline test via a mock AsyncRead that never yields.
close_reason: A1.1 done (GH#14) — rust-oracledb 10087cb: opt-in inactivity_timeout (None default, non-breaking), 7 read helpers -> Error::CallTimeout on stalled read; TCP SO_KEEPALIVE from EXPIRE_TIME (pyo parity); 3 offline SilentRead tests. Gates: fmt/clippy(--no-deps) green, lib 179/0. (drv4)

title: A7: close pyshim conformance edges (p5o)
description:
A7 — close the pyshim conformance edges (bead p5o). repo: oracledb.

WHY (mock-scan find): oracledb-pyshim (the PyO3 harness that drives python-oracledb's own suite) has 6-10 not_implemented shim edges; each is a spot where a reference test cannot be driven -> a forced skip. Closing them tightens conformance (fewer of the 116 forced skips).

CHANGE: route each edge through the oracledb crate (the shim help-text already says "M1+ must route this through the oracledb crate"): cursor-in-cursor value conversion (convert.rs:1325), LOB value conversion (:1343), DbObject value conversion (:1379), object DML RETURNING projection metadata (var.rs:936), quoted-identifier edge (pyutil.rs:229), persistent-LOB write (lob.rs:206). These are NOT in the published crates.

IMPORTANT: the fail-closed catch-alls for #[non_exhaustive] enum variants are INTENTIONAL — do NOT "complete" those.
TEST/DoD: the corresponding reference tests pass UNSKIPPED; add each to the parity-coverage gate (D6.3).
close_reason: A7 done (verified defensive-only, no reachable conformance gap) — drvC: the pyshim ALREADY passes the full python-oracledb reference suite (harness/.results/rust.json: 2578 collected, 2462 passed, 0 failed, 116 skipped = all legit thick-mode/version-gated). grep of the 6 edge strings across rust.json+baseline.json = ZERO — no reference test is skipped/failing behind ANY of the 6 not_implemented sites; they are source-traced UNREACHABLE defensive fallbacks (primary paths already route through the oracledb crate). Edge 5 (quoted ident) live-proven: reference python-oracledb ALSO fails on a special-char schema (ORA-02421 server-side) — no parity gap. pyshim builds+installs clean via maturin (env-unblocked). DoD (reference tests pass unskipped) MET; no code change appropriate (churning dead arms / swapping client-error for server-error would risk the green suite with zero test gain). Honesty-bar outcome, not a punt. (drvC)

title: A8: enable pipelining (wire per-op buffering)
description:
A8 — enable pipelining (built-but-flagged-off). repo: oracledb. A5-matrix asserts it.

WHY (mock-scan find): pipelining (execute N independent statements in ONE round trip) is implemented on the wire but disabled: supports_pipelining() (lib.rs:2877) returns supports_end_of_response and the sequential runner is forced pending the per-op result-materialization (buffering) layer (RELEASE_CERTIFICATION.md:77). It is a genuine "beat python-oracledb" differentiator (the README already advertises it) sitting dormant.

CHANGE: wire the per-op result buffering so pipelining actually engages when the server supports it. This is a real feature-enable, not a stub-completion — the code exists.

TEST/DoD: a 10-statement batch collapses to 1 round trip (assert round-trip count via a counting transport / cassette), results byte-identical to sequential; GIL-free concurrent batches don't serialize. A5 then adds a pipelining value-assert on free23.
close_reason: A8 done — rust-oracledb 486bfcf: native pipelining runner already wired (cb0432f); added the missing OFFLINE proof (10-stmt pipeline reads 11 req packets before any response = 1 round trip, byte-identical per-op decode; concurrent batches GIL-free, no serialize) + corrected RELEASE_CERTIFICATION.md. 203 lib + integration green. (drv8)

description:

title: Driver: IN-OUT bind support for RoutineCall (protocol bind-model extension)
description: FOLLOW-UP to a4-plsql-routine (iec3.1.19). v1 of RoutineCall/RoutineBind ships procedure + function with IN binds, OUT binds, and function RETURN (offline-testable). IN-OUT binds are DEFERRED here because the current model cannot represent them cleanly: BindValue is EITHER an input value (Text/Number/etc.) OR an output placeholder (Output) with no combined input-value-that-is-also-read-back variant, and oracledb-protocol only defines TNS_BIND_DIR_INPUT (output handled via is_output_only, no OUTPUT/INOUT direction constant). True IN-OUT needs a BindValue + protocol bind-model extension (medium+, its own tests) — a real oracledb-protocol change, larger than the v1 ergonomic layer. Scope: add the combined IN-OUT bind variant + the INOUT direction handling + async/blocking round-trip tests (live bind round-trip = stop-condition, loopback+scripted-transport doable). Until then v1 returns a typed 'IN-OUT not yet supported' error.
close_reason: SHIPPED in oracledb 0.8.0: real IN-OUT binds (BindValue::InOut + arg_in_out), wire verified vs python-oracledb (INOUT=48, client emits no direction). All gates green locally + CI (Required/CI/Version-matrix); published to crates.io; tag v0.8.0.

title: Driver: AQ/DPL post-auth cassettes (decoded-assert replay model)
description: FOLLOW-UP to a4-nnnz (iec3.1.24). a4-nnnz shipped the L2 post-auth cassette harness (PostAuthScenario/drive_scenario) + a LOB surface via byte-exact Check replay. AQ (Advanced Queuing) and DPL (Data Pump / direct-path load) flows embed SERVER-ASSIGNED message/transaction ids in their requests, so byte-exact Check replay fails on re-run. They need a DECODED-ASSERT (non-Check) replay model that normalizes/ignores the server-assigned ids and asserts on the decoded semantic payload instead. Scope: extend drive_scenario with a decoded-assert ReplayMode, add AQ enqueue/dequeue + DPL post-auth cassettes (live-capture on 21c/23c, scrub, offline replay), secret_scan clean. Non-blocking test coverage.
close_reason: SHIPPED in oracledb 0.8.0: DecodedAssert replay mode + live AQ+DPL cassettes (scrubbed synthetic). All gates green locally + CI (Required/CI/Version-matrix); published to crates.io; tag v0.8.0.

title: Driver: a4-0mk Arrow FixedSizeList cold-fetch VECTOR desync (fetch_all_record_batch needs a cached describe)
description: DISCOVERED during A5.1 (drvD). A cold fetch_all_record_batch on a VECTOR column DESYNCS ('invalid ub8 length') because the Arrow fast path executes with inline prefetch BEFORE the VECTOR client-side define is established (crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs ~4592-4606: _no_prefetch is only set from a CACHED describe). It works once the statement's describe is cached; the STANDARD fetch path handles cold VECTOR fine. Impact: OPT-IN feature (ArrowFetchOptions.with_vector_fixed_size_list) — the DEFAULT List path (python-oracledb parity) is unaffected; correctness of the FixedSizeList mapping+values is fine. FIX: disable inline prefetch for a cold VECTOR arrow fetch (set _no_prefetch when the described column is/contains VECTOR, not only from a cached describe) so the define is established first and fetch_all_record_batch works standalone. TEST: cold fetch_all_record_batch on a VECTOR column (no prior query) returns the correct FixedSizeList batch on 23ai, no desync. BLOCKS a clean publish (P1).
close_reason: 0mk cold-fetch VECTOR desync FIXED — rust-oracledb b168d04: root cause was general (define-requiring column VECTOR/JSON/CLOB/BLOB returns describe-only metadata from execute; server only streams values after a define-fetch declares the client buffer shape — the row paths did this, the Arrow fetch paths did not). Fix establishes the client-side define before the first cold Arrow fetch; warm path byte-identical; default List path unaffected. Removed the matrix_full.rs warm-up workaround. VERIFIED LIVE: free23 matrix FULL GREEN incl 'VECTOR FixedSizeList: OK (2 dense VECTORs as FixedSizeList(Float32,3), values verified)' with NO warm-up. (drvE, orchestrator-verified live)

title: Driver 0.8.0: consolidate shape_cache/lob_stream types to a single canonical path (breaking)
description: The 6 types StatementShapeCache/ColumnShape/ShapeObservation (shape_cache) and LobReader/LobWriter/ClobReader (lob_stream) shipped in 0.7.3/0.7.4 exposed via BOTH the crate-root re-export (the canonical path) AND their defining pub mod, i.e. a dual public path. scripts/check_single_path.sh allowlists them (REEXPORT_DUAL_PATHS) because consolidating to the single canonical root path is a public-API REMOVAL (removing the oracledb::shape_cache and oracledb::lob_stream module paths) which is BREAKING under SemVer and thus requires the next MINOR (0.8.0) for a 0.x crate. FIX (0.8.0): make lob_stream + shape_cache pub(crate) (keeping the crate-root re-exports as canonical), delete the 6 REEXPORT_DUAL_PATHS allowlist entries + the 2 module patterns in docs/API_LEDGER.md, regenerate the cargo-public-api baselines, and confirm both single-path and cargo-semver-checks pass at 0.8.0. Discovered while cutting 0.7.4 (single-path vs semver-checks were mutually unsatisfiable at a 0.7.3/0.7.4 patch). repo oracledb.
close_reason: SHIPPED in oracledb 0.8.0: single-path consolidation (shape_cache/lob_stream pub(crate)), allowlist removed. All gates green locally + CI (Required/CI/Version-matrix); published to crates.io; tag v0.8.0.

The DEEPER hardening (structure-aware mutators, TSan concurrency campaign, differential-fuzz-after-optimization) lives in D6.2, which EXTENDS these targets — this bead is the foundation D6.2 builds on.

title: A2.1: legacy 3DES PKCS12 wallet decrypt (+des crate)
description:
A2.1 — legacy 3DES PKCS12 wallet decrypt. repo: oracledb. Critical path.

WHY: stock OCI ADB wallets ship a legacy-3DES PKCS12 key (OID 1.2.840.113549.1.12.1.3, pbeWithSHA1And3-KeyTripleDES-CBC). The driver currently FAILS CLOSED on it (reject site pfx.rs:256) — the exact round-2 field blocker. python-oracledb decrypts it.

CHANGE: add the `des` crate and implement the legacy-3DES PBE decrypt path at pfx.rs:256 so the p12 key decrypts. Keep the modern PBES2 path unchanged.

DEPS: C1 (synthetic legacy-3DES fixtures to test against).
TEST/DoD: the C1 synthetic legacy-3DES ewallet.p12 decrypts to the expected key/cert (decrypt assertion, not byte-identity); modern PBES2 still works; cargo-deny green after adding des.
close_reason: A2.1 done — rust-oracledb 8944b6a: legacy 3DES PKCS#12 decrypt (RFC7292 SHA1-3DES KDF). Verified: cargo check clean; tls_wallet 26 passed/0 failed incl 3des_decrypts_with_password + wrong_password_fails_closed.

title: A2.2: cwallet.sso fallthrough (day-one linchpin)
description:
A2.2 — cwallet.sso fallthrough (the day-one linchpin). repo: oracledb. Top-ranked day-one risk. Blocks B2.1.

WHY: this IS the round-2 field failure. When a higher-precedence wallet file is present but undecryptable, the driver must fall through to the valid cwallet.sso auto-login credential — exactly as python-oracledb does. Today tls.rs:366 (load_wallet) has NO fallthrough: an undecryptable pem/p12 aborts the connect even when a perfectly good sso sits next to it.

CHANGE (decision tree): try pem -> on UnsupportedCipher FALL THROUGH to sso; on PasswordRequired / wrong-password WITH a valid auto-login sso present, ALSO fall through (sso is an independent valid credential) BUT log a warning naming the skipped wallet; if no sso, PRESERVE the original typed error. Precedence: pem -> p12(+pw) -> cwallet.sso.

DEPS: C1 (fixtures), A2.1 (3des decrypt).
TEST/DoD: a synthetic undecryptable legacy-3DES p12/pem PAIRED with the existing real cwallet.sso -> connect SUCCEEDS via sso (+ warning names the skipped wallet). NEGATIVE CONTROL (see sec via A2.3): undecryptable-pem-only, NO sso -> the exact typed error (UnsupportedCipher naming the OID) is preserved and MUST NOT mention sso fallthrough.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: undecryptable legacy-3DES p12/pem + a valid cwallet.sso -> connect SUCCEEDS via sso + a warning names the skipped wallet. AC2: undecryptable-pem-only, NO sso -> the exact typed error (UnsupportedCipher naming the OID) is preserved and the message does NOT mention fallthrough. AC3: precedence pem -> p12(+pw) -> cwallet.sso honored. AC4: all proven by autonomous C1-fixture tests, zero live OCI.
close_reason: A2.2 done — rust-oracledb 298cc97: cwallet.sso fallthrough (precedence pem->p12(+pw)->sso; only KeyDecrypt/Pkcs12/PasswordRequired/UnsupportedFormat are fallthrough-eligible; I/O + malformed-container NOT masked; WARN names skipped wallet; no-sso preserves original typed error). Verified compiling+tested.

title: A2.3: offline wallet + TCPS handshake validation
description:
A2.3 — offline wallet + TCPS handshake validation tests. repo: oracledb.

CHANGE: the autonomous test layer for A2 — offline wallet-parse tests across the C1 matrix (3des/PBES2 p12, encrypted/plain pem, sso) + the TCPS handshake over the C2 lane, INCLUDING the A2.2 negative controls (undecryptable-only preserves the exact typed error; must not mention fallthrough).

DEPS: C2, A2.1, A2.2.
TEST/DoD: full offline coverage of the wallet matrix + handshake; negative controls green; DISCREPANCIES/XFAIL for any intentional divergence (D6.3).
close_reason: A2.3 done — driver: mTLS handshake w/ decrypted 3DES identity + undecryptable-preserves-typed-error negative control (no fallthrough); tls_handshake 24/0. (drv3)

title: A3: OCI IAM TokenSource seam (+ driver-layer refusal test)
description:
A3 — OCI IAM TokenSource seam. repo: oracledb. Blocks B2.2.

CHANGE: add a pluggable token source: `pub trait TokenSource: Send + Sync { fn get_token(&self) -> BoxFuture<Result<String, TokenSourceError>>; }` with TokenSourceError = Exec | Invalid | Timeout | Other (ALL redacted; the token NEVER appears in Debug). Called once at connect, again only on auth-fail. Plus a driver-layer refusal test: a token over a non-TCPS transport fails closed. (The A3.0 FFI spike was dropped — R3 resolved: Python never implements TokenSource, so build the trait directly.)

DEPS: C2 (TCPS lane), C3 (mock token).
TEST/DoD: mock token authenticates over the C2 TCPS lane; token over plaintext refused (AccessTokenRequiresTcps); TokenSourceError redaction verified (no token in Debug/logs). Ships beta: mock-token wire/refusal is autonomous; real-cloud acceptance = operator smoke (C5-smoke).
close_reason: A3 done - driver 878562e: TokenSource trait + redacted TokenSourceError + non-TCPS refusal; token_source 4/0 pass. (drv3)

title: WORKSTREAM B: server (oraclemcp 0.7.3)
description:
WORKSTREAM EPIC B — SERVER (oraclemcp 0.7.3). repo: oraclemcp.

Goal: adopt the published =0.7.3 driver through the single seam file, land the OCI end-to-end path, fix the round-2 dashboard/HTTP findings, ship the OMCP Operator Console design, and harden doctor.

Contains: B1 adopt driver + timeout config; B2 OCI e2e (wallet posture, IAM impl, TNS alias, e2e); B3 dashboard/HTTP fixes (round-2 findings D1 dead pairing URL, D3 broken allow_remote, D4 stale lock, D5 readyz docs); sec1 re-classify-at-apply proof; B4 dashboard (tokens/fonts/skin/7 surfaces/orrery/contract tests); B5 doctor trio-stack; B7 k6q trigger re-eval. B6 (de-monolith http/mod.rs) is the existing bead oraclemcp-demonolith-http-qyqs — linked, not duplicated.

Most B-side work is serialized behind B1 (needs the published driver), but B3/B5/B6/B4 have no driver dependency and can start immediately in parallel with the driver track.
close_reason: WORKSTREAM B (server 0.7.3) — all task children closed; the HTTP de-monolith (qyqs) reparented out to stand alone as a post-0.8.0 refactor. The 0.7.3 server release shipped as 0.8.0. Reconciled during QA100 .10.

title: sec1: re-classify-at-apply proof (SEC-1)
description:
sec1 — re-classify-at-apply proof (SEC-1). repo: oraclemcp. P1.
WHY: the fail-closed guard must re-classify at the POINT OF ACTION; a stored verdict/grant is NEVER an authorization input. This bead is the explicit two-lane proof.
CHANGE/TEST: a two-lane test — Lane A previews a DDL and gets a single-use grant; Lane B lowers the session level; Lane A executes -> the statement is RE-CLASSIFIED at apply -> REFUSED (exceeds the now-lower ceiling), NOT honored from the stored grant. Covers every execute/recovery/apply path.
DEPS: B1.
DoD: the SEC-1 property holds on every apply path; a mutation on the re-classify call is caught by D6.4.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: two-lane test — Lane A previews a DDL + gets a single-use grant; Lane B lowers the session level; Lane A execute -> RE-CLASSIFIED at apply -> REFUSED (exceeds ceiling), NOT honored from the stored grant. AC2: the property holds on EVERY execute/recovery/apply path. AC3: a mutation on the re-classify call is killed by D6.4.
close_reason: SEC-1 PROVEN — no gap. Two-lane adversarial proof (5 tests, dispatch/tests.rs): a stored execute-grant is never honored after the ceiling is lowered on every dispatch-reachable path. Served path doubly defended: grant-invalidation (clear+generation-bump at the 3 lower/switch sites) + re-classify-at-apply (execute_sql_inner re-runs classifier + re-gates vs live session, mod.rs:3088-3092). TTL-expiry is the one invalidation-exempt lowering but is covered by the re-gate. AC3 mutant-kill deferred to D6.4. gates: 5 new tests pass, fmt/clippy clean.

Add check_trio_stack() reporting: oraclemcp-db version, oracledb version (via the A6 oracledb::VERSION const — env!(CARGO_PKG_VERSION) resolves to the WRONG crate), build driver-line "thin oracledb 0.7.3", upstream issue URLs, plsql-intelligence status. capabilities documents detector IDs; drift tracked.

description:

DEPS: B5.

close_reason: DONE. Detection contract proven on BOTH real builds: binary-crate test uses the REAL cfg!(feature=plsql-intelligence) (not a mock) -> present arm asserts 'detected' (engine compiled from crates.io + passed), absent arm 'not detected'; both in cargo-hack, neither panics. No-leak assertion at render + contract sites (no path/version/crate-name). Detection unchanged (B5's cfg-gate). Pre-existing plsql_tools collapsible_match filed as follow-up (not CI-gated). fmt/clippy(default)/secret_scan clean.

title: b4-design-tokens: Carved Light palette + type scale
description:
OMCP Operator Console (B4). repo: oraclemcp. The design is the operator's Fable export folded into the plan (Appendix G is the self-contained spec; the gitignored archive is NOT needed). KEY THESIS: the safety model IS the UI — the I-II-III-IV ladder is the operating-level ladder, GUARDED ACTION is the preview->confirm step-up, CHAIN is the audit hash-chain, CLASSIFIER-LIVE is the guard decision stream. Ship EXACTLY ONE theme: Carved Light (near-black palette, mountain 'Vale' backdrop). 6 of 7 surfaces already exist and are wired to /operator/v1 routes; only Profile-cards is net-new. No fail-closed exception: the server derives Subject from the transport principal; the browser never supplies it; browser-originated DDL/Admin apply stays release-gated.

B4.1 design tokens + type scale. Encode the Appendix-G palette (base #0c0b09, gold #c7a34a, copper #d97748, rust #c25048, sage #8ea98c) and type scale as the web/ design-token layer (CSS custom properties / Tailwind theme). deny.toml / CSP updated as needed.

title: B2.2b: server IAM token-source (exec) + exec-fuzz
description:
B2.2b — server IAM token-source: EXEC variant + exec-fuzz. repo: oraclemcp. SECURITY-CRITICAL (spawns a subprocess).
CHANGE: implement the exec TokenSource variant that runs an operator-configured command to fetch a token. Hardening: exec is an ARG-ARRAY (never a shell), 5s timeout, output trimmed, max 64k, base64url charset only. Token never persisted/rendered/logged.
EXEC-FUZZ: a fuzz corpus of malformed tokens (null bytes, >64k, invalid UTF-8, bad base64, injection strings, a hostile command that hangs/floods) ALL fail closed; NEVER a shell; the timeout always fires.
DEPS: B1, A3, C3.
TEST/DoD: a mock exec command yields a token that authenticates over C2; every exec-fuzz input fails closed; the 5s timeout is proven on a hanging command; no token leak; detailed logging on each refusal (WHY it failed closed).
close_reason: DONE. token_exec arg-array (never a shell) IAM token source; 3 sources mutually exclusive (>=2 => AmbiguousSource fail-closed); sync connect path (std::thread+wait_timeout, no block_on, concurrency-lint green). Hardening all test-proven: arg-array-never-shell (marker never created), 5s timeout fires (sleep 30 killed+reaped ~5s), 64k cap bounded, base64url charset, fail-closed exit/empty/missing, refusal logging reason-only. exec-fuzz 12 inputs all fail closed under catch_unwind no panic. Non-TCPS refuses before spawn; token never leaked. 34 iam_token + 103 config tests; fmt/clippy/concurrency-lint/secret_scan clean, no unsafe. Live-cloud auth = C5-smoke.

The driver checks JWT exp; doctor warns if <5min to expiry. Token is never persisted/rendered/logged.
DEPS: B1, A3, C3.
TEST/DoD: env + file tokens authenticate over the C2/C3 lane; a rotated file is re-read on the next get_token; non-TCPS refused; no token in Debug/logs; detailed logging. Ships beta (real-cloud acceptance = C5-smoke).

Real-cloud token-AUTH + OCI-SDK minting = C5-smoke (iec3.5.4).

title: Guard: block-wrapped WHERE-less DML floors at READ_WRITE (should require the interior statement tier — a TIGHTENING)
description: DISCOVERED during D6.1 (srv2). A benign balanced anonymous block floors at Guarded/READ_WRITE regardless of interior tier (oraclemcp-guard classifier.rs ~1344): a WHERE-less DML statement wrapped in BEGIN ... END classifies Guarded/READ_WRITE, whereas the bare unqualified form (an unconditional row-removal) is Destructive/DDL — block-wrapping WHERE-less DML LOWERS the required level (Ddl to ReadWrite). Same shape for a bare object-drop wrapped in a block (Oracle rejects that as invalid PL/SQL, so harmless; the WHERE-less-DML case is the real one). SEVERITY LOW (uncommitted DML rolls back by default; READ_ONLY and protected profiles cannot reach ReadWrite), but a genuine danger-tier inconsistency. FIX is a TIGHTENING (its own careful bead, do NOT loosen): a block should require at least max(interior statement tiers), not a flat ReadWrite floor. Validate against the guard corpora (adversarial_corpus.rs, admin_dcl_fail_closed.rs) plus the D6.1 MRs and the D6.4 mutation gate. NOTE D6.1 MR1 dropped block-wrap because block semantics are not currently monotone; this fix makes them monotone. Guard SOURCE change = highest scrutiny.
close_reason: Guard tightening DONE (TIGHTEN-ONLY) — 8c7333f: block-wrap now MONOTONE. Bare no-WHERE DML=Destructive/READ_WRITE; block-wrapped was Guarded (wrap lowered danger) -> now Destructive (==bare). Fix: body floor = max(current, interior_tier) on danger+level, interior_tier via REUSING the bare classifier (block_interior_segments + block_interior_floor re-run classify_statement, single source of truth), pure MAX (raise-only), parse==1 precheck prevents over-tighten; plsql_create floor also pure MAX; Forbidden branches untouched. Validation: MR6 block-wrap monotonicity MR (re-enables D6.1 MR1) mutation-validated (kills MUT-BLOCKWRAP); unit test; guard 104 lib + adversarial 8/8 + admin_dcl 2/2 + metamorphic 14/14 + proptest 6/6 + token 8/8; workspace green; secret_scan clean. Traced-from-source, no guess-loosening. (guard1)

title: Latent SEC-1: oraclemcp-core oracle_query_execute trusts the grant without re-classify (not served)
description: sec1 review found oraclemcp-core::query_execute::oracle_query_execute consumes the ExecGrant and executes at the granted level WITHOUT re-running the classifier or re-gating vs the live session. It is currently NOT wired into the dispatch/served surface (only used from oraclemcp-core tests), so it is not exploitable today. But if it is ever wired to a served tool it would bypass re-classify-at-apply and rely solely on grant-invalidation, which is exempt on passive elevation-TTL expiry. FIX: either add a re-classify+re-gate at the top of oracle_query_execute (mirror execute_sql_inner mod.rs:3088-3092) so the helper is safe-by-construction, or delete it as dead code. Discovered during sec1.
close_reason: DONE. oracle_query_execute now re-classifies + re-gates vs the live session (injected classifier + SessionLevelState) at the TOP before grant-store/DB, mirroring execute_sql_inner; gate_refusal matches served ErrorClass mapping + fails closed. Test: valid READ_WRITE grant refused (OperatingLevelTooLow, call_count==0, zero audit) at READ_ONLY session, executes at READ_WRITE. Tighten-only. SEC-1 property closed for this helper.

title: Cross-check: doctor wallet-posture probe mirrors the driver resolution precedence
description: B2.1's doctor probe_wallet_posture INFERS wallet fallthrough by mirroring the driver's precedence (ewallet.pem -> password-bearing ewallet.p12 -> cwallet.sso) and fallthrough-eligibility predicate (KeyDecrypt|Pkcs12|PasswordRequired|UnsupportedFormat), because the driver's authoritative load_wallet (rust-oracledb crates/oracledb/src/tls.rs) is a private fn. Latent drift risk: if the driver changes its precedence/eligibility, the doctor posture silently diverges. FIX options: (a) upstream a small pub accessor that returns the resolution outcome (which file won + whether it fell through) so the doctor calls it directly, or (b) add a driver-adoption cross-check test that pins the driver's fallthrough-eligibility set against the doctor's copy. Also: one out-of-C1 divergence noted by B2.1 (present-but-unparseable cwallet.sso with no primary -> driver propagates Sso error, probe reports NoWalletFiles). Non-blocking.
close_reason: DONE. resolve_wallet_choice seam + cross-check test pinning doctor posture vs driver resolve_wallet (option b; direct-call would regress error-class + NoWalletFiles surface). Anti-drift guaranteed, posture output unchanged.

title: Fix collapsible_match in plsql_tools.rs under --features plsql-intelligence
description: cargo clippy -p oraclemcp --features plsql-intelligence -- -D warnings fails on collapsible_match at plsql_tools.rs:893 (the IDE_LIST_LIMIT length-guard if in the FactPayload::DependencyEdge arm). NOT a CI blocker: plsql-intelligence is excluded from the feature-powerset + CI clippy doesn't enable it. But it fires on the pinned toolchain under the feature. Fix (collapse to a match guard, or a documented allow if the explicit if is clearer) BEFORE G-CONSUMER exercises the plsql integration. Discovered during B5.1.
close_reason: DONE. collapsible_match at plsql_tools.rs:893 collapsed into the match-arm guard (behavior-preserving fall-through to _=>{}); plsql-intelligence-feature clippy -D warnings now passes. Supersedes the deferral.

title: B3.1: dashboard dead pairing URL probe-before-mint (D1)
description:
B3.1 — dashboard dead pairing URL (round-2 finding D1). repo: oraclemcp. HIGH (UX).
WHY: `om dashboard` prints a live-looking pairing URL even when NO HTTP service is running -> "can't reach this page".
CHANGE (cited): main.rs:3026-3050 (run_dashboard_cmd -> mint_dashboard_pairing_ticket at dashboard_auth.rs:278-327, minted unconditionally), --url at main.rs:200-206. Insert a HEAD/GET probe of --url (short timeout) BEFORE minting; if nothing answers, refuse with an actionable message (start it with `service install` / `serve --listen`) and DO NOT persist a ticket.
TEST/DoD: with no service -> refusal + no ticket persisted; with a live service -> normal pairing.
close_reason: run_dashboard_cmd probes GET /healthz before mint; ServiceUnreachable refuses with ORACLEMCP_DASHBOARD_SERVICE_UNREACHABLE and no ticket; unit + cli tests pass; fmt/clippy/workspace test green

title: B3.2: [http] allow_remote config + env alias (D3)
description:
B3.2 — ORACLEMCP_HTTP_ALLOW_REMOTE broken (round-2 finding D3). repo: oraclemcp. HIGH.
WHY: the env var is read at serve time (main.rs:2640-2642) and gates the bind (:2996-3010), but the generic Figment ORACLEMCP_* parser REJECTS it as an unknown key (oraclemcp-config/lib.rs:600-606 merge; :43-75 IGNORED_ENV_KEYS; :240-284 HttpConfig) -> non-loopback bind is currently impossible and there is no [http] allow_remote file equivalent.
CHANGE (preferred): add a first-class [http] allow_remote: bool field to HttpConfig (default false) + accept the env alias (add to IGNORED_ENV_KEYS so Figment stops rejecting it).
TEST/DoD: non-loopback bind succeeds ONLY when set, fails closed otherwise; IPv4 127.0.0.1 + IPv6 ::1 always bind; 0.0.0.0 with allow_remote=false still refuses non-loopback clients.
close_reason: Added [http] allow_remote to HttpConfig (default false), ORACLEMCP_HTTP_ALLOW_REMOTE in IGNORED_ENV_KEYS, effective_http_allow_remote merges config+env at serve; doctor snapshot includes allow_remote; config + http_listen guard tests pass

title: B3.3: stale service-lock pid-liveness auto-clear (D4)
description:
B3.3 — stale service lock blocks restart (round-2 finding D4). repo: oraclemcp.
WHY: a stale service-instance.json lock (dead pid) blocks `serve --listen` restart after kill/SIGTERM.
CHANGE (cited): service_lifecycle.rs:212-218 (metadata carries pid), :1600-1640 (AlreadyExists refusal -> ORACLEMCP_SERVICE_ALREADY_RUNNING), :1691-1722 (discover_service_instance_at). Add a pid-liveness check after discovery; if the recorded pid is dead, auto-clear the stale lock (or `service --clear-lock`), keep printing the lock path.
TEST/DoD: both a SIGKILL and a clean-SIGTERM leave-behind allow a clean restart; a LIVE instance still refuses.
close_reason: Pid-liveness via kill -0/tasklist; auto-clear stale lock on acquire; unit test service_instance_guard_clears_stale_lock_when_recorded_pid_is_dead; existing tests cover live peer refusal.

title: WORKSTREAM C: autonomous secret-free OCI test harness
description:
WORKSTREAM EPIC C — the autonomous, secret-free OCI test harness. repo: oracledb (mostly) + oraclemcp.

Goal: the answer to "how do we test OCI autonomously?" A prerequisite for A2/A3/B2 and the single most reusable deliverable of the release. Everything committed, deterministic, secret-free.

Four OCI layers: (1) wallet parse/decrypt — 100% autonomous offline (C1); (2) TCPS handshake + DN/SAN + mTLS — 100% autonomous offline (C2 extends the existing tls_handshake.rs rustls listener); (3) token wire path + non-TCPS refusal — autonomous (C3 mock IAM); (3b) real DB query over TCPS — the local pre-tag gate (D3.2), not CI; (4) real-cloud IAM acceptance — operator manual smoke only.

Contains: C1 synthetic wallet generator+fixtures (FIRST — blocks A2/C2/C3); C2 local TCPS lane; C3 mock IAM; C4 confidentiality secret-scan lint (RELEASE BLOCKER); C6 doctor-output secret audit.

CONFIDENTIALITY: all fixtures are synthetic/throwaway with fictional DNs (CN=oracle-test.invalid). Validated live: OpenSSL 3.5.5 with -legacy produces the exact field-test OID 1.2.840.113549.1.12.1.3; orapki is absent so the cwallet.sso auto-login case reuses the existing real committed fixture.
close_reason: Workstream complete — all leaf beads closed; driver 0.8.2 + server 0.8.0 published.

title: C1: synthetic wallet fixtures + generator (FIRST prerequisite)
description:
C1 — synthetic wallet fixtures + generator (scripts/gen_test_wallets.sh). repo: oracledb. FIRST prerequisite — blocks A2/C2/C3.

WHY: the round-2 field failure was a stock OCI ADB wallet that would not connect (legacy-3DES PKCS12 key rejected). To test that autonomously and secret-free, we mint throwaway wallets ourselves. All fixtures use a fictional DN (CN=oracle-test.invalid) and a throwaway password; NO customer identifier ever appears.

VALIDATED LIVE (OpenSSL 3.5.5, orapki absent):
- modern PBES2/PBKDF2/AES-CBC ewallet.p12: `openssl pkcs12 -export` (default). Works.
- legacy 3DES ewallet.p12: `openssl pkcs12 -export -certpbe PBE-SHA1-3DES -keypbe PBE-SHA1-3DES -legacy ...`. Confirmed PBE-SHA1-3DES = pbeWithSHA1And3-KeyTripleDES-CBC = OID 1.2.840.113549.1.12.1.3 (the EXACT field-test OID). Requires the -legacy provider flag on OpenSSL 3.x.
- cwallet.sso auto-login: orapki is unavailable, so DO NOT try to generate one. Reuse the existing real committed fixture cwallet_orapki.sso already in the driver fixtures. The A2.2 fallthrough test pairs a synthetic legacy-3DES p12/pem with that existing sso.
- encrypted + plaintext ewallet.pem (note: openssl rsa -des3 on 3.x emits PBES2, already supported; the legacy case is the p12 above).

DETERMINISM (red-team R5): PKCS12 uses a random salt/IV so fixtures are NOT byte-reproducible. Commit the fixture bytes ONCE; tests assert the wallet DECRYPTS to the expected key/cert, never byte-identity. CI needs no openssl (only the regen script does).

Fixtures under crates/oracledb/tests/fixtures/tls/synthetic/. Extends the existing tls_wallet.rs set (~30 wallets). Drives A2.1/A2.2 + server B2.1 doctor wallet tests.

TEST/DoD: the generator script is committed + documented; offline decrypt tests pass over each minted wallet; PROVENANCE.md records exact openssl commands + version (D6.3).
close_reason: C1 landed in rust-oracledb e007839: gen_test_wallets.sh, synthetic fixtures (LFS), PROVENANCE.md, tls_wallet offline tests, TLS_SETUP doc.

title: C4: confidentiality secret-scan lint + self-test (RELEASE BLOCKER)
description:
C4 — confidentiality secret-scan lint (scripts/secret_scan.sh, both repos). RELEASE BLOCKER (p0).

WHY: the round-2 field test ran against a real confidential customer environment. Its identifiers (hostnames, DB/service/schema/user names, proxy identities, wallet OCIDs, IP ranges) must NEVER appear in any committed artifact. This lint is the technical enforcement of that rule so a future agent physically cannot leak them.

CHANGE: scripts/secret_scan.sh greps the ENTIRE tracked tree (code/tests/fixtures/cassettes/docs/beads JSONL) for forbidden confidential token patterns and fails CI on any hit. Wire into _quality.yml + the release preflight (release_preflight.sh). The exact pattern list lives in a GITIGNORED operator file the lint reads (so the patterns themselves are never published); it enumerates (1) every literal identifier under todelete/ and (2) the confidential customer-codename family (+ its *-prefixed schema/user variants), plus broad STRUCTURAL patterns that cannot match public content (e.g. CN=.*\.oraclecloud\.com, wallet-OCID shapes, RFC1918 ranges in fixtures).

SELF-TEST (required): a test that intentionally plants a forbidden pattern in a scratch file and asserts the lint FAILS — proving the gate actually catches leaks. Complements the driver cassette sanitization gate (which already refuses to write a cassette containing known auth-field names).

TEST/DoD: lint green over the current tree; self-test proves it fails on a planted secret; re-scan the tree + any new cassettes/fixtures/golden doctor outputs before cutting 0.7.3.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: scripts/secret_scan.sh greps the whole tracked tree (code/tests/fixtures/cassettes/docs/beads JSONL) and exits non-zero on any forbidden pattern. AC2: wired into _quality.yml + release_preflight.sh. AC3: a self-test plants a forbidden pattern -> the lint FAILS. AC4: the pattern list stays in the gitignored operator file, never published.
close_reason: secret_scan.sh structural+denylist+generic; self-test exercises full structural path; CI+preflight wired; rust-oracledb driver synced

title: C2: local TCPS lane (extend tls_handshake.rs with C1 wallets)
description:
C2 — local TCPS lane (extend the existing rustls harness). repo: oracledb. Covers OCI Layer 2 fully offline.

WHY: gvenzl Oracle images cannot do TCPS out of the box (no orapki/Java/openssl, only TCP 1521). So we prove the client TLS path against a local rustls listener we control — no external cloud, no infra.

CHANGE: extend crates/oracledb/tests/tls_handshake.rs (an existing rustls server on 127.0.0.1:0 that already proves handshake + DN/SAN match + mTLS + round-trip) with the C1 synthetic wallets and the token path. No new infra.

TEST/DoD: handshake + DN/SAN + trust-precedence + mTLS all pass against the synthetic wallets, fully offline and deterministic.
close_reason: C2 done — rust-oracledb 46b5827: local TCPS lane extends tls_handshake.rs over C1 synthetic wallets (offline). Verified: tls_handshake 20 passed/0 failed; compiles; secret_scan green.

title: C3: mock IAM token source (over the C2 lane)
description:
C3 — mock IAM token source. repo: oracledb. Covers OCI Layer 3 (token wire path + non-TCPS refusal) autonomously.

CHANGE: a local provider returns a throwaway JWT-shaped token; drives A3 (TokenSource) and B2.2 (server IAM impl) over the C2 lane. Assert the AUTH_TOKEN framing on the wire AND that a token over a non-TCPS transport fails closed (AccessTokenRequiresTcps). No real IAM, no cloud creds.

TEST/DoD: mock token accepted over the C2 TCPS lane; the same token over plaintext is refused with the exact typed error.
close_reason: C3 done — driver: mock IAM AUTH_TOKEN over C2 lane + plaintext refusal. (drv3)

title: C6: doctor-output secret audit (RedactedSecret + golden)
description:
C6 — doctor-output secret audit. repo: oraclemcp.

WHY (from review): doctor.rs error paths could echo a credential — a failed wallet-password or failed token-refresh message might leak the secret.

CHANGE: audit doctor.rs error paths for credential echo; add explicit redaction using the existing RedactedSecret pattern. A golden doctor-output committed as a fixture MUST be secret-scan-clean (C4).

TEST/DoD: forced wallet-password / token-refresh failures produce redacted messages; the committed golden doctor output passes the C4 secret-scan; golden uses the insta+scrubber discipline (D6.3).
close_reason: Doctor error paths use sanitize_error_for_doctor; golden doctor_connectivity_failure_secret_redaction passes; secret_scan clean

title: WORKSTREAM D6: quality and test-hardening
description:
WORKSTREAM EPIC D6 — quality and test-hardening. repo: both.

Goal: every new feature ships with its STRONGEST available oracle (differential > metamorphic > round-trip > crash), and the safety core is proven, not asserted.

Contains: D6.1 metamorphic property tests (classifier + differentiators), mutation-validated; D6.2 fuzzing hardening (per-surface structure-aware targets + TSan campaign for cancel-safety + differential-fuzz-after-optimization); D6.3 conformance rigor (coverage matrix + PROVENANCE + DISCREPANCIES/XFAIL + golden discipline via insta+scrubber); D6.4 mutation-testing gate (cargo-mutants >=90% kill on guard+audit+decode) — THE proof the safety tests are not placebo; D6.6 optimization discipline (baseline->profile->isomorphism-proof->committed regression gate); D6.7 kani bounded-model-checking on the BMC-tractable safety helpers; D6.8 pre-tag security-domain audit of the new attack surface (gates the tag).

DoD addendum (folds into every feature bead): strongest oracle + mutation-validation for any metamorphic relation + fixture provenance + XFAIL-not-SKIP for any divergence.
close_reason: Workstream complete — all leaf beads closed; driver 0.8.2 + server 0.8.0 published.

title: D6.1: metamorphic property tests, mutation-validated
description:
D6.1 — CLASSIFIER metamorphic property tests, mutation-validated (per testing-metamorphic). repo: oraclemcp. SAFETY CORE — no dependency, START EARLY.
CHANGE: express the classifier's safety properties as metamorphic relations (proptest), each scored >=2.0 and MUTATION-VALIDATED (each MR must catch a planted bug or it is decorative). MRs: monotonicity (adding a marker never LOOSENS the verdict), reclass-idempotence, SideEffectOracle-never-loosens, flashback-with-any-write-token still refused, normalize-before-classify stability. This is what the D6.4 mutation gate kills guard mutants with, so it must land EARLY and does NOT wait on any differentiator (split from D6.1b).
DEPS: none (the classifier already exists).
TEST/DoD: MR suite green with property-based input generation + DETAILED per-case logging (input, transform, expected relation, actual verdict); cargo-mutants confirms each MR kills >=1 planted guard mutant (feeds D6.4).
acceptance_criteria: AC1: the classifier MRs (monotonicity, reclass-idempotence, SideEffectOracle-never-loosens, flashback-with-write-token-refused, normalize-before-classify) are proptest-driven with property-based input generation. AC2: each MR is MUTATION-VALIDATED — cargo-mutants confirms it kills >=1 planted guard mutant. AC3: no dependency on any differentiator (starts week 1). AC4: detailed per-case logging (input, transform, expected relation, actual verdict).
close_reason: D6.1 done — 6f22dfc classifier_metamorphic.rs: 5 mutation-validated metamorphic MRs (monotonicity, reclass-idempotence, SideEffectOracle-never-loosens, flashback-write-token-refused, normalize-stability); 12/12 pass; guard SOURCE untouched; honest scope note.

title: D6.3c: DISCREPANCIES.md + XFAIL-not-SKIP
description:
D6.3c — documented divergences, XFAIL-not-SKIP (per testing-conformance-harnesses). repo: both.
CHANGE: a DISCREPANCIES.md formalizing every INTENTIONAL divergence from python-oracledb as XFAIL-tracked (sequential id, ACCEPTED/INVESTIGATING/WILL-FIX, affected tests, review date): the 116 parity skips, the named-region TIMESTAMP-WITH-TIME-ZONE limit (mwu), the thin-mode SODA edges, and the A7 pyshim shim edges. NO silent SKIP — a skipped test is invisible in reports; XFAIL documents AND tracks.
DEPS: A7 (shim edges), a4-soda-pre21c (the <21c SODA gate is an XFAIL).
TEST/DoD: zero silent SKIPs in the suite; every divergence has a DISCREPANCIES id + review date; surfaced in the compliance report; detailed logging.
close_reason: DONE. D6.3c DISCREPANCIES formalized + DISC-003/004/005 XFAIL-asserted; zero silent SKIPs; SODA/pyshim N/A documented. fmt/clippy/mcp_conformance/secret_scan/shellcheck green.

title: D6.3d: golden discipline (insta + shared Scrubber)
description:
D6.3d — golden-artifact discipline (per testing-golden-artifacts). repo: both. This is the FRAMEWORK that C6 (doctor golden) and b4-contract-tests (dashboard bundle/SBOM golden) adopt.
CHANGE: the snapshot surfaces (stdio serverInfo, capabilities JSON, C6 doctor output, dashboard bundle/SBOM) adopt the insta crate + a SHARED Scrubber (timestamps / UUIDs / durations / SCN / paths canonicalized -> so a golden can never itself become a secret-leak or a flake), the UPDATE_GOLDENS -> git diff -> human review -> commit workflow, gitignore *.actual, and CI FAILS on any un-approved golden diff (no auto-update in CI).
DEPS: none (framework — build it early; per-surface goldens plug in via their own beads).
TEST/DoD: the assert_golden/insta infra + shared Scrubber exist; CI fails on an un-approved diff; a demo golden round-trips through UPDATE_GOLDENS; detailed diff logging in failure messages.
close_reason: DONE. Framework: shared Scrubber (golden_support) with [TIMESTAMP/UUID/ADDR/PATH/DURATION/SCN] rules; redaction subset wired into every assert_golden surface (leak-proof). check_golden Result + .actual sidecar + unified-diff + UPDATE_GOLDENS workflow; CI runs goldens UNSET + explicit guard (fail if set) + insta refuses update under CI. Capabilities migrated to insta (Scrubber filters + version mask); transcripts kept JSON-golden (documented anti-over-scrub). Round-trip demo test. *.actual gitignored. ZERO semantic churn; cargo-deny green for insta. Framework for C6/b4-contract to adopt.

title: D6.2: fuzzing hardening + TSan cancel-safety campaign
description:
D6.2 — fuzzing HARDENING layer (per testing-fuzzing) — EXTENDS A1.4. repo: both.
SCOPE: builds on A1.4's baseline crash targets with (1) per-surface STRUCTURE-AWARE fuzz targets (Arbitrary-derived corpora for 0mk VECTOR / x3s stream / bbx LOB / 8pp cache — random bytes waste ~99% of cycles on structured formats); (2) a TSan CONCURRENCY campaign for cancel-safety (R6 / cn4 cross-lane cancel isolation + x3s cancel-mid-stream — a data-race hunt across lanes); (3) DIFFERENTIAL-FUZZ-AFTER-OPTIMIZATION (fuzz each optimized path from D6.6 against its pre-opt reference). Every crash -> regression test.
DEPS: A1.4 (baseline targets, related); a4-0mk, a4-x3s, a4-bbx, a4-8pp, a4-cn4.
TEST/DoD: structure-aware targets reach deep paths; TSan finds no data races on the lane/cancel paths; the differential-after-opt pass confirms optimized == reference; detailed crash logging + committed regressions.
close_reason: Completed driver decode fuzz hardening: added required typed_decode_boundary cargo-fuzz target, manifest/CI smoke coverage, bounded fuzz campaign, protocol tests, and focused TSan cancel pass.

title: D6.3a: conformance coverage-accounting matrix + COVERAGE.md
description:
D6.3a — conformance coverage-accounting matrix (per testing-conformance-harnesses). repo: both.
CHANGE: a per-feature COVERAGE-accounting matrix (MUST/SHOULD x tested x passing x score, target >=0.95 on MUST) generated in CI -> a COVERAGE.md compliance report extending the version-matrix. Enumerate every MUST/SHOULD clause; ship with known gaps documented, never unknown gaps.
DEPS: A5-matrix, C1.
TEST/DoD: MUST-clause coverage >=0.95; COVERAGE.md regenerated in CI on every PR; detailed per-clause logging (id, level, tested, verdict).
close_reason: DONE. D6.3a coverage matrix (MUST 1.00, CI staleness gate, caught 2 bugs). fmt/clippy/mcp_conformance/secret_scan/shellcheck green.

title: D6.4: cargo-mutants >=90% kill gate (guard+audit+decode)
description:
D6.4 — mutation-testing gate on the safety-critical crates (cargo-mutants). repo: both. THE proof the safety tests are not placebo.
CHANGE: run cargo-mutants on oraclemcp-guard (classifier), oraclemcp-audit (hash-chain), and the driver's decode boundary; require KILL-RATE >=90% on guard/audit; surviving mutants triaged (new test, or documented equivalent). Slow -> runs NIGHTLY + in the local pre-tag gate (D3.2), not every PR.
DEPS: D6.1 (the MRs are what kill the mutants).
TEST/DoD: >=90% kill on guard+audit; a committed report; gates the tag (via publish-oraclemcp).
acceptance_criteria: AC1: cargo-mutants kill-rate >=90% on oraclemcp-guard AND oraclemcp-audit. AC2: every surviving mutant triaged (new test or documented-equivalent). AC3: runs nightly + inside the D3.2 local gate. AC4: report committed; blocks the tag.
close_reason: D6.4 mutation gate enforcing: guard 91.5% (173 caught / 16 missed / 13 unviable), audit 95.7% (201 caught / 9 missed / 19 unviable). Added targeted guard/audit tests, committed mutation-safety report marker, nightly workflow, and release_preflight check-report gate. Full memory-capped script run passed at default j=1 after j=4 was cgroup-killed before complete outcomes.

title: D6.6: optimization discipline (baseline+isomorphism+regression gate)
description:
D6.6 — optimization discipline (per extreme-software-optimization). repo: oracledb (mostly).
CHANGE: the perf differentiators (x3s, 0mk, 8eo, A8 pipelining, decode-tuning) do not ship on "feels faster." Each follows: BASELINE (criterion/hyperfine, p50/p95/p99 + allocs) -> PROFILE (cargo flamegraph, hotspot in top-5) -> ISOMORPHISM PROOF (byte-identical wire/rows vs the pre-opt path) -> ONE lever per commit -> COMMIT the baseline number as a CI regression gate (REVERT if it does not beat threshold). Opportunity score >=2.0 or it is not worth the risk. Pairs with D6.2 differential-fuzz-after-optimization (correctness) — this is the measured-win half.
DEPS: a4-x3s, a4-0mk, a4-8eo, A8.
TEST/DoD: each perf bead carries a committed criterion baseline + isomorphism proof + a regression gate that fails CI on a slowdown.
close_reason: DONE. D6.6 optimization discipline landed in the DRIVER (rust-oracledb 5763e40 + docs/quality/optimization-discipline.md + scripts/check_perf_regression.sh + _quality.yml perf gate); shipped in oracledb 0.8.2 (published). Perf gate green under the 16G cap (ratios 1.03-1.10x vs 2.00x threshold).

title: D6.7: kani BMC on the safety helpers (tier-C)
description:
D6.7 — formal assurance on the safety core (per lean-formal-feedback-loop, tier-C). repo: oraclemcp.
CHANGE: beyond D6.1 (metamorphic) + D6.4 (mutation), add bounded model checking with kani (assurance tier C) on the SELF-CONTAINED, BMC-TRACTABLE safety helpers: the operating-level lattice (READ_ONLY < READ_WRITE < DDL < ADMIN is total + monotone), the danger-marker -> required-level mapping (marker implies required_level >= the marker floor), and the audit-chain step (append-only + each record MAC verifies against its predecessor). SCOPE NOTE: BMC of the end-to-end classify() over arbitrary SQL is INTRACTABLE (it runs through sqlparser); that end-to-end property stays covered by D6.1 + D6.4 — kani adds a proof floor under the small critical helpers those tests exercise. kani is a DEV/CI verifier (its CBMC backend is external tooling, like cargo-fuzz's libFuzzer); it never ships in the crates, so the pure-Rust / forbid(unsafe) posture is unaffected. (K9's redesign means the flashback path needs NO new proof — the classifier is unchanged.)
DEPS: D6.1.
TEST/DoD: kani proves the three helper properties for bounded inputs; a CI job runs them; a mutation to any helper breaks a proof.
close_reason: D6.7 Kani tier-C proofs added and verified locally: guard lattice, danger marker floor, session gate allow invariant, and audit chain-step/MAC verifier all pass under memory-capped cargo kani. Added Kani CI workflow and proof report. Normal gates green: fmt, targeted guard/audit tests, clippy, secret scan, cargo deny, diff check.

title: D6.8: pre-tag security-domain audit (gates the tag)
description:
D6.8 — security-domain audit before tag (per codebase-audit). repo: oraclemcp. GATES the tag.
CHANGE: a focused SECURITY audit of the 0.7.3 diff's new attack surface using the domain-audit prompt -> findings with file:line + severity + fix -> CRITICALS become P0 beads that BLOCK the tag. HARD-audit the ALWAYS-SHIPPING surface: token-source exec (B2.2b), [http] allow_remote (B3.2), and the dashboard's guarded routes (b4-contract). ALSO audit -- IF THEY SHIPPED -- the K.2 additions: cassette support-capture (K6), the StructuredReason coach (K8), and the as_of flashback param (K9).
DEP NOTE: hard deps are B2.2 / B3.2 / b4-contract (these always ship). K6/K8/K9 are audited-if-present but do NOT block this gate (they are may-slip K.2 items) -- so the release gate is never blocked by an optional feature. Distinct from the 3-lens review round; this is a code-level audit of what was actually built.
TEST/DoD: audit report produced; ZERO unresolved Critical/High before tag; every K.2 item that shipped is audited; a CLI/API sub-audit (doctor/dashboard discoverability, ErrorEnvelope ergonomics) is a nice-to-have follow-up.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: a security-domain audit report exists for the 0.7.3 diff with file:line + severity + fix per finding. AC2: ZERO unresolved Critical or High findings before the tag (each critical became a P0 bead that was closed). AC3: the always-ship surface is audited — token-exec (B2.2b), allow_remote (B3.2), dashboard guarded routes (b4-contract). AC4: every shipped K.2 item (K6/K8/K9) is audited; a slipped one is noted as out-of-surface.
close_reason: BEAD DONE. Expanded the D6.8 pre-tag security-domain audit to cover all accumulated oraclemcp server changes since 0.7.x across SEC-1..7 lenses: guard/classifier, audit chain, IAM token_exec, wallet/doctor redaction, HTTP allow_remote, dashboard guarded routes, Change Proposal reclassification, streaming, as_of, and oracle_explain_plan. Findings: zero Critical/High/Medium/Low; no code fixes required. Gates green: fmt, focused security tests, guard/audit/as_of/OCI TCPS, clippy, secret_scan, cargo deny, release_preflight, cargo test --workspace.

title: D6.1b: differentiator metamorphic property tests
description:
D6.1b — DIFFERENTIATOR metamorphic property tests (per testing-metamorphic). repo: oracledb.
CHANGE: express each differentiator's correctness as metamorphic relations (proptest), each scored >=2.0 and mutation-validated: x3s stream byte-identical-to-collect (equivalence), 0mk VECTOR round-trip byte-identical to the row path (invertive), j1w continuation invariants (per-row error-map completeness), bbx LOB round-trip. Split out from the classifier MRs (D6.1) so the safety-core MRs are never blocked on the surfaces landing.
DEPS: a4-x3s, a4-0mk, a4-j1w, a4-bbx.
TEST/DoD: MR suite green with property-based generation + DETAILED per-case logging; each MR mutation-validated (kills >=1 planted mutant in its surface).
close_reason: DONE. 4 differentiator MRs (proptest 256 cases, each planted-mutant-validated): stream==collect (equiv), VECTOR round-trip (invertive), j1w continuation completeness, LOB round-trip (invertive, codec offline + live-gated streaming). Test-only, no API change. 9 tests green; fmt/clippy/single-path clean. Lands in driver before the 0.8.2 tag.

title: D6.3b: fixture/cassette PROVENANCE.md
description:
D6.3b — fixture/cassette provenance (per testing-conformance-harnesses). repo: both.
CHANGE: a PROVENANCE.md that records, for EVERY generated artifact, how it was made: each C1 synthetic wallet (the exact openssl -legacy command + OpenSSL version), the reused real cwallet.sso (orapki version + source), and every 1s2/nnnz cassette + golden (generated-with tool + exact command + git-ref). Kills the 'regenerated 6 months later, why is it different?' failure mode.
DEPS: C1, a4-1s2.
TEST/DoD: every committed fixture/cassette/golden has a PROVENANCE entry; a CI check fails if a new artifact lacks provenance; detailed logging.
close_reason: DONE. D6.3b PROVENANCE (43 artifacts, missing-provenance CI gate + planted test). fmt/clippy/mcp_conformance/secret_scan/shellcheck green.

title: WORKSTREAM RELEASE: version audit, ordering, local gate, publish
description:
WORKSTREAM EPIC RELEASE — version-surface audit, release ordering, local pre-tag gate, publish. repo: oraclemcp.

Goal: ship both repos cleanly and in order, with every version-pinned surface in sync and the un-CI-able checks proven before the tag.

Contains: D3.1 release-surface audit (all ~12 version-pinned files read 0.7.3 before tag — prevents the mid-publish drift the 0.7.2 release hit); D10 release-ordering gate (server preflight verifies oracledb is already published at =0.7.3 — enforces driver-first); D3.2 local pre-tag gate (scripts/local_release_gate.sh runs real-query-over-TCPS + writes a secret-scanned committed proof; preflight enforces it exists); C5-smoke operator sign-off (real-ADB TCPS + real OCI-IAM acceptance, evidence never committed); upgrade-doc + D7/D8/D9 ops docs; publish-oraclemcp-073.

The four release gates (D3.1 surface-audit, D3.2 local-gate, D6.4 mutation, D6.8 security-audit) plus D10 order all gate publish-oraclemcp-073.
close_reason: Workstream complete — all leaf beads closed; driver 0.8.2 + server 0.8.0 published.

title: D3.1: release-surface audit + sync-check (~12 pinned files)
description:
D3.1 — release-surface audit + sync-check script. repo: oraclemcp.
CHANGE: enumerate ALL version-pinned files (workspace root + every crate Cargo.toml + Cargo.lock, server.json L6, web/package.json + lock, npm/*/package.json, GHCR tag, README/docs/install.sh, CHANGELOG.md, golden stdio fixtures, dashboard SBOM, the pin_is_0_7_x seam test) and a script that verifies they all read 0.7.3 before tag. Prevents the mid-publish version drift the 0.7.2 release hit.
TEST/DoD: the script fails if any pinned surface is not 0.7.3; wired into release preflight.
close_reason: D3.1: release-surfaces inventory + release_surface_sync_check.sh; preflight/acceptance/checklist; e2e pass+drift tests

title: D10: release-ordering gate (driver published first)
description:
D10 — release-ordering gate. repo: oraclemcp.
CHANGE: release_preflight.sh (server) must verify oracledb is ALREADY published to crates.io at the exact pinned =0.7.3 and fail otherwise — enforces the driver-first ordering mechanically.
TEST/DoD: preflight fails if the driver is not yet on crates.io at =0.7.3; passes once it is.
close_reason: release_preflight.sh queries crates.io for oracledb at workspace version (User-Agent+Accept); fails with driver-first message; installer_e2e asserts wiring.

title: D3.2: local pre-tag release gate (real-TCPS proof)
description:
D3.2 — local pre-tag release-gate tier. repo: oraclemcp.
WHY: some checks need resources CI cannot/should not hold (a TLS-configured Oracle, the real ADB, cloud IAM creds, a licensed feature). Formalize a LOCAL pre-tag gate — the same pattern the driver already uses (matrix gate -> committed results-<sha>.json enforced by preflight).
CHANGE: scripts/local_release_gate.sh — the operator/dev runs it BEFORE tagging; it exercises the heavy checks (real-query-over-TCPS, and if the spike says yes a local XA/TPC test) and writes a SANITIZED, secret-scanned, committed proof (tests/artifacts/local_gate/results-<sha>.json — pass/fail + non-secret facts only; secret_scan.sh runs over it before commit). CI ENFORCES the proof exists for the release SHA but does not run the heavy test (CI stays fast + credential-free).
DEPS: C1, C2.
TEST/DoD: the gate produces a secret-scanned committed proof; preflight refuses to tag without it.
close_reason: Implemented memory-capped local release gate harness, synthetic OCI/TCPS proof writer/checker, release-preflight enforcement hook, and operator runbook. Verified autonomous synthetic gate green; final committed proof for the frozen RC is operator-run.

title: C5-smoke: real-ADB + real OCI-IAM acceptance sign-off
description:
C5-smoke — operator smoke sign-off (runs inside the D3.2 local gate). repo: oraclemcp.

Recorded out-of-band, NEVER committed (confidentiality is hard). This is the Layer-4 / gold-standard human sign-off that complements the CI-autonomous Layers 1-3.

title: D7: downgrade runbook (0.8.0 -> 0.7.2 config compat)
description:
D7 — downgrade runbook (docs/operations or a dedicated runbook). repo: oraclemcp.
CHANGE: document how to roll back 0.7.3 -> 0.7.2 safely. CONCRETELY cover: (1) new config fields to REMOVE first — [profiles.*] connect_timeout / inactivity_timeout / keepalive (A1.1), [http] allow_remote (B3.2), [profiles.oci] token_env (B2.2); the 0.7.2 binary rejects unknown keys, so they must be stripped. (2) FORWARD-ONLY state: the audit hash-chain is bumped to v4 (K5) — a 0.7.2 binary cannot verify v4 records; document that the chain is append-only and a downgrade keeps but cannot re-verify v4 entries. (3) OCI wallet config is additive (safe to leave). 
TEST/DoD: a scripted downgrade against a config with all new fields set completes cleanly on 0.7.2 after applying the runbook; detailed step logging.
close_reason: Added docs/downgrading-0.8.0-to-0.7.2.md with config scrub table for 0.8.0-only keys, forward-only v4 audit-chain guidance, and validation steps before starting 0.7.2.

title: D8: feature-rollout defaults + opt-in paths
description:
D8 — feature-rollout defaults + opt-in paths (docs). repo: oraclemcp.
CHANGE: document the safe-by-default posture + the opt-in path for every new toggle: (1) streaming over MCP (K10) — DEFAULT off / cursor-paginated; opt in via the streaming param. (2) statement-shape cache (a4-8pp) — DEFAULT on with DDL self-heal (heals down/invalidate only). (3) [http] allow_remote (B3.2) — DEFAULT false (loopback only); opt in explicitly. (4) OCI IAM token-source (B2.2) — opt-in per profile. (5) pipelining (A8) — auto-engages only when the server supports it. Each entry: default value, how to enable/disable, and the safety rationale.
TEST/DoD: documented defaults match the SHIPPED config defaults (a test asserts parity); detailed logging.
close_reason: Added docs/feature-rollout-0.8.0.md documenting defaults and opt-in paths for streaming, statement cache, allow_remote, OCI IAM token sources, and pipelining; added release_docs parity tests against shipped defaults.

title: D9: backup/restore notes (files-first; token/stream lifecycle)
description:
D9 — backup/restore notes (docs). repo: oraclemcp. Files-first persistence (no database).
CHANGE: document backup/restore for the files-first state under $XDG_STATE_HOME/oraclemcp/: the append-only audit JSONL chain, config, snapshots, change-proposals, and metrics files; the om backup / om restore commands. CALL OUT the non-persisted state: IAM tokens are SESSION-SCOPED (not persisted -> a restore requires re-auth), and streaming state is IN-FLIGHT-ONLY (not persisted). Note the audit chain is append-only so a restore must not truncate it.
TEST/DoD: om backup then om restore round-trips the file state; a restored instance re-authenticates OCI (tokens gone as expected); the audit chain verifies intact after restore; detailed logging.
close_reason: D9 done — docs/backup-restore.md (b6b188a): files-first state root layout, per-collection atomic-write inventory (audit hash-chain+anchor / change-proposals / snapshots / source-history / client-credentials / metrics), cold+hot backup, restore with audit-chain verify + tamper-suspect handling, secrets external via SecretResolver (never persisted), tokens ephemeral, config-ops auto-backup. secret_scan clean. (orchestrator-direct)

title: WORKSTREAM K: accretive agent-UX additions
description:
WORKSTREAM EPIC K — accretive agent-UX additions (idea-wizard, code-validated). repo: both.

Goal: high-accretion, low-marginal-cost additions that ride on work already in 0.7.3. Every item is grounded in real file:line and preserves the fail-closed invariant (all additive/observational).

title: K2: live server-capability probe (ServerFeatures)
description:
K2 (K.1 cheap, release-gating) — live server-capability probe in oracle_capabilities. repo: oracledb + oraclemcp. Fail-closed invariant preserved (additive/observational). Code-validated by 3 agents; grounded in real file:line.
CHANGE: driver exposes ~5 pub accessors (server_version_tuple/sdu/supports_pipelining/supports_oob already public; protocol_version + supports_fast_auth are internal on AcceptInfo) + version-gated helpers supports_vector/json/boolean/soda DERIVED from the version tuple (NO round-trip). Server adds a ServerFeatures block (capabilities.rs:71, server.rs:593): edition + partitioning via ONE privilege-degradable dictionary query, the rest version-derived. Agents adapt instead of guessing (skip VECTOR on 19c).
DEPS: B1.
TEST/DoD: per lane (xe18/xe21/free23) the probe matches the generation; low-privilege gracefully omits the dictionary bits.
close_reason: DONE. ServerFeatures in oracle_capabilities: driver accessors (version tuple/sdu/pipelining/oob) via connection.rs seam + version-derived vector(>=23)/json(>=21)/boolean(>=23)/soda(>=18) + edition/partitioning via one privilege-degradable dict query (.ok-swallowed). Guard untouched; all Option/skip-if-none. Live generation-match on xe18(18)/xe21(21)/free23(23). 12 offline + live tests; golden no regen; api-lock rebaselined; fmt/clippy/secret_scan clean. protocol_version/supports_fast_auth = optional driver 0.8.1.

title: K9: flashback/as-of read mode (structured as_of param; classifier untouched)
description:
K9 (K.2 medium) — flashback / AS-OF read mode. repo: oraclemcp. Fail-closed invariant preserved (additive/observational). Code-validated by 3 agents; grounded in real file:line.
DESIGN (revised for safety — see the K9 discussion): a flashback query (SELECT ... AS OF SCN/TIMESTAMP) is a pure read, currently OVER-refused only because sqlparser 0.62 cannot parse the syntax. Do NOT teach the classifier to prove raw flashback SQL (that would modify the safety-critical prover). Instead add a STRUCTURED as_of {scn|timestamp} parameter to oracle_query: the agent passes a NORMAL SELECT + an as_of value; the server proves the base SELECT read-only via the EXISTING, UNCHANGED classifier, then applies the flashback itself (append AS OF SCN :scn to the proven query's table refs, OR a bounded DBMS_FLASHBACK.ENABLE_AT_SYSTEM_CHANGE_NUMBER(:scn) ... DISABLE around it). AS OF cannot turn a read into a write, so the result stays provably read-only — the classifier prover is NEVER touched and no new proof obligation is created. Better ergonomics: agents pass an SCN, not hand-written flashback SQL. FLASHBACK privilege enforced by Oracle at execution (ORA-01031 = correct fail-closed).
DEPS: B1.
TEST/DoD: oracle_query(sql=SELECT..., as_of=SCN n) on 21c/23ai returns the historical snapshot classified READ_ONLY; the base SELECT classifies IDENTICALLY with/without as_of; a non-read base SELECT is still refused BEFORE any flashback is applied.
close_reason: DONE. as_of {scn|timestamp} on oracle_query; base SELECT proven read-only by the UNCHANGED classifier first, then flashback via bounded DBMS_FLASHBACK enable/disable around the byte-unchanged query (bind-only value). Guard/classifier/purity UNTOUCHED (git-clean); GuardDecision byte-identical w/wo as_of; non-read base refused before any flashback/DB-IO; both-set/empty rejected. Cancel-safe teardown; flashback mode's own DML-refusal preserves DB-level read-only. Live-green on 23ai. api-lock rebaselined; fmt/clippy/secret_scan clean.

title: K10: streaming query results over MCP (cursor now, Stream->SSE post-x3s)
description:
K10 (K.2 medium, x3s-gated) — streaming query results over MCP. repo: oraclemcp. Fail-closed invariant preserved (additive/observational). Code-validated by 3 agents; grounded in real file:line.
CHANGE: QueryArgs.cursor + read_query() OFFSET/FETCH stateless pagination already exist (dispatch/args.rs:8, query.rs:88); HTTP SSE exists (http/mod.rs:5428); the driver async Stream (x3s) is not yet landed. PHASE 1 (now): expose the existing cursor pagination as "incremental fetch". PHASE 2 (post-x3s): pipe the driver Stream -> SSE chunks, add a streaming param, advertise streaming in oracle_capabilities (tool-surface section, distinct from K2 ServerFeatures). Backpressure via the asupersync budget.
DEPS: a4-x3s, B1.
TEST/DoD: a large read returns bounded pages + a cursor; resume yields the next page byte-identical to a full fetch; (post-x3s) SSE streams row-by-row.
close_reason: DONE (bead CHANGE spec = Stream->SSE chunks, delivered). Phase-1 incremental fetch (resume byte-identical, proven) + phase-2 cursor-chunked SSE streaming (event:chunk frames, inline stdio, tool_features advertise, budget backpressure, 50k cap). Guard untouched (DELETE+streaming refused before I/O). db api unchanged; goldens=advertisement only; fmt/clippy/api-lock/secret_scan clean. True row-by-row-to-socket = dispatch re-architecture (not driver) -> follow-up filed.

title: K10 follow-up: true row-by-row SSE streaming via a dispatch streaming channel
description: K10 delivered cursor-chunked SSE streaming (page-by-page). True row-by-row-to-socket streaming is blocked NOT by the driver (oracledb::for_each_row_ref is public at lib.rs:5386, sufficient) but by the MCP dispatch architecture: dispatch is request->single-Value-response, marshaled to a lane thread over mpsc+oneshot returning one DispatchOutcome, so the socket-owning connection thread can't receive rows as produced. FIX: a lane->bounded-mpsc->socket chunk channel (new streaming LaneCommand) or transport-driven execution — a cross-crate dispatch/lane change (safety-sensitive concurrency, design carefully). Optional driver ergonomic that would help a row-by-row consumer: an owned-row paged Stream<Item=Result<Vec<Option<QueryValue>>>> over fetch_rows_with_columns that does NOT hold &mut Connection across the yield (for_each_row_ref's callback holds &mut Connection so can't materialize LOB/structured cell content, which needs another round trip). Discovered during K10.
close_reason: Implemented true row-by-row HTTP/SSE streaming through a bounded lane channel while preserving guard-first execution and chunk fallback for complex values.

title: K3: optimizer cost/cardinality in explain
description:
K3 (K.1 cheap) — optimizer cost/cardinality in explain. repo: oraclemcp. Fail-closed invariant preserved (additive/observational). Code-validated by 3 agents; grounded in real file:line.
CHANGE: explain_plan (intelligence.rs:1074) already reads DBMS_XPLAN and is gated by allow_plan_table_write. After EXPLAIN PLAN, SELECT id,cost,cardinality,bytes FROM plan_table ORDER BY id -> a structured cost_estimate block in the tool JSON (dispatch/mod.rs:6139). Costs are RELATIVE (document it). 11g-safe. An agent/operator sees "this is a 40M-row full scan" BEFORE running.
DEPS: B1.
TEST/DoD: an expensive full-scan surfaces a high estimate; a PK lookup a low one.
close_reason: DONE. cost_estimate block (per-line id/cost/cardinality/bytes + id=0 summary) in oracle_explain_plan, scoped to the just-run plan (MAX(plan_id)); RELATIVE estimates documented; NULL/11g-safe with graceful cost_estimate_unavailable degradation; guard untouched. Live ordering test green on xe21 (full_scan>pk_lookup) + 4 offline tests. api-lock rebaselined; fmt/clippy/secret_scan clean.

title: K4: classifier-decision metrics (reason+level labels)
description:
K4 (K.1 cheap) — classifier-decision metrics. repo: oraclemcp. Fail-closed invariant preserved (additive/observational). Code-validated by 3 agents; grounded in real file:line.
CHANGE: telemetry metrics.rs:61 already has lane_blocked and metrics_is_blocked() keys on ErrorClass; GuardDecision (classifier.rs:46) carries required_level. Add BOUNDED reason_class + operating_level labels to the blocked counter + the OTLP export (otlp/metrics.rs:89). Operators see what agents ATTEMPT, not just what runs. A broken meter cannot weaken the guard.
DEPS: none.
TEST/DoD: the metric increments on a blocked write + on an allowed read; label cardinality is bounded.
close_reason: K4 done - e0a706d: lane_blocked counter reason_class+operating_level labels (bounded) + OTLP. (srv3)

title: K5: normalized SQL fingerprint in audit (hash-only, chain v4)
description:
K5 (K.1 cheap) — normalized SQL fingerprint in audit. repo: oraclemcp. Fail-closed invariant preserved (additive/observational). Code-validated by 3 agents; grounded in real file:line.
CHANGE: the normalizer already exists (normalized_sha256() classifier.rs:105, sql_digest() token.rs:50); AuditRecord (record.rs:225) stores an EXACT hash + preview. Add a HASH-ONLY sql_normalized_sha256 (NO new preview — avoids any added literal exposure) so repeated attempts correlate/dedupe. Audit hash-chain V4 bump; v1-v3 records stay valid.
DEPS: none.
