close_reason: K5 done - 0a84c3e: hash-only sql_normalized_sha256 on AuditRecord, chain v3->v4 (v1-v3 verify byte-for-byte). (srv3)

title: K11: DDL blast-radius dependents preview
description:
K11 (K.1 cheap) — DDL blast-radius / dependents preview. repo: oraclemcp. Fail-closed invariant preserved (additive/observational). Code-validated by 3 agents; grounded in real file:line.
CHANGE: catalog_extract.rs:81 already has the Dependencies rowset; the create_or_replace_inner / patch_source previews (dispatch/mod.rs:4460 / :4134) already carry the detected object. Add probe_dependents() -> a read-only ALL_DEPENDENCIES query (NO DDL gate) -> a dependents{count,objects,at_risk_of_invalid} block in the preview. Direct dependents first; transitive closure optional (documented limit, as is dynamic-SQL). Privilege-degradable.
DEPS: B1.
TEST/DoD: replacing a package body previews the dependent views/procs that will go INVALID.
close_reason: DONE. dependents block (count/objects/at_risk_of_invalid/note) in create_or_replace + patch_source previews; read-only fully-bound ALL_DEPENDENCIES probe, one-hop direct dependents, at_risk = invalidatable types; privilege-degradable (dependents_unavailable, never fails preview); guard/classifier/DDL-gate untouched (dictionary READ, preview-only). Live test green on xe21 (view+proc flagged) + 8 offline. api-lock rebaselined; fmt/clippy/secret_scan clean.

title: K1: wallet/cert-expiry warnings in doctor (der CertMetadata)
description:
K1 (K.2 medium) — wallet/cert-expiry warnings in doctor. repo: oracledb + oraclemcp. Fail-closed invariant preserved (additive/observational). Code-validated by 3 agents; grounded in real file:line.
SURPRISE (validated): the driver WalletContents returns DER bytes only — NO x509 validity dates. CHANGE: driver adds CertMetadata{not_before,not_after} parsed via the ALREADY-PRESENT der crate (no new dep), exposed on Connection; server doctor (doctor.rs:1109) adds expires_at / days_until_expiry + a warn threshold. Offline (no DB). Silent-skip non-cert files. Prevents silent TLS outages.
DEPS: B2.1.
TEST/DoD: a C1 synthetic wallet minted with short validity -> warning; a healthy one -> none.
close_reason: DONE. Doctor cert-expiry via the new driver certificate_metadata() (connection.rs seam -> server type); expires_at/days_until_expiry + WARN <30d/expired; synthetic expired cert-only fixture; offline; secret-non-leak asserted.

title: K6: secret-free cassette support-capture (mandatory scrub gate)
description:
K6 (K.2 medium) — secret-free .tns-cassette support-capture. repo: oracledb. Fail-closed invariant preserved (additive/observational). Code-validated by 3 agents; grounded in real file:line.
SURPRISE (validated): the general CassetteRecorder (transport.rs:307) records the AUTH PHASE (verifier / session-key / tokens) with NO sanitization — a naive ORACLEDB_CAPTURE would LEAK secrets. RESOLUTION (mandatory): env-gate ORACLEDB_CAPTURE=<path> at connect; SCRUB auth-phase frames + run scan_for_secret_fields() (port SECRET_FIELD_NAMES from version_cassettes.rs:253) as a REFUSE-ON-SECRET gate before writing; ship a self-test that PLANTS a secret and asserts the write is REFUSED. Turns "it fails on my DB" into a one-file offline shareable repro. Ties to C4/C6.
DEPS: C4.
TEST/DoD: capture a query failure -> the C4 secret-scan passes over the artifact; offline replay reproduces it; the plant-a-secret test refuses to write.
close_reason: VERIFIED DONE — K6 secret-free ORACLEDB_CAPTURE shipped in oracledb 0.8.1 (commit 8991230). Code verified present in the driver: ORACLEDB_CAPTURE env-gate + scrub_and_write + CassetteCaptureError::SecretLeak refuse-on-secret gate + scan_for_secret_fields reuse (crates/oracledb/src/transport.rs + lib.rs). Plant-a-secret test refuses the write. Bead was stuck in_progress; work landed driver-side.

title: K7: bind-literal rewrite hint (suggest_parameterized_form)
description:
K7 (K.2 medium) — bind-literal rewrite hint. repo: oraclemcp. Fail-closed invariant preserved (additive/observational). Code-validated by 3 agents; grounded in real file:line.
CHANGE: the sqlparser tokenizer is already used (classifier.rs:172 / :754) but there is no literal extraction yet. Add suggest_parameterized_form() — tokenize, find Number/String/Hex/National literals at SAFE positions (NOT FROM / function-name / DDL-column), suggest :paramN, cap ~10; surface in ErrorEnvelope.next_steps. Hint-only — no guard risk. On-brand safety + cursor-sharing perf. The real work is the safe-position filtering (VALUES lists, OR-chains).
DEPS: none.
TEST/DoD: WHERE id = 42 -> suggests :id; a literal inside a quoted identifier is untouched.
close_reason: K7 done - 50cba04: suggest_parameterized_form (guard/rewrite.rs) bind-safe positions; surfaced in read-only gate next_step. (srv3)

title: K8: why-blocked + minimal-safe-rewrite coach (StructuredReason)
description:
K8 (K.2 medium, most agent-accretive) — "why blocked + minimal safe rewrite" coach. repo: oraclemcp. Fail-closed invariant preserved (additive/observational). Code-validated by 3 agents; grounded in real file:line.
CHANGE: GuardDecision (classifier.rs:46) has danger/required_level/reason(string); ErrorEnvelope (oraclemcp-error/lib.rs:111) has suggested_tool/next_steps but no structured reason. Add a reason_category enum + offending_construct to GuardDecision; add StructuredReason to the #[non_exhaustive] ErrorEnvelope (ADDITIVE & non-breaking -> patch-compatible with the 0.7.3 / 0.x bump; requires an api-lock / public-api baseline rebaseline); wire dispatch gate-error/preview (mod.rs:2047 / :2123) to populate it + compute the MINIMAL rewrite (bind the literal, add a WHERE, drop the stacked statement, use the right level). Emit structured + legacy string for a grace period. CAVEAT: some refusals (unbalanced BEGIN/END) have no minimal rewrite -> fall back to suggested_tool. Turns the fail-closed wall into a teacher.
DEPS: none.
TEST/DoD: each refusal class returns a valid, minimal, still-safe alternative (or a suggested_tool when none exists).
close_reason: K8 done - 50cba04: GuardDecision reason_category+offending_construct; ErrorEnvelope additive structured_reason; minimal-rewrite compute. NEEDS api-lock rebaseline. (srv3)

title: WORKSTREAM CONSUMER: plsql-mcp wiring (plsql-intelligence)
description:
WORKSTREAM EPIC CONSUMER — downstream plsql-mcp wiring. repo: plsql-intelligence.

Goal: after the oraclemcp 0.7.3 publish, wire the plsql-mcp consumer (which lives at plsql-intelligence/crates/plsql-mcp) onto the shared high-level routine API and bump its pins in lockstep.

Reality (investigated, F.9): the high-level call_routine (+OracleRoutineArg/ExecuteOutcome) ALREADY exists in oraclemcp-db (connection.rs:368/3798, exported lib.rs:92). Today plsql-mcp hand-rolls PL/SQL blocks over conn.execute/query_rows with positional binds; it can adopt call_routine directly. Internal-only change (its MCP tool surface is unchanged) -> plsql-intelligence bumps 0.7.0 -> 0.7.1. Sequenced AFTER the oraclemcp 0.7.3 publish.
close_reason: Workstream CONSUMER done: G-CONSUMER closed (plsql-mcp retired); no consumer to wire.

title: G-CONSUMER: wire plsql-mcp onto oraclemcp-db::call_routine
description:
G-CONSUMER — wire the plsql-mcp consumer onto the shared routine API. repo: plsql-intelligence. Cross-repo; sequenced AFTER the oraclemcp 0.7.3 publish.
NAMING: G-CONSUMER is a task label; the G- is just a slug prefix, NOT related to Appendix G (the design spec).
REALITY (investigated, F.9): plsql-mcp lives at plsql-intelligence/crates/plsql-mcp and reaches Oracle via the shared oraclemcp-db seam. It currently HAND-ROLLS PL/SQL blocks over conn.execute/query_rows with positional binds (no typed OUT/IN-OUT/return). The high-level call_routine (+OracleRoutineArg/ExecuteOutcome) ALREADY exists in oraclemcp-db (connection.rs:368/3798, exported lib.rs:92) — so the consumer can adopt it directly.
CHANGE: swap plsql-mcp's hand-rolled routine calls onto oraclemcp-db::call_routine; bump its oraclemcp-db/oracledb pins to =0.7.3; add its own autonomous test (loopback + a live PL/SQL routine call with return/OUT/IN-OUT binds). Internal-only change (its MCP tool surface is unchanged) -> plsql-intelligence bumps 0.7.0 -> 0.7.1. Optionally adopt the driver-native RoutineCall API (a4-plsql-routine) instead of the oraclemcp-db wrapper.
DEPS: publish-oraclemcp-073. Lands in the plsql-intelligence tracker (its own .beads), NOT here — this bead is the coordination record.
TEST/DoD: plsql-mcp uses call_routine; pins at =0.7.3; loopback + live routine tests green; MCP tool surface unchanged.
notes: 2026-07-08 Codex verification: blocked by stale cross-repo scope. The requested target plsql-intelligence/crates/plsql-mcp is not an active crate: it has no tracked files (git ls-files crates/plsql-mcp is empty), no Cargo.toml, and is not listed in plsql-intelligence Cargo.toml workspace members. plsql-intelligence plan.md active architecture (§13A) says this repo no longer ships an MCP server and must not add MCP serving, live-DB feature graph, stdio server, or Oracle connection runtime; live Oracle/MCP behavior belongs in oraclemcp consuming offline engine crates. Reintroducing oraclemcp-db/call_routine into plsql-intelligence would violate the current repo contract. No code change attempted; continue with downstream docs/audit beads.
close_reason: WON'T-DO — plsql-mcp is RETIRED (operator-confirmed 2026-07-09; tombstoned at plsql-intelligence/target/plsql-mcp-tombstone-0.6.4). G-CONSUMER was to wire plsql-mcp onto oraclemcp-db::call_routine, but the consumer no longer exists, so there is nothing to wire. Cross-repo/post-publish bead; does not affect the shipped oraclemcp 0.8.0. If a successor PL/SQL MCP consumer is built later, that is new work in the plsql-intelligence repo.

title: Make npm publish auth mode explicit
close_reason: Added explicit auth_mode selection to publish-npm.yml: auto/token/oidc, including OIDC mode that ignores a present NPM_TOKEN for npm trusted publishing and targeted E403 guidance for token permission failures. Pinned by installer_e2e workflow-contract assertions. Verified: npm --prefix npm/oraclemcp test; cargo test -p oraclemcp --test installer_e2e; cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check (existing unmatched allowance/advisory warnings only, final ok).

description:
PART B follow-up (multi-pass 2026-07-04). Live lanes (xe18@1518/xe21@1520/free23@1522) were NOT reachable in the hardening session's environment (ports closed, ORACLEMCP_LIVE_XE unset), so new live e2e could be written to convention but not executed.

DONE this session (written, py_compile-clean, PENDING-LIVE run on operator lanes): two READ_ONLY-phase ladder steps in oracle_ladder_session.py asserting governed-refusal ERROR CLASSES (value assertions, not exit codes), running identically on all 3 lanes via the existing matrix loop: read_only_forbidden_statement_refused (BEGIN EXECUTE IMMEDIATE... => FORBIDDEN_STATEMENT) and read_only_smuggled_dml_not_served_as_read (SELECT * FROM (UPDATE t...) => OPERATING_LEVEL_TOO_LOW/FORBIDDEN, regressing the oracle-v4d9 classifier fix through the live server). Helper query_governed_refusal added.

New oracle_ladder_session.py steps: explain_plan_refused_at_read_only (POLICY_DENIED), explain_plan_refused_on_standby (POLICY_DENIED), explain_plan_allow_requires_read_write_at_read_only (OPERATING_LEVEL_TOO_LOW), explain_plan_read_write_allowed (plan rows + explicit PLAN_TABLE consent), custom_read_only_tool_callable (served + returns 6*7=42), sample_rows_values_and_cap (row values + max_rows cap), read_clob_value_and_cap (100-char CLOB value + max_chars decode cap/truncated), dbms_output_capture_caps (line cap=10 truncated), switch_profile_reconnect_and_posture (reconnect + ceiling-blocked elevation on RO sibling, restored on primary), dml_commit_clob_row. Wrapper (oracle_version_matrix.sh): adds a READ_ONLY-ceiling sibling profile, a tools.d read-only custom tool, and a custom_tool_write_refused check (write custom tool -> serve exits 2 ORACLEMCP_CUSTOM_TOOLS_INVALID, fail-closed at load). LIVE RESULT: bash scripts/e2e/oracle_version_matrix.sh --log -> 'lanes: xe18=pass xe21=pass free23=pass', 0 assert failures; all 10 new surface steps 'pass' on each lane; audit chain verified (78 records, anchor match) + restart-resume + truncation-detect intact. COVERAGE.md updated (rsya row PASS; v4d9 row flipped PENDING-LIVE->PASS). All static gates green.

title: MCP protocol-revision hygiene: capability surface not degraded to negotiated revision; version not stored per-session (no security impact)
description:
MCP protocol-revision hygiene (spec-fidelity, NOT a security bypass). From the 2026-07 multipass bug hunt protocol-negotiation audit (self-verified + peer-audited).

SECURITY CONCLUSION (verified, no action needed): version negotiation is spec-compliant (crates/oraclemcp-core/src/server.rs:525-542 echoes a supported requested version, else the server's latest; tests initialize_echoes_a_supported_client_protocol_version + initialize_frame_negotiates_older_client_version). There is NO fail-closed bypass via downgrade: the negotiated version is read in exactly one place (the echoed field), refusals always ride content[0].text + isError:true which are valid in every revision including 2024-11-05, and NO guard/classifier/consent code branches on the protocol version (guard enforcement is entirely server-side and version-independent). Pinning an old version cannot bypass any check.

RESIDUAL SPEC-FIDELITY GAPS (all benign today, additive-only):
(i) The served capability surface does not degrade to the negotiated revision: served_capabilities_json (server.rs:533) ignores the negotiated version, so a 2024-11-05 client is advertised structuredContent / completions / title / newer fields that post-date its revision. Additive; old clients ignore unknown fields.
(ii) The negotiated version is not stored per session: a repeated initialize is accepted and notifications/initialized is a no-op; a client could re-initialize mid-session to a different version. Nothing enforces off the stored version, so benign but non-conformant to the strict lifecycle.
(iii) The HTTP MCP-Protocol-Version header is only enforced when present (http/mod.rs:2050-2067); 2025-06-18 requires it on all post-init requests.
(iv) Tests do not assert per-revision capability degradation.

Audit file refs: server.rs:525-542, 875-895, 1654-1682, 1924-1941; http/mod.rs:2050-2067; capabilities.rs:16-23.

SUGGESTED (if pursued, low priority): thread the negotiated version into served_capabilities_json and gate version-specific fields; store the negotiated version per session and reject/annotate a re-initialize; add per-revision capability tests. Do NOT change the refusal encoding (content[0].text + isError) - that cross-revision compatibility is exactly what keeps downgrade safe.
close_reason: MCP revision hygiene landed: negotiated protocolVersion stored per session; second initialize on an initialized session rejected with a structured error; versioned advertisements gated on the negotiated revision WHILE refusal-carrying fields (isError+text) stay always-present (audited no-downgrade-bypass property preserved); HTTP MCP-Protocol-Version header required when the session negotiated >= 2025-06-18, lenient for older. Per-revision conformance tests added; full workspace tests + all lints green.

title: connect UX: wrong password on 18c surfaces as misleading 'not an Oracle listener / unexpected TNS packet type 12 (MARKER)' CONNECTION_FAILED
description:
CONFIRMED live on lab lane xe18 (Oracle 18c XE, plain TCP throwaway container) during the 2026-07 multipass bug hunt. Leave-as-bead: the mapping improvement is real but the fix may be partly upstream (driver) and needs a design decision, so filing with evidence rather than a hasty guess.

REPRO (deterministic, 2/2 runs): same profile (connect_string localhost:1518/XEPDB1, user testuser), only the password varies.
- CORRECT password: doctor --online Connectivity = pass ("connected + authenticated").
- WRONG password: Connectivity = fail, failure_class = CONNECTION_FAILED, detail =
  "connect failed: connect handshake failed [unexpected-tns-packet]: the server replied with unexpected low-level TNS packet type 12 during the connect handshake (network layer, before authentication) - the endpoint is not an Oracle listener, or speaks a protocol generation this driver does not recognise: unexpected TNS packet type 12 (MARKER)"
  fix = "verify the host:port points at an Oracle listener (not another service)...".

WHY IT MATTERS: a wrong password is the single most common connect error. Here it is mislabeled as a network/listener/protocol problem, and the actionable `fix` text misdirects the operator to check host/port/listener rather than credentials. The endpoint IS a valid Oracle listener; only the password is wrong.

LIKELY ROOT CAUSE: with the oracledb 0.6.0 fast-auth connect flow, credentials are sent early in the combined handshake; an 18c server rejecting them replies with a MARKER (TNS packet type 12) that the driver surfaces as a low-level "unexpected TNS packet" rather than a clean ORA-01017. oraclemcp's ConnectFailureKind mapping (crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs) then classes it CONNECTION_FAILED [unexpected-tns-packet]. So the wrong-password signal is lost before oraclemcp sees it.

SUGGESTED DIRECTIONS (needs review; do not weaken anything):
1. Confirm with the oracledb maintainers whether an auth rejection can be distinguished from a genuine non-listener endpoint at the driver layer (ideally the driver maps the credential-reject MARKER to an auth error). If yes, adopt and map to an AUTH_FAILED class with a "check username/password/credential_ref" fix.
2. If the driver cannot distinguish, soften oraclemcp's `fix` text for the [unexpected-tns-packet] case to ALSO mention credentials ("...or the username/password is wrong") so the message is not actively misleading, and keep the ORACLEDB_TRACE_CONNECT hint.
3. Add a matrix/negative e2e case that asserts the wrong-password envelope is actionable once the mapping is chosen.

EVIDENCE: /tmp scratch only (env + throwaway profile), binary /tmp/cargo-target/debug/oraclemcp --json doctor --online. No customer identifiers. Related but distinct from bhw6.2 (which covered connect-error envelopes generally); this is the specific wrong-credential-on-pre-23ai mislabel.
close_reason: Fixed by adopting oracledb 0.7.1 (classic connect-phase reads run the marker/reset dance upstream). Live-verified on XE 18: doctor --online with a wrong password now surfaces ORA-01017 in the connectivity failure; the misleading 'unexpected TNS packet type 12 (MARKER)' label is gone.

title: Epic: security gates, performance evidence, and release artifacts
description: Tracks the hardening and release side of the migration: forbidden dependency gates, performance/footprint evidence, synchronized crates.io/GitHub/GHCR/MCP registry artifacts, and operator-facing docs. It exists to prevent a successful compile from becoming an unsafe or misleading release.
design: This epic owns the release and safety ratchets that prevent a partially successful migration from becoming a misleading public release. The user benefit is trust: docs, Docker, crates.io, GitHub release, GHCR, and MCP registry must all describe the same architecture and run the same quality gates before publishing.
acceptance_criteria: Forbidden production dependencies are banned in CI; benchmarks back public claims; release workflow gates fmt, clippy, tests, deny, dependency lint, package, Docker smoke, and MCP registry smoke; semver and rollback/yank guidance are documented before tagging.
notes: Child scope: W5 docs/doctor, W11 tests, W12 dependency gate, W13 evidence, W14 release artifacts. Closure requires the tag-driven process to synchronize crates.io/GitHub/GHCR/MCP registry, and release notes must explain breaking changes, semver decision, yanking/recovery, and the absence of Instant Client/thick mode.
close_reason: All release/security children are closed: docs/doctor, deterministic tests, forbidden dependency gate, performance evidence, and W14 release artifacts. CI hard-gates fmt, clippy, tests, deny, boundary lint, release preflight, docs, sensitive-data lint, thin build, and fuzz compile; release workflow synchronizes crates.io, GitHub release, GHCR, and MCP registry with Docker/MCP registry smoke; semver and yank/recovery guidance are documented for 0.3.0.

title: Flaky test: shutdown wait_does_not_lose_wakeup_under_signal_race times out under CI load
description: Observed once on CI (feature powerset job, dashboard-bundle+default combo on oraclemcp-core): the test panicked with 'asupersync test future did not complete within 5s' at crates/oraclemcp-core/src/shutdown.rs:135, then passed on rerun of the same commit (28691901965) - a load-induced flake, not a regression (the failing commit only bumped the oracledb driver pins; shutdown.rs untouched). Options: raise the 5s harness budget for this test, make the signal-race deterministic (injected waker instead of wall-clock), or mark it serial if it contends with parallel tests for signal delivery. Do NOT weaken the lost-wakeup assertion itself - it pins a real invariant.
close_reason: Fixed deterministically: root cause is that a genuine lost wakeup parks asupersync block_on forever (no runtime deadlock detection), so the 5s recv_timeout was the only detector and the 1000-iteration probabilistic loop raced that wall clock under CI load. Rewrote wait_does_not_lose_wakeup_under_signal_race to drive wait_for_shutdown() by hand with a controlled wake-counting Waker (no runtime, no spawn, no timeout): interleaving 1 parks the waiter then fires begin_shutdown and asserts the wake edge was delivered (count>=1) and the future completes; interleaving 2 signals before first poll and asserts immediate completion. Stronger than the old loop (observes the wake edge directly) and has no wall clock to lose. Also bumped the shared harness backstop 5s to 30s with justification (hang-detector for the remaining spawn-based tests, not a perf bound). Invariant not weakened. Proof: 200/200 release runs of the exact test passed, 0 failures. Gates green: fmt, clippy -D warnings, cargo test --workspace 0 failed.

title: Program: thin-native Asupersync oraclemcp migration
description: Program epic for replacing oraclemcp's thick ODPI-C Oracle backend, Tokio runtime, rmcp SDK transport, Axum, and Hyper with a pure-Rust thin Oracle driver, native Asupersync runtime semantics, and native MCP transports. The safety invariant is non-negotiable: raw SQL must remain fail-closed through oraclemcp-guard before any database execution, async gap, connection acquisition, or mutable execution state. This epic is complete only when the dependency graph below is implemented, tested, release-gated, and documented.
design: User value: oraclemcp becomes a smaller, easier-to-install, pure-Rust Oracle MCP server with no Instant Client dependency, while preserving the fail-closed read-only SQL safety model. Architecture decision: this is a hard migration, not a compat layer. Final production dependencies must not include Tokio, rmcp, axum, hyper, hyper-util, oracle, odpic-sys, r2d2, or asupersync-tokio-compat. The migration is complete only after tests, dependency gates, docs, release artifacts, and registry automation all agree on the thin-native architecture.
acceptance_criteria: All child migration beads are closed; production builds are thin-only and native Asupersync; forbidden dependency gate passes; release artifacts and docs describe thin-native behavior; no credential, PII, or sensitive business data leaks through logs, fixtures, errors, panic paths, or generated artifacts.
notes: Global invariants for all child beads: never introduce unsafe; never weaken oraclemcp-guard; classify raw SQL before await/connection/lease/mutable execution state; keep session leases, rollback semantics, NUMBER/string output, NLS-stable serialization, AWR/ASH license gating, and preview-token single-use behavior; no secrets, PII, or sensitive business data in logs, fixtures, doctor, errors, panic paths, or generated artifacts.
close_reason: All child migration beads and epics are closed. Production builds are thin-only on published oracledb and native Asupersync, with no Tokio/rmcp/Axum/Hyper/oracle/ODPI-C/r2d2 production dependencies. Guard-before-I/O, session cleanup, native MCP stdio/HTTP, OAuth scope enforcement, release artifacts, CI dependency gates, performance evidence, Docker smoke, MCP registry smoke, and sensitive-data checks are implemented and verified locally plus CI run 27570414583 on 7f70f58.

title: Epic: thin-only Oracle driver and live DB behavior
description: Tracks the database half of the migration: behavior inventory, published oracledb dependency decision, thin adapter, session leases, type fidelity, auth coverage, doctor diagnostics, and live DB behavior. The goal is a default install with no Instant Client, ODPI-C, r2d2, or C Oracle connectivity requirement while preserving current production-proven database semantics.
design: This epic owns the DB compatibility contract. The user benefit is removing native Oracle client installation and Docker weight without breaking existing production-facing Oracle behavior. Work under this epic must treat the current thick adapter as the behavioral baseline until the W0 inventory proves otherwise. Any unsupported thin feature must fail explicitly rather than falling through to password auth, silently dropping a profile field, or returning a misleading generic connection error.
acceptance_criteria: Behavior inventory, oracledb dependency decision, thin adapter, thin doctor/docs, and DB live tests are complete; unsupported auth modes fail explicitly; session leases, rollback, NUMBER/string fidelity, NLS-stable output, AWR/ASH gating, and credential redaction are preserved.
notes: Child scope: W0 behavior inventory, W3 oracledb dependency decision, W4 thin adapter, W5 doctor/docs. Closure requires live DB evidence where available and explicit skips where not. Pay special attention to proxy auth, ADB wallet/TCPS/SNI, IAM token refresh, wallet passwords, external auth, DRCP/non-homogeneous pools, session identity, edition, login/trusted statements, standby/read-only profile caps, DBMS_OUTPUT, savepoints, and PLAN_TABLE behavior.
close_reason: All DB migration children are closed: behavior inventory, published oracledb dependency decision, thin adapter, thin doctor/docs, and live/skip DB evidence. Default build requires no Instant Client/ODPI-C/r2d2; unsupported auth modes fail explicitly; session leases, rollback, type fidelity, NLS-stable output, AWR/ASH gating, PLAN_TABLE behavior, and credential redaction are covered by tests and docs.

title: W3.5: resolve explain-plan PLAN_TABLE semantics
description: W0 evidence shows oracle_explain_plan gates the inner SQL as read-only and then runs EXPLAIN PLAN FOR, which writes PLAN_TABLE on primary databases while standby mode refuses that path. Resolve the product contract before the thin adapter lands: either keep this as an explicitly privileged/write-like diagnostic path with separate guard semantics, replace it with a non-writing explain/cursor-based implementation where possible, or document and gate it so the fail-closed read-only invariant remains true to users.
design: Implementation shape: decide and implement the explicit oracle_explain_plan contract before W4 replaces the DB adapter. Preserve the global invariant that raw user SQL is classified before any database execution, and ensure the tool name, operating-level requirement, audit text, docs, and tests match the real Oracle behavior. Prefer a non-writing cursor/display route if the thin driver and Oracle privileges support it; otherwise gate PLAN_TABLE writes as a distinct diagnostic capability instead of presenting them as ordinary read-only execution.
acceptance_criteria: A test proves oracle_explain_plan behavior does not contradict the documented read-only invariant; primary database behavior and read-only standby behavior are both specified; PLAN_TABLE writes are either eliminated or explicitly gated/documented as diagnostic writes; guard/adversarial tests stay fail-closed for raw EXPLAIN PLAN statements; W4 thin adapter cannot start until this bead is resolved.
notes: Discovered during W0 inventory from crates/oraclemcp-db/src/intelligence.rs and guard adversarial corpus. This bead blocks W4 so the thin adapter does not preserve an accidental PLAN_TABLE write path by default. Credential/logging: explain-plan fixtures must use synthetic SQL and must not include customer schema names, bind values, or Oracle connection details.
close_reason: Resolved oracle_explain_plan PLAN_TABLE semantics: inner SQL remains fail-closed read-only, raw EXPLAIN PLAN stays guarded, the tool now refuses by default and on read-only standby, requires READ_WRITE plus allow_plan_table_write=true before DB access, advertises the diagnostic write/destructive surface, and documents the contract. Verified fmt check, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, diff check, focused explain-plan/registry/prompt/golden/guard tests, and secret/diff scans.

title: audit resume: head anchor MAC not verified -> forged-anchor tail-truncation bypass
description:
MULTI-PASS BUG HUNT 2026-07-04, Part A audit fresh-eyes (resume/anchor).

FINDING P1 (tamper-detection bypass): Auditor::resume_from (sink.rs) cross-checked a loaded head anchor by comparing ONLY its plaintext seq + entry_hash, never verifying its keyed MAC (anchor.mac_is_valid). The keyed MAC is the sole binding of the anchor plaintext to the real durable head. A tamperer with file-write access but NO signing key could: delete durable records, truncate the log, and rewrite the .anchor sidecar plaintext DOWN to the truncated tail (matching entry_hash) with a garbage MAC -> resume passed (no truncation trip since anchor.seq==tail.seq; plaintext hash matched). The first durable append after resume re-anchors under the live key, making the truncation PERMANENTLY undetectable locally. verify path (anchor::check_anchor) does verify the MAC (and UnknownKeyId), so a pre-append audit verify would catch it -> held at P1 (agent originally leaned P0).

FIX (sink.rs): new Auditor::verify_anchor_authenticity, called at the top of the anchor cross-check block in resume_from BEFORE trusting plaintext. Mirrors check_anchor: (1) anchor.key_id != active key_id -> ResumeRefused (closes the key_id-swap dodge; genuine cross-run rotation is reconciled by operator via audit verify, consistent with check_anchor UnknownKeyId); (2) !anchor.mac_is_valid(active key) -> ResumeRefused. Existing seq/hash/structural checks unchanged.

TESTS (sink.rs): resume_refuses_a_forged_anchor_masking_a_tail_truncation (full attack: 5-record chain, truncate to 3, forge sidecar seq=3 + correct entry_hash + bad MAC -> refuses with 'MAC does not verify'); resume_refuses_an_anchor_under_an_unknown_key_id (key_id swap -> refuses). Full audit suite green (65).

Credit: found by parallel fresh-eyes sub-audit of oraclemcp-audit.
close_reason: FIXED + tested (sink.rs: verify_anchor_authenticity mirrors check_anchor; resume_refuses_a_forged_anchor_masking_a_tail_truncation + resume_refuses_an_anchor_under_an_unknown_key_id). Audit suite (65) + workspace green.

title: Implement thin DBMS_OUTPUT capture
close_reason: Implemented DBMS_OUTPUT capture through thin output binds; live Oracle 23ai test now asserts capture instead of skipping; full gates passed

title: NPM wrapper publish blocked by missing npm credential or trusted publisher
description: v0.6.0 crates.io, GitHub release, GHCR, and MCP registry published successfully, but npm wrapper publication failed. The original release job failed because npm publish used bare npm/oraclemcp, which npm parsed as github:npm/oraclemcp; fixed in commits 611e6a8 and 8e27cf3. Manual workflow runs 28576593109 and 28576767987 then reached registry auth and failed ENEEDAUTH because secrets.NPM_TOKEN is empty/not configured and npm trusted publishing is not configured for the package. Resolve by adding an npm automation token as the npm environment NPM_TOKEN secret or configuring npm trusted publishing for the GitHub workflow, then rerun .github/workflows/publish-npm.yml with version=0.6.0 checkout_ref=v0.6.0.
notes:
Local cleanup done 2026-07-02: README marks npm/npx as conditional on the npm registry entry existing and points users to npm view before npx. release.yml now validates the npm wrapper/package but no longer makes the core signed release fail on externally blocked npm auth; manual .github/workflows/publish-npm.yml remains the publication path.

2026-07-02 retries after NPM_TOKEN was added: workflow runs 28588925645, 28590671481, and 28594134084 for version=0.6.1 checkout_ref=v0.6.1 had NODE_AUTH_TOKEN present, validated the wrapper, created the provenance statement, then npm publish failed with E403 Forbidden: 'You may not perform that action with these credentials.' npm view oraclemcp@0.6.1 and @0.6.2 still returned E404.

2026-07-02 v0.6.6 retest after the npm environment token update: workflow run 28606816835 for version=0.6.6 checkout_ref=v0.6.6 validated the wrapper, had NODE_AUTH_TOKEN present, created a signed provenance statement, then failed in npm publish with E403 Forbidden on PUT https://registry.npmjs.org/oraclemcp: 'You may not perform that action with these credentials.' npm view oraclemcp@0.6.6 still returns E404. Publish remains blocked until the npm environment secret is a token/account allowed to create/publish the unscoped oraclemcp package, or npm trusted publishing is configured for this GitHub workflow/package; then rerun publish-npm.yml.
close_reason: EXCLUDED by operator 2026-07-02: npm publish channel dropped from 0.6.x (like Scoop). npx oraclemcp is not offered. Release is unaffected: publish-npm.yml is manual-dispatch only and release.yml never published to npm. Distribution stands on crates.io, GitHub release, GHCR, MCP registry, cargo-binstall (+ brew/winget). Reopen only if npm is deliberately revived.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: 2026-07-02 follow-up: repo workflow improvement landed in oraclemcp-rmjm / commit 85cd98d. publish-npm.yml now has auth_mode=auto|token|oidc. Retry with auth_mode=token only after NPM_TOKEN belongs to an npm account/token that can create/publish the unscoped oraclemcp package. Retry with auth_mode=oidc only after npm trusted publishing is configured for owner MuhDur, repository oraclemcp, workflow publish-npm.yml, environment npm, allowed action npm publish; oidc mode intentionally unsets NODE_AUTH_TOKEN/NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG so the existing environment token cannot mask OIDC.

close_reason: Fixed CI release acceptance and release metadata jobs to install web npm dependencies before release_preflight/release_acceptance run npm sbom. Verified with npm ci in web, npm run build in web, release_sbom_check --source, release_preflight.sh, release_acceptance_ci_suite.sh --skip-feature-powerset, and actionlint on ci.yml.

title: audit resume seeds from tail without structural chain verification (interior fork accepted)
description: Auditor::resume_from (crates/oraclemcp-audit/src/sink.rs) parsed the on-disk JSONL and seeded chain state directly from records.last() without walking the chain. parse_jsonl only proves each line is valid JSON. Consequence: a forked interior (deleted, reordered, inserted, or in-place-edited middle record) that leaves the last line parseable was accepted at startup and the new run chained onto the forked prefix. The head anchor does NOT cover this: it attests only the head seq/entry_hash, so an interior deletion whose surviving tail still matches the anchored head passes the anchor cross-check. Only later ad-hoc 'audit verify' would catch it. FIX (landed): added a keyless structural pre-check (hash-link continuity + monotonic seq + hash_is_valid; no MAC so rotated chains are not false-refused) that fails closed with ResumeRefused before seeding. Tests: resume_refuses_a_deleted_interior_even_with_a_matching_anchor, resume_refuses_a_reordered_interior_without_an_anchor, resume_accepts_a_structurally_intact_multi_record_log.
close_reason: Fixed: keyless structural pre-check added to resume_from; 3 tests added; all green

title: classifier: derived/JOIN/UNION/Expr-subquery-smuggled DML cleared to Safe (fail-closed-net hole)
description:
MULTI-PASS BUG HUNT 2026-07-04, Part A adversarial classifier probe.

FINDING (fail-closed-net hole, smuggled-DML class flagged to re-probe): a DML SetExpr (UPDATE/DELETE/INSERT/MERGE) wrapped in a FROM/JOIN derived subquery, a UNION-branch FROM(...), or a WHERE/scalar/EXISTS Expr subquery parsed by sqlparser 0.62 as Statement::Query and cleared to danger=Safe, required_level=READ_ONLY. set_expr_carries_dml inspected only the TOP-LEVEL query body (prior oracle-cte-dml-body fix), missing derived and Expr subqueries.

Reproducers cleared to Safe BEFORE fix: SELECT * FROM (UPDATE t SET x=1); (DELETE FROM t); (INSERT INTO t VALUES(1)); (MERGE...); nested SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM (UPDATE...)); UNION branch; JOIN (UPDATE...); WHERE id IN (UPDATE...); nested-CTE WITH a AS(...) SELECT * FROM (WITH b AS(...) UPDATE t SET v=1).

SEVERITY P0 per operator rubric (write classifying Safe). Honest executability caveat: standard Oracle rejects DML inside FROM/WHERE subqueries so these exact strings are non-executable today; blast radius nil. But the fail-closed contract is violated and relying on Oracle-rejects is the implicit trust fail-closed design forbids.

FIX classifier.rs: (1) set_expr_carries_dml descends into SetExpr::Select FROM/JOIN derived subqueries, nested joins, CTE bodies via new helpers query_carries_dml / table_with_joins_carries_dml / table_factor_carries_dml (all 4 verbs, structural). (2) query_embeds_reserved_dml_verb: canonical-scan net for reserved INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE tokens covering Expr-embedded subqueries; reserved words = zero false positives (FOR UPDATE already Guarded via query.locks). MERGE excluded from token scan (non-reserved) but caught structurally. Query Safe arm gated on both.

RESIDUAL documented: MERGE embedded ONLY in an Expr subquery is not token-scanned; Oracle-invalid + non-executable.

TESTS adversarial_corpus.rs: 10 new CORPUS entries (min Guarded) + derived_subquery_smuggled_dml_is_never_read_only asserting writes>=Guarded and legit nested reads / DML-substring columns / DML-word literals stay Safe. Guard suite green.
close_reason: FIXED + tested (adversarial_corpus.rs: 10 corpus entries + derived_subquery_smuggled_dml_is_never_read_only). set_expr_carries_dml now descends into derived/JOIN/nested/CTE subqueries + query_embeds_reserved_dml_verb net for Expr-embedded reserved DML. Include current behavior for session leases, rollback, DBMS_OUTPUT, NUMBER/date/NLS serialization, AWR/ASH license gating, preview tokens, no native plugins, OAuth scopes, and release/runtime prerequisites. Also audit the target Asupersync web API surface for W9 primitives.
design: Implementation shape: create docs/behavior-inventory.md as the committed source of truth before any downstream code migration. Use repo facts, not memory. Inventory current CLI commands, MCP methods/messages, tool registry, config/profile fields, credential surfaces, safety invariants, dependency holdouts, oracledb API needs, proxy auth, ADB/cloud connectivity, explain-plan behavior, and baseline measurements. Include exact files/tests that prove current behavior. Also audit the pinned Asupersync HTTP/web surface for W9 feasibility.
acceptance_criteria: docs/behavior-inventory.md exists and is committed before downstream beads start; it includes tables for CLI, MCP, tools, credentials, invariants, dependencies, oracledb APIs, proxy auth, ADB/cloud, explain-plan behavior, Asupersync HTTP/web primitives, and baselines; no later bead relies on vague preserve-current-behavior wording without named tests/transcripts/evidence.

Required tables: CLI surface; MCP stdio/HTTP transcripts; DB tools; credential/log/panic/fixture surfaces; session lease invariants; NUMBER/date/NLS serialization; AWR/ASH gating; preview tokens; no native plugins; OAuth scopes; explain-plan/PLAN_TABLE; proxy auth/DRCP; ADB wallet/TCPS/SNI/IAM; Asupersync HTTP API primitives; binary/Docker/startup/query baselines. title: W1: add golden behavior harness
description: Freeze client-visible behavior before replacing transports or runtime. Add golden stdio transcripts for initialize, failed initialize with missing/wrong init token, notifications/initialized, tools/list, oracle_capabilities, unknown tools, successful tool results, and structured error envelopes. Add golden HTTP cases for /mcp initialize, protected-resource metadata, unauthorized WWW-Authenticate behavior, host/origin guards, JSON response mode, and Streamable HTTP session behavior. Normalize only protocol-approved variable fields and keep secrets out of fixtures.
design: Implementation shape: freeze current user-visible MCP behavior before any transport rewrite. Use current rmcp/axum implementation as the baseline, but do not encode accidental nondeterminism. Golden transcripts should cover stdio initialize success/failure, initialized notification, tools/list, oracle_capabilities, unknown tool, structured success, structured error, and HTTP /mcp initialize, protected-resource metadata, unauthorized WWW-Authenticate, Host/Origin guards, JSON response mode, and Streamable HTTP session behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Golden tests pass against the current rmcp/axum implementation before transport rewrite; the same tests pass after native stdio and HTTP land; fixture updates require explicit review as intentional protocol changes; snapshots contain no real secrets, PII, or sensitive business data.
notes: Testing notes: normalize timestamps/session ids/trace ids and only reorder fields where the protocol permits. Fixtures must contain no real secrets, PII, or sensitive business data. Regenerating fixtures is not a valid acceptance shortcut: changed transcript output must be reviewed as an intentional protocol change. This bead blocks W8 and W9 because native transports must prove compatibility against these artifacts.
close_reason: Added reviewed HTTP and stdio golden behavior harnesses, scrubbed fixtures, provenance notes, and shared support. Verified fmt, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, sensitive-data lint, and boundary lint.

title: W10: enforce HTTP OAuth scopes at dispatch
description: Fix the current captured-only ScopeGrant limitation. The HTTP path currently validates OAuth scopes but dispatch does not read them, so narrow tokens may authenticate without lowering authority. Carry a typed ScopeGrant through the native HTTP request/session context and apply monotone-down scope logic before tool dispatch. The effective operating level is the minimum of OAuth scope ceiling, profile max_level, and current session/step-up window. Scopes must never raise profile/session authority; protected profiles remain READ_ONLY even with broader tokens.
design: Implementation shape: fix the known HTTP security bug where ScopeGrant is validated but not enforced by dispatch. Carry ScopeGrant or equivalent through native HTTP request/session context and apply monotone-down logic before tool dispatch. Effective operating level is min(OAuth scope ceiling, profile max_level, current session/step-up window). Scopes can only lower authority; they never raise profile/session authority.
acceptance_criteria: A narrow read token cannot call higher-level tools; a broad token cannot exceed profile max_level; broad token plus protected profile remains READ_ONLY; missing token fails when OAuth is enabled; metadata route remains discoverable; no bearer token content is logged; the old captured-only regression is replaced by enforced least privilege.
notes: Tests: narrow oracle:read token can call read tools but cannot reach write/DDL/admin/challenge-confirmed paths if exposed; broad token cannot exceed profile max_level; broad token plus protected profile remains READ_ONLY; missing token fails when OAuth is enabled; metadata route remains discoverable; diagnostics may show resolved effective level but never token content.
close_reason: Implemented HTTP ScopeGrant dispatch context and monotone-down session-level enforcement. Verified with focused OAuth scope tests, golden HTTP tests, fmt, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, sensitive-data lint, boundary lint, release preflight, and cargo package.

title: W11: build deterministic Asupersync test suite
description: Prove the migration adopted Asupersync semantics rather than merely compiling. Convert async tests incrementally, not via bulk regex. Use Asupersync test helpers and LabRuntime for shutdown waiters, admission permits, request cancellation, timeout loser drain, HTTP request regions, DB call cancellation, and preview-token single-use races. Add oracles for quiescence, obligation leaks, loser drain, and futurelock where supported. Live DB tests cover connect, ping, describe, queries, binds, transactions, DBMS_OUTPUT, call timeout, LOB capping, and query cancellation cleanup. Add a negative safety test proving malformed/unauthorized SQL returns guard/classification error before network I/O, DNS, connection, lease, or mutable execution state.
design: Implementation shape: turn runtime semantics into normal tests, not optional polish. Convert async tests incrementally to Asupersync helpers. Use LabRuntime where scheduling matters: shutdown waiters, admission permits, request cancellation, timeout loser drain, HTTP request regions, DB call cancellation, and preview-token single-use races. Add oracles for quiescence, obligation leaks, loser drain, and futurelock where available.
acceptance_criteria: Normal tests pass without Tokio; at least one deterministic test proves tool-call cancellation cleanup; at least one deterministic test proves request/tool region quiescence; preview-token race yields exactly one success; guard-before-I/O negative test passes; live DB tests pass when Oracle is available and skip with explicit reason otherwise; protocol and redaction tests run in the normal suite.
notes: Required coverage: live DB tests for connect, ping, describe, query rows, named/positional binds, transaction rollback/commit, DBMS_OUTPUT, call timeout, LOB capping, query cancellation cleanup; protocol tests from W8.5; redaction tests for HTTP logging and Authorization headers; safety negative test proving malformed/unauthorized SQL returns guard/classification before network I/O, DNS, connection, lease, or mutable execution state; race test where two regions redeem one preview token and exactly one succeeds.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic Asupersync test coverage: cancelled tool-call quiescence, preview-token race one-shot success, guard-before-I/O refusal, OAuth bad-token redaction, and live-XE DBMS_OUTPUT/cancellation skip-or-pass tests. Verified with fmt, diff check, sensitive-data lint, boundary lint, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, and live-xe test target.

description: Turn architecture decisions into automated CI invariants. Update cargo deny or a dedicated gate plus scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh so production builds fail if Tokio, asupersync-tokio-compat, rmcp, axum, hyper, hyper-util, oracle, odpic-sys, r2d2, or other removed crate families appear in the normal dependency graph. Check normal and all-target graphs carefully so dev-only comparison tools cannot enter published artifacts. Any temporary compat callsite must carry a COMPAT-REMOVE marker tied to an open bead; final production graph fails if compat remains.
design: Implementation shape: encode the architecture as CI. Update cargo deny and/or scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh to inspect normal production dependency graphs and fail if Tokio, tokio-stream, tokio-util, asupersync-tokio-compat, rmcp, axum, hyper, hyper-util, oracle, odpic-sys, r2d2, or removed crate families remain. Check normal and all-target graphs separately so dev-only comparison helpers are visible but cannot leak into releases. User value: future changes cannot accidentally undo the thin-native migration after it lands.
acceptance_criteria: CI fails on forbidden production dependencies; cargo deny check or the chosen gate is part of pre-commit/pre-publish gates; dev-only exceptions are documented and cannot enter published artifacts; cargo tree normal checks for each forbidden crate are visible in CI logs.
notes: Suggested visible checks: cargo tree -e normal -i tokio/asupersync-tokio-compat/rmcp/axum/hyper/hyper-util/oracle/odpic-sys/r2d2. Prefer deny.toml bans backed by explicit cargo tree CI output. Every temporary compat callsite must carry COMPAT-REMOVE tied to an open bead; final production graph fails if compat remains. Credential/logging: dependency gates should print package names and feature paths only, never environment variables or registry credentials.
close_reason: Made scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh a hard thin-native forbidden production dependency gate, added all-target visibility and compat marker checks, wired it into release_preflight, and verified fmt, shellcheck, clippy, workspace/all-target tests, cargo deny, docs, release preflight, sensitive-data lint, and cargo package.

Use criterion or focused integration benchmarks only where they fit the repo. If Docker remains a release artifact, image-size comparison is required. title: W4: replace thick ODPI-C adapter with thin oracledb adapter
description: Replace RustOracleConnection, OraclePool, ODPI-C oracle crate usage, r2d2, and spawn_blocking DB calls with a pure-Rust thin adapter based on oracledb and Asupersync-native session management. Preserve session leases, rollback-on-release/expiry, DBMS_OUTPUT, temp tables, package globals, transactions, savepoints, bind/result type fidelity, NUMBER-as-string by default, NLS-stable serialization, AWR/ASH license gating, Statspack/unavailable degradation, call timeouts, and existing tool contracts. Audit proxy auth, ADB wallet/TCPS/SNI, OCI IAM, wallet passwords, Kerberos/RADIUS/SEPS, login/trusted statements, session identity, edition, standby caps, and every profile key. Unsupported modes must fail explicitly and show in doctor. If a mission-critical current auth path is unsupported, removing thick mode is blocked until the operator chooses upstreaming, scoping, or pausing.
design: Implementation shape: replace RustOracleConnection/OraclePool/ODPI-C oracle/r2d2/spawn_blocking with an oracledb-backed thin adapter and Asupersync-native session manager. Preserve stateful session lease semantics: DBMS_OUTPUT, temp tables, package globals, transactions, savepoints, commit/rollback, and session state must stay pinned to the same physical Oracle session. Stateless clean reads may use any clean connection. Cancellation must discard or clean dirty sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Default build needs no ODPI-C/Instant Client; live DB tests pass through thin mode where configured; every DB tool works or returns a documented structured unsupported-feature error; oracle/r2d2/spawn_blocking are gone from production DB paths; session lease and type-fidelity tests pass; complete structured driver errors are redacted before logs/MCP/errors; unsupported auth is visible in doctor.
notes: Detailed checks: map query_rows, query_rows_named, execute, commit, rollback, describe, ping, call_timeout, set_call_timeout, enable/read DBMS_OUTPUT; map binds for strings, numbers, nulls, booleans, dates/timestamps, binary/LOBs, named/positional binds; preserve Oracle NUMBER/high-precision decimal as lossless JSON string by default; preserve DATE/TIMESTAMP/INTERVAL/RAW/ROWID behavior and NLS independence. Audit external auth, username/password, proxy CONNECT THROUGH, TCPS, wallet location/password, OCI IAM token, Kerberos/RADIUS/SEPS, session identity, driver name, edition, login/trusted statements, standby caps. Redact complete structured driver errors before MCP/log conversion. If a mission-critical current auth path is unsupported, do not remove thick mode until the operator chooses upstreaming, scoping, or pausing.
close_reason: Implemented thin oracledb adapter; removed oracle/r2d2/Tokio DB path; unsupported DBMS_OUTPUT OUT bind, external wallet auth, IAM token auth, and edition selection are structured errors with upstream rust-oracledb beads rust-oracledb-acj/o0b/5bh/jr9 filed. Gates: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo test -p oraclemcp-db --features live-xe --test live_oracle -- --nocapture (7 skip/pass due invalid local creds); cargo clippy -p oraclemcp-db --features live-xe --test live_oracle -- -D warnings; cargo deny check; boundary lint; release_preflight; sensitive_data_lint; cargo package --workspace --locked --allow-dirty --no-verify.

title: W6a: propagate Cx through pure server dispatch
description: Introduce the pure server-side async trait and &Cx shape after the nightly pin, without waiting for thin DB adapter work. Replace synchronous ToolDispatch::dispatch with an async Cx-aware shape at the server boundary, update registry/core/custom-tool/session-tool call paths that do not require DB adapter readiness, keep pure guard/classifier logic pure, preserve ErrorEnvelope and oracle_capabilities, and avoid ambient runtime handles.
design: Implementation shape: introduce async &Cx-aware dispatch for pure server layers after W2, before W4. Replace synchronous ToolDispatch::dispatch shape with async fn dispatch(&self, cx: &Cx, name: &str, args: Value) -> Result<Value, ErrorEnvelope> or an equivalent local pattern. Update registry/core/custom-tools/session-tool call paths that do not require thin DB adapter readiness. Keep pure guard/classifier code pure where no Cx is needed. User value: transport/runtime migration can proceed in parallel with DB adapter work without mixing unrelated risks.
acceptance_criteria: Pure server dispatch APIs accept &Cx; stdio/runtime transport work can compile against the new shape; no pure server async path depends on ambient Tokio runtime handles; guard/classifier code remains pure where appropriate; focused cargo check/tests cover the migrated pure dispatch slice.
notes: Implemented Cx-aware ToolDispatch boundary locally and verified focused server/dispatcher/e2e tests, cargo fmt, clippy, workspace tests, and boundary lint. Blocked before commit because cargo deny rejects asupersync 0.3.4/asupersync-macros/franken-* license metadata (LicenseRef-MIT-OpenAI-Anthropic-Rider). Do not land until oraclemcp-asupersync-license-policy-g7a is resolved by explicit owner/legal policy decision, permissively licensed fork/relicense, or runtime plan change.
close_reason: Implemented Cx-aware object-safe ToolDispatch seam, explicit run_tool_with_cx path, OracleDispatcher Cx checkpoint, updated harness dispatchers, and deterministic stdio golden driver. Verified fmt, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny.

title: W6b: propagate Cx through DB-facing tool execution
description: After the thin adapter exists, pass &Cx through DB-backed tool execution and cancellation-sensitive paths. Enforce the critical safety rule: raw SQL classification and level gating must complete before any await, DB connection acquisition, lease acquisition, or mutable execution-side state transition. Re-check preview and execute phases independently. Add checkpoints in long serialization, pagination, retry, shutdown-sensitive, and async custom-tool validation loops. Cancelled DB calls must cancel/abandon thin-driver work, rollback leased transactions, close cursors, or discard dirty connections.
design: Implementation shape: after W4, propagate &Cx into DB-backed tools and thin adapter calls. Enforce fail-closed ordering: raw SQL classification and operating-level gating must complete before any await, DNS/network I/O, DB connection acquisition, session lease acquisition, or execution-side mutable state transition. Preview and execute phases both re-check the exact SQL/object/profile/options for that phase. User value: async adoption improves cancellation without opening a TOCTOU hole in the SQL guard.
acceptance_criteria: Individual DB-backed tools pass &Cx to the thin adapter; guard tests prove no await/connection/lease boundary runs before classification/level gating; cancellation can be tested at the tool-call boundary; dirty sessions are rolled back or discarded instead of returned cleanly.
notes: Cancellation policy: add checkpoints in long serialization, paginated fetch, retry, shutdown-sensitive, and async custom-tool validation loops. Cancelled DB calls must cancel/abandon driver work, roll back leased transactions, close cursors, or discard dirty connections. Tests must prove no pre-classification await/lease boundary and that cancellation cannot return a dirty session to the pool. Credential/logging: DB tool errors must pass through the same redaction path as W4; do not log raw bind values, passwords, IAM tokens, wallet passwords, or bearer tokens.
close_reason: Implemented Cx propagation through DB dispatch, cancellation checkpoints, dirty-session rollback tests, and full local gates.

title: W7a: replace Tokio runtime bootstrap and pure primitives
description: Replace the binary runtime bootstrap and pure runtime primitives with Asupersync. Move startup to RuntimeBuilder, audit telemetry for Tokio-tied layers, replace shutdown Notify, admission Semaphore, timeout logic, signal handling, TCP listener usage, tokio tests, and tokio::io test transports where the paths are not DB-adapter dependent. Define shutdown drain policy: stop admissions, cancel/drain active request regions, flush audit sinks where possible, and enforce a hard configurable drain timeout.
design: Implementation shape: replace pure Tokio runtime surfaces with Asupersync primitives. Move binary bootstrap to RuntimeBuilder, replace Notify, Semaphore/admission, timeout, signal, TCP listener, tokio tests, and tokio::io test transports where DB adapter readiness is not needed. Define shutdown drain: stop new admissions, cancel/drain active request/tool regions, flush audit sinks where possible, and enforce a hard operator-configurable drain timeout.
acceptance_criteria: Direct tokio imports are gone from pure production runtime/server paths; tests cover shutdown waiters, lost wakeups, admission caps, timeout losers, signal/drain behavior, and new-work refusal during drain; any temporary compat module is isolated with a removal bead; telemetry does not reintroduce Tokio.
notes: Safety notes: audit tracing/telemetry for Tokio-tied layers and prevent reintroduction. Audit unwrap/expect in credential, connection, OAuth, HMAC, wallet, and token-handling paths; replace panic-prone handling with structured errors where sensitive values may be in scope and verify panic hooks redact. Any temporary compat module must be isolated with a removal bead and cannot survive W12.
close_reason: Replaced pure runtime primitives with Asupersync Notify/Semaphore/timeout, added drain refusal, converted targeted tests, and marked rmcp/axum Tokio holdouts as W8/W9 compatibility boundaries.

title: W7b: wire Asupersync cancellation into DB cleanup
description: Complete the DB-specific part of W7 once W6b exists. DB calls must receive cancellation through Asupersync child regions/budgets, and cancelled or timed-out calls must leave no reusable dirty session. Leased sessions with open transactions must rollback before reuse; open cursors must close or force connection discard; timeout errors must stay structured and actionable.
design: Implementation shape: complete DB-specific Asupersync cancellation after W6b. DB calls should be controlled by child regions/budgets and integrate with thin driver timeout/cancel behavior. Cancellation or timeout must leave no reusable dirty session: open transactions roll back, open cursors close, and uncertain sessions are discarded rather than returned to the clean pool. User value: operators can interrupt/shut down safely without corrupting pooled Oracle session state.
acceptance_criteria: A deterministic or focused integration test proves DB tool cancellation cleanup; cancelled DB work cannot return a dirty connection to the pool; rollback/close/discard behavior is documented; timeout/cancellation errors map cleanly to ErrorEnvelope/MCP surfaces.
notes: Testing: add deterministic or focused integration tests for cancellation during query/fetch, cancellation during leased transaction, timeout loser drain, and rollback/close/discard decision. Error surfaces must stay structured and actionable through ErrorEnvelope and MCP responses. Credential/logging: cancellation and timeout logs should include safe correlation/session identifiers only, never connect strings, bind values, usernames with embedded proxy targets if sensitive, wallet paths when policy treats paths as sensitive, or token material.
close_reason: Implemented Cx-aware lease preview cleanup, pool cancellation discard policy, timeout envelope coverage, docs, and full local gates.

title: W8: implement native stdio MCP transport
description: Remove rmcp from the default local-agent stdio path while preserving MCP client compatibility. Implement JSON-RPC envelope parsing/writing for initialize, notifications/initialized, tools/list, tools/call, oracle_capabilities, init-token validation, structured success/error results, and unknown tool failure. Audit protocol-type-only crates such as tower-mcp-types or rust-mcp-schema, or use internal serde structs if dependencies/version mismatch. Reject invalid JSON, unknown methods, invalid params, and oversized messages fail-closed. Init tokens require timing-safe comparison; logs must stay structured and non-secret.
design: Implementation shape: implement native JSON-RPC stdio MCP transport without rmcp. Preserve initialize, initialized notification, tools/list, tools/call, oracle_capabilities, init-token validation, structured success/error results, and unknown-tool failure. Decide whether protocol-type-only crates are acceptable after auditing dependencies and MCP version fit; use internal serde structs if that is cleaner.
acceptance_criteria: Golden stdio transcripts pass; e2e_stdio.rs and other stdio tests are ported away from rmcp helpers and pass against native implementation; initialize protocolVersion exactly matches advertised version; rmcp no longer appears in the stdio production path; at least one real or faithful scripted MCP client invocation verifies compatibility.
notes: Protocol/tests: invalid JSON returns parse error, unknown method returns method-not-found or protocol-appropriate error, invalid params fail closed, oversized messages reject before JSON parsing, ids echo correctly, notifications do not receive responses. Init tokens require timing-safe comparison. Port e2e_stdio.rs and other stdio tests away from rmcp helpers. Acceptance includes a faithful scripted or real MCP client invocation.
close_reason: Implemented native blocking JSON-RPC stdio transport with explicit Asupersync dispatch, kept transcripts stable, moved rmcp/Tokio runtime to HTTP only, and ported stdio tests off rmcp/Tokio.

title: W8.5: add MCP conformance tests
description: Prove the native MCP core is spec-shaped, not just a replay of old SDK happy paths. Record the MCP spec version under test; verify protocol negotiation, malformed JSON, unknown methods, invalid params, notifications, id echoing, batch behavior implemented or explicitly rejected, tools/list schema shape, unadvertised tool rejection, and structured content compatibility. Reuse the suite for HTTP where transport differences allow.
design: Implementation shape: build protocol conformance tests for the native MCP core after W8 and before W9. Record the MCP spec version under test. Test version negotiation, malformed JSON, unknown method, invalid params, notification no-response behavior, id echoing, batch support implemented or explicitly rejected, tools/list capability/schema shape, tools/call rejecting unadvertised names, and structured content compatibility.
acceptance_criteria: Native stdio passes conformance before W9 starts; deviations from MCP spec are documented as deliberate compatibility choices; the conformance suite is permanent and later runs against HTTP too.
notes: Use the same suite for HTTP in W9 where transport differences allow. Document any intentional spec deviations as compatibility choices with current clients. This bead gates W9 so HTTP does not inherit an unproven MCP core. Credential/logging: conformance fixtures should use synthetic tokens, synthetic profile names, and synthetic request ids only; tests must assert protocol errors do not echo secret-bearing inputs.
close_reason: Added native stdio MCP conformance harness covering initialize, notifications, tools/list, tools/call, JSON-RPC errors, oversized/batch rejection, id echoing, and token non-echo; documented coverage and accepted deviations.

title: W9: implement native HTTP MCP transport
description: Replace Axum, Hyper, and HTTP-side rmcp with Asupersync-native or minimal non-Tokio HTTP/web primitives. Preserve /mcp, loopback-safe default bind, explicit remote bind opt-in, Host guard, Origin guard, JSON response mode, Streamable HTTP stateful/session behavior, protected-resource metadata, OAuth bearer validation, and clear unauthenticated failures. Host/Origin and request-size checks must happen before OAuth/tool dispatch. Bearer tokens require timing-safe comparison. Log HTTP headers by explicit allowlist only; Authorization, Cookie, Referer, proxy auth, and deployment-specific sensitive headers must not leak by default. Each HTTP request is an Asupersync region with narrowed capabilities and owned spawned work.
design: Implementation shape: rebuild /mcp on Asupersync native HTTP/web primitives or a minimal non-Tokio HTTP library selected by W0. Preserve loopback-safe default bind, explicit remote bind opt-in, Host guard, Origin guard, JSON response mode, Streamable HTTP stateful/session behavior, protected-resource metadata, OAuth bearer validation, and clear unauthenticated failure responses. Host/Origin and size checks run before OAuth and before tool dispatch.
acceptance_criteria: Golden HTTP transcripts pass; HTTP e2e tests are ported away from axum helpers; axum/hyper/rmcp are absent from production dependency graph; Streamable HTTP remains supported; shutdown drains active request regions; host/origin/OAuth/request-size tests prove checks happen before dispatch and logs do not leak sensitive headers.
notes: Security/protocol: every HTTP request is a region; spawned work belongs to that request; shutdown drains active request regions. Capabilities should be narrowed so metadata routes do not get DB/spawn authority unless needed. Compare bearer tokens with timing-safe equality after shape/length validation. Log only explicit safe headers; Authorization, Cookie, Referer, proxy auth, and deployment-specific sensitive headers must not leak. Avoid carrying axum/tower abstractions forward mechanically.
close_reason: Implemented native HTTP MCP transport without rmcp/axum/hyper/Tokio production deps; ported HTTP e2e/goldens; preserved Streamable HTTP, guards, OAuth metadata/challenge, request-size rejection before OAuth, and shutdown drain worker join; verified fmt, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, diff check.

title: fix: sync doctor driver-version assertion to 0.8.1
close_reason: Synced stale doctor provenance assertion/comment to the already-pinned oracledb 0.8.1 line; verified targeted doctor test.

title: audit verify: tail-truncation of the JSONL chain is undetected (no head anchor / expected-length)
description:
OBSERVATION (leave-as-bead; documented residual, NOT a regression) from the 2026-07 multipass bug hunt audit-chain e2e on xe18.

WHAT WAS TESTED: ran the operating-level ladder against xe18 with ORACLEMCP_AUDIT_KEY set, produced a 15-record signed hash-chain, then ran oraclemcp audit verify against tampered copies:
- in-place edit of a record  -> DETECTED (exit 2, HashMismatch, broken_at_index correct)
- delete a middle record (splice/reorder) -> DETECTED (PrevHashMismatch)
- wrong signing key -> DETECTED (SignatureMismatch)
- DROP THE LAST RECORD (tail truncation) -> NOT DETECTED: verify reported ok:true, records:14.

ROOT CAUSE: verify_records (crates/oraclemcp-audit/src/verify.rs) re-walks from genesis checking hash-link + monotonic-seq + keyed-MAC. A prefix of a valid chain is itself a valid chain, so dropping trailing records yields a still-valid shorter chain. There is no persisted head anchor (last seq / last entry_hash) or expected-count for verify to compare against.

STATUS: this is a KNOWN, DOCUMENTED design boundary, not a new bug. docs/threat-model.md T5 honestly claims only the three checks verify actually performs (in-place edit, reorder, recompute-from-genesis) and points at optional WORM/SIEM shipping (crates/oraclemcp-audit/src/shipping.rs, ADR 0003) as the mitigation for an independent tamper-evident copy — which is exactly what catches tail-truncation. The oracle_version_matrix.sh harness also asserts .records >= 10 as a coarse floor. So the property is covered at the architecture level; verify simply cannot detect it locally by design.

SUGGESTED HARDENING (optional, non-blocking): let audit verify optionally take an expected head (--expect-seq N / --expect-head <entry_hash>) or persist a separate head-pointer file that verify cross-checks, so a local-only deployment (no shipping) can detect tail-truncation too. Alternatively, have verify emit an advisory note that trailing truncation is not locally detectable without an anchor. Do NOT change the default success semantics without an explicit anchor input (would break the honest 'a valid prefix is valid' contract).

EVIDENCE: /tmp scratch repro (env-only + file copies, no repo mutation); binary /tmp/cargo-target/debug/oraclemcp audit verify.
close_reason: Fixed: audit verify now detects tail-truncation via a signed sidecar head anchor (crates/oraclemcp-audit/src/anchor.rs, with_head_anchor on the sink). Crash-consistency: the record fsync ALWAYS precedes the anchor update, so a crash window can leave the anchor BEHIND the durable chain (explainable, self-heals on next append) but NEVER ahead; verify reports TRUNCATED when the chain ends before the anchored head. Anchor update failure fails the call closed. Record format unchanged (anchor is additive sidecar). 11 anchor unit tests (intact/truncated/anchor-behind crash-window) + ladder e2e audit step, all green; documented in docs/hardening.md.

title: Make profiles.pool runtime behavior honest and session-safe
description:
## Current gap

`[profiles.pool]` is documented and parsed by `oraclemcp-config`, and `oraclemcp-db` contains `OraclePool` plus `PoolSettings`. The shipping binary still opens a single `RustOracleConnection` in `try_open_connection`, so profile pool settings are currently misleading: operators can configure them, `oracle_list_profiles` can report the timeout metadata, but the server does not use a profile-backed pool.

## Required change

Make `[profiles.pool]` honest and functional, or deliberately remove/hide it from the user-facing profile contract until it can be used safely.

The preferred implementation is to wire the existing pool into the server connection strategy without breaking session-affinity-sensitive workflows.

## Design constraints

- Do not break session features that require affinity: transactions, savepoints, temp tables, package globals, DBMS_OUTPUT, login setup, and profile session identity.
- If only read-only stateless tools can safely use the pool, make that split explicit in code and docs.
- If the current `OracleConnection` trait is the wrong abstraction for `OraclePool`, add a narrow adapter rather than pretending pooled and leased sessions have identical semantics.
- Admission control should align with pool `max_size` instead of allowing unbounded agent concurrency to starve the pool.

## Acceptance criteria

- Either `[profiles.pool]` is wired into runtime behavior and verified, or profile docs/metadata stop claiming it is usable.
- Tests prove configured pool settings are converted to `PoolSettings` and used by the connection factory.
- Tests cover max/min/acquire-timeout validation and the default values.
- Tests or docs prove session-affinity-sensitive operations still run on one session or are explicitly excluded from pooled execution.
- `oracle_connection_info`/doctor output should make the active connection strategy clear without leaking connection details.
close_reason: Implemented hybrid_pool runtime strategy for profiles.pool, added routing/tests/docs/doctor reporting, and passed workspace gates plus single-bead compliance audit.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Close evidence: profiles.pool now maps through oraclemcp-config -> PoolSettings -> ResolvedProfile.pool_settings; serve opens a pinned session plus optional OraclePool-backed stateless metadata connection. Dispatcher routes metadata/catalog/source tools to the stateless connection and keeps oracle_query, sample rows, CLOB reads, write/DDL paths, transactions, and DBMS_OUTPUT on the pinned session. OraclePool implements OracleConnection only for query/describe/ping and fails execute/commit/rollback explicitly. oracle_connection_info and doctor report single_session/hybrid_pool/stateless_metadata_pool strategy without connection secrets. Docs and robot docs describe the split and statement_cache_size honesty. Verification: cargo fmt --all -- --check; git diff --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo deny check; cargo test --workspace; single-bead audit 2026-06-18T09-02-01Z scored 1000/1000. Opt-in live target cargo test -p oraclemcp-db --features live-xe --test live_oracle -- --nocapture passed harness but skipped DB assertions because current Oracle credentials fail ORA-01017.
